genesis/1.bible_quote: And God said let there be light and there was light...And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night.  And the evening and the morning were the first day.
genesis/1.note: This was symbolic as the sun was not created until the fourth day.

genesis/1.ref: Star of the West, XVI:?, p. 64 (*)
genesis/1.bible_quote: the spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
genesis/1.note: the face of the waters is the sea of humanity.  Mankind likened to a drop of the sea. (* the Christ spirit in Boy Scout training)

genesis/1.bible_quote: And God said, Le us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image, in the image of God created He him.
genesis/1.note: God created all, all are under His protection.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let us make man in Our image, after Our likeness...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man admonished to strive to become the reflection and likeness of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image, after Our likeness...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man from the beginning was in this perfect form and was the manifestation of the image of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us created man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Baha'u'llah bore all ordeals that infants may be likenesses of God.

genesis/1.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden of Acca, p. 30
genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: For though of the earth earthly, yet in reality they are of heaven...their characteristics heavenly.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God made man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man is of the image of God, that is, the perfections of God, the divine virtues are reflected in man.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...So God created man in His own image.
genesis/1.note: This applies to women as well as men...all are creatures and servants of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: It requires that we become recipients of all the perfections of God.  It necessitates the focalization of all heavenly virtues.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: The spirit of man distinguishes him above all the lower kingdom.  It is he spirit to which the verse...refers when it states that man has been created after the image of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: The highest development of man is his entrance into the divine kingdom--his honor, attainment of knowledge of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God made man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: The highest development of man is his entrance into the divine kingdom.  If spiritual fruits are forthcoming he is enkindled with fire of God's love.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: By this is meant the attainment of heavenly virtues, get rid of defects and show forth the attributes of God...

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: If one lives up to these divine commandments, this world shall be transformed into a paradise of glory.  This is being created in the image of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: This is the supreme goal of the world of humanity...Divine education consists in acquiring divine perfections.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: He must educate the human reality that it may become the center of the divine appearances...that the attributes and names of God shall be resplendent in the mirror of man...image and likeness may become true.

genesis/1.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:4, p. 128
genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Just as the effulgence and light of the sun, when cast upon a mirror, is reflected fully, gloriously, if the mirror be polished, so likewise the virtues of Divinity are possible of reflection in the human reality...

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: make thy heart a pure, clear and polished mirror...that the pictures of the Supreme Concourse may be printed on it, this is what is meant by "Let Us create man after Our image and likeness."

genesis/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 97
genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man can attain a very high station by reflecting the attributes of God.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man in created in God's image and is eternal.

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man is made in God's image.  Jesus Christ said, "I am in the Father, and the Father in Me." (see John 10:38)

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: To man God has given such wonderful power that he can guide, control and overcome nature...How can man be content to lead only an animal existence?

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: Man must become the reflection and likeness of God even as it hath been revealed in the bible, "We shall create man in Our image."

genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: God created man in the utmost perfections...Every excellent soul, if confirmed by the heavenly bounty...putteth forth wonderful blossoms and growth...

genesis/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 55
genesis/1.bible_quote: Let Us make man in Our image...so God created man in His own image...
genesis/1.note: The coming of the Manifestation results in man being adorned with spiritual qualities, they become the image of God, the true station of man planned from the beginning.

genesis/2.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 3 (*)
genesis/2.bible_quote: And the Lord took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
genesis/2.note: Realm of humanity to be the garden of Eden if basis is peace, love, etc. (*New Year's greeting)
Symbolism explained: Adam is spirit of man; Eve is soul of man; Tree of good and evil is human world; Serpent is attachment to this world; Tree of life is position of universal Manifestation.

genesis/2.bible_quote: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished and on the seventh day God ended his work.
genesis/2.note: This statement is symbolic.  The texts of the holy books are all symbolic needing authoritative interpretation.

genesis/2.bible_quote: God breathed the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
genesis/2.note: After man was created from the dust he required this breath of life to become a living soul.  Real existence comes from God...from the breath of the Holy Spirit to every man...man's real existence comes from the Holy Spirit...

genesis/2.bible_quote: And the Lord breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
genesis/2.note: Explanation of the proceeding of the human spirit from God.

genesis/2.bible_quote: And the rib which the Lord God had taken from the man made He a woman and brought her unto the man...
genesis/2.note: Adam is the spirit of man, Eve is his soul.

genesis/2.bible_quote: God made to grow...the tree of life in the midst of the garden
genesis/2.note: The tree of life is Baha'u'llah and the daughters of the kingdom are the leaves

genesis/3.bible_quote: Ye may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden ye shall not eat of it...she took of the fruit thereof and did eat and he did eat.
genesis/3.note: The mass of Christians believe that the consequence of this disobediance has been transmitted as a heritage.  Hence Adam is responsible for the death of humanity--this is unreasonable and wrong...

genesis/3.bible_quote: Ye shall not eat of it...lest ye die...she took of the fruit thereof and did eat...and he did eat.
genesis/3.note: Explanation of the origin of sin and the results therefrom.

genesis/3.bible_quote: Ye shall not eat of it lest ye die...Eve took of the fruit thereof and did eat and he did eat.
genesis/3.note: They say Adam sinned which was transmitted to his descendants.  Could we conceive of [God] Who is justice itself inflicting punishment upon the posterity of Adam for his own sin?  There is no connection between the two.

genesis/3.bible_quote: therefore the Lord God sent [Adam and Eve] from the garden of Eden.
genesis/3.note: Because of Adam's sin in eating of the tree of knowledge, God saved him by guarding him from eating of the tree of life and God sent him out of the garden.

genesis/4.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 205 (*)
genesis/4.bible_quote: It came to pass that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the Lord.  And Abel he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and the fat thereof.
genesis/4.note: These were sacrifices.  This is a symbol and this word extended after the time of Christ. (*Talk on 10/22/1912)

genesis/4.bible_quote: And Cain talked with Abel and rose up against Abel and slew him...
genesis/4.note: More infamous than even the act perpetrated by Cain was the behavior of Mirza Yahya against Baha'u'llah.

genesis/6.bible_quote: Noah found grace in the eyes of the Lord...Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations and Noah walked with God...with thee will I establish My Covenant...
genesis/6.note: Among the Prophets was Noah.  For 950 years He prayerfully exhorted His people and summoned them to the haven of security and peace...None heeded his call.

genesis/6.ref: Star of the West, VIII:16, p. 219 (*)
genesis/6.bible_quote: with thee will I establish My Covenant; and thou shalt come into the Ark, thou and thy sons and thy wife and thy sons' wives...
genesis/6.note: This Covenant is the Ark of Noah, its moving power is the fire of the love of God and its captain is Baha'u'llah. (* Address on 12/6/1912)

genesis/7.bible_quote: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights...
genesis/7.note: Baha'u'llah symbolized His tears as the measure of Noah's flood.

genesis/9.bible_quote: Ham saw the nakedness of his father and told his two brethren without.
genesis/9.note: More perfidious than the action of Ham was the behavior of Mirza Yahya towards Baha'u'llah.

genesis/9.ref: Gleanings from the Writings of Baha'u'llah, p. 84
genesis/9.bible_quote: and he [Noah] said, Cursed be Canaan a servant of servants shall he be to his brethren.
genesis/9.note: Canaan sinned against Noah and was rejected as a member of the family.  He deprived himself of God's favor.  Arise, lift up your voice and proclaim His Cause.

genesis/9.bible_quote: Cursed be Canaan! A servant of servants shall he be to his brethren.
genesis/9.note: like Canaan, who is not reckoned as being of the race of Noah.

genesis/9.bible_quote: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.
genesis/9.note: Family relationship is cut off because of his sin against his father Noah.  Those in the kingdom are spiritually related.

genesis/12.bible_quote: the Lord had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country and from thy kindred and from thy father's house unto a land that I will shew thee.
genesis/12.note: The enforced departure of Baha'u'llah to Baghdad recalled the banishment of Abraham from Ur.

genesis/12.bible_quote: thou shalt be a blessing and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed...
genesis/12.note: The Manifestations of universal Prophethood who appeared independently are Abraham, Moses, Christ, Muhammad, the Bab and Baha'u'llah.

genesis/12.bible_quote: Abraham departed...out of Haran and they went forth and into the land of Canaan they came.
genesis/12.note: Abraham then came into the region of the Holy Land.

genesis/12.ref: Star of the West, XIV:3, p. 87
genesis/12.bible_quote: And Abraham took Sarai his wife and Lot, his brother's son, and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan and into the land of Canaan they came.
genesis/12.note: Abraham's migration from his native land caused the bountiful gifts of the Almighty to be made manifest...The banishment of the Sacred Beauty led to the diffusion of the Light of Divine Revelation.  Blessing of homelessness shall endure for ever.

genesis/13.bible_quote: And the Lord said to Abram...lift up now thine eyes, and look.  For the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it and to thy seed for ever.
genesis/13.note: Baha'u'llah now on the Holy Land promised to Abraham's seed.

genesis/13.bible_quote: and the Lord said unto Abraham...Lift up now thine eyes and look from the place where thou art, northward, southward, eastward and westward, for all the land which thou seest to thee will I give it, a
genesis/13.note: Prophecy of the occupation of the Holy Land and fulfillment of the Covenant.

genesis/15.bible_quote: the word of the Lord came unto Abram in a vision, saying, Fear not, Abram; I am thy shield, and thy exceeding great reward.
genesis/15.note: It was the love of God that led Abraham.

genesis/15.bible_quote: The Lord made a Covenant with Abram saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land...
genesis/15.note: The land promised Abraham now occupied by Baha'u'llah, glimpse of fulfilment of the Covenant.

genesis/15.bible_quote: The Lord made a Covenant with Abram saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river Euphrates.
genesis/15.note: Abraham stood fast and showed forth supernatural firmness...he established the unity of God.  His descendants became powerful, and the Holy Land was given to them.

genesis/16.bible_quote: And Abraham was fourscore and six years old when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.
genesis/16.note: Hagar was the mother of one of Abraham's prosterity, Ishmael; one of his descendants was Muhammad.

genesis/17.bible_quote: Walk before Me and be thou perfect.
genesis/17.note: Later the Beauty of the countenance of the Friend of God [Abraham] appeared from behind the veil and another standard of divine guidance was hoisted...

genesis/17.bible_quote: And I will establish My Covenant between Me and thee and thy seed after thee...for an everlasting Covenant, to be a God unto thee and to thy seed after thee.
genesis/17.note: The Holy Land was conquered by the power of the Covenant of God with Abraham, due to the religion of God.

genesis/17.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 31
genesis/17.bible_quote: I will establish My Covenant between Me and thee and thy seed after thee...for an everlasting Covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.
genesis/17.note: This Covenant finally fulfilled in Baha'u'llah, seed of Abraham came to full fruitage.

genesis/17.bible_quote: And I will establish My Covenant between Me and thee and thy sed after thee in their generations for an everlasting Covenant, to be a God unto thee and to thy seed after thee.
genesis/17.note: Through Keturah and Abraham is the seed of Abraham, one line of descent, from which came Baha'u'llah.

genesis/17.bible_quote: twelve princes will he beget. (see Rev 12:1)
genesis/17.note: Twelve prince are the twelve Imams after Muhammad. (see PUP, p. 78)

genesis/17.bible_quote: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee; behold I have blessed him and will make him fruitful and will multiply him exceedingly; twelve princes shall he beget and I will make him a great nation.
genesis/17.note: Hagar was found from whom Ishmael was born; descendants, Muhammad, the Bab and other Prophets of God.

genesis/18.bible_quote: Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him.
genesis/18.note: All Revelators from Abraham's line to bless the whole earth.

genesis/20.bible_quote: and yet indeed she is my sister; she is the daughter of my father but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
genesis/20.note: During the cycle of Adam it was expedient for a man to marry his own sister, but in the law of Moses this was forbidden.  Social laws were changed by the Manifestation according to the needs and conditions.

genesis/22.bible_quote: And Abraham took the fire in his hand and a knife...and took the knife to slay his son...
genesis/22.note: Baha'u'llah gives vent to the agonies of His sorrow-laden heart...Abraham's fire an ebullition of My soul.

genesis/22.bible_quote: Take now thy son, thine only son...whom thou lovest and offer him there for a burnt offering...
genesis/22.note: A prayer revealed by Baha'u'llah exalts Mirza Mihdi's death to the rank of those acts of atonement associated with Abraham's intended sacrifice, crucifixion of Jesus Christ and the martyrdom of the Imam Husayn.

genesis/22.ref: Gleanings from the Writings of Baha'u'llah,p.75-76
genesis/22.bible_quote: And Abraham took the knife to slay his son...
genesis/22.note: The purpose of God...was to sacrifice his son as a ransom for the sins of all.  This same honor Jesus besought God to confer upon Him, also the sacrifice by Muhammad.

genesis/22.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 101
genesis/22.bible_quote: Lay not thine hand upon the lad; for I know that thou fearest God.
genesis/22.note: Sacrifice of Ishmael not required, but through the treachery of His enemies the sacrifice of the Bab was carried through.

genesis/22.bible_quote: By Myself have I sworn, saith the Lord...in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed...
genesis/22.note: The foundation of the divine religions is reality.  His Holiness Abraham heralded the reality; Moses promulgated the reality; Christ established the reality; the Bab was the door of reality, Baha'u'llah was the splendor of the reality.  Reality is one.

genesis/22.bible_quote: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed...Walk before Me and be thou perfect.
genesis/22.note: The beauty of the countenance of the Friend of God [Abraham] appeared and another standard of guidance was hoisted...

genesis/22.bible_quote: and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed.
genesis/22.note: It is an especially blessing that from among the descendants of Abraham should have come all the Prophets of the children of Israel.

genesis/22.bible_quote: and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed; because thou hast obeyed My voice.
genesis/22.note: It was the love of God that led Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, that strengthened Joseph...Thus I exhort each of you, realizing its power and beauty,to sacrifice...and bring knowledge of the love of God into every heart.

genesis/25.note: Baha'u'llah derived His descent from Keturah.

genesis/26.bible_quote: I will be with thee [Isaac] and will bless thee [isaac].
genesis/26.note: It was the love of God that led Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.

genesis/29.bible_quote: Laban...took his daughter Leah and brought her to [Jacob]....he gave him Rachel, his daughter to wife also.
genesis/29.note: Give to them the names of Leah and Rachel for they were sisters beloved of God.

genesis/32.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca,p.30
genesis/32.bible_quote: And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of day.
genesis/32.note: Angel has two meanings, divine confirmation is one, the other is explained.

genesis/32.bible_quote: Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel, for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men.
genesis/32.note: As a result the teachings of Abraham were spread abroad--a Jacob appeared among his posterity.

genesis/32.ref: Star of the West, XII:3, p. 58
genesis/32.bible_quote: Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel; for as a prince thou hast power with God and with men...
genesis/32.note: This is a title of Jacob himself, His descendants wre called the children of Israel and eventually Israel.

genesis/35.bible_quote: the which I gave Abraham and Isaac, to thee [Israel] will I give it...
genesis/35.note: It was the love of God that led Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.

genesis/35.ref: Star of the West, XII:3, p. 58
genesis/35.bible_quote: Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel shall be thy name...
genesis/35.note: This is a title of Jacob himself, His descendants were called the children of Israel, and eventually Israel.

genesis/35.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca,p.30
genesis/35.bible_quote: Thy name is Jacob: thy name shall not be called any more Jacob but Israel shall be thy name...
genesis/35.note: Jacob held on to the hem, the heavenly attributes; he knew its value.

genesis/37.bible_quote: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children...and he made him a coat of many colors.
genesis/37.note: His Holiness Jacob, in Canaan, longed for meeting with Joseph in Egypt yet he was near and inhated the fragrance of the garment...affinity from a far distance...

genesis/37.bible_quote: and they stripped Joseph out of his coat, and they took him and cast him into a pit.
genesis/37.note: Baha'u'llah recalled in anticipation of His own lot that of Joseph as he languished in a pit by reason of the treachery of his brothers.

genesis/37.bible_quote: And it came to pass when Joseph was come unto his brethren...they took him and cast him into a pit...
genesis/37.note: The betrayal of Baha'u'llah by Mirza Yahya was more shameful than the betrayal of Joseph by His brothers.

genesis/37.bible_quote: when Joseph was come unto his brethren, that they stript Joseph out of his coat...and they took him and cast him into a pit...
genesis/37.note: How the conduct of these former evildoers agreeth with the course of the succeeding followers of the great darkness!

genesis/37.ref: Star of the West, IX:14, p. 162 (*)
genesis/37.bible_quote: and they took Joseph's coat and brought it to their father...[Jacob said] It is my son's coat.
genesis/37.note: Explanation.  The love of God causes development of the spirit by the grace of the Beneficent Lord. (* Tablet to Mrs. Kate Ives, Eliot, Maine, 10/1904)

genesis/37.bible_quote: And they sent the coat of many colors and they brought it to their father...and he knew it and said, It is my son's coat...
genesis/37.note: Jacob, whose nostrils were pure inhaled the fragrance of Joseph from a long distance.

genesis/37.bible_quote: And Jacob mourned for his son [Joseph] and all his sons and daughters rose up to comfort him but he refused to be comforted.
genesis/37.note: Baha'u'llah said, Jacob's grief is but a reflection of My sorrows.

genesis/39.bible_quote: the Lord was with Joseph and showed him mercy and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.
genesis/39.note: It was the love of God that strengthened Joseph in Egypt and gave to Moses courage and patience.  Thus I exhort each of you, realizing its power...to sacrifice your actions to bring the knowledge of the love of God into every heart.

genesis/41.bible_quote: And Pharaoh said unto Joseph...Thou shalt be over my house and according to thy word shall all my people be ruled...
genesis/41.note: As a result of the teachings of Abraham...a Joseph became ruler in Egypt.

genesis/45.ref: Star of the West, II:10, p. 3
genesis/45.bible_quote: Joseph made himself known unto his brethren.  Be not angry with yourselves that ye sold me hither, for God did send me before you to preserve you a posterity in the earth.  God made me a father to Pha
genesis/45.note: Perchance the splendor of the Beauty of the real Joseph, the most Great Name, may shine from the Occident.  His bounties are conditioned upon thy serving the real Master with sincerity.

genesis/45.bible_quote: It is enough, Joseph my son is yet alive...I will go and see him before I die, and Israel took his journey...
genesis/45.note: Like Jacob you must sacrifice your outward eyes.  The lover seeks his Beloved.

genesis/46.bible_quote: Joseph made ready his chariot and went up to meet Israel, his father...and he fell on his neck and wept in his neck a good time.
genesis/46.note: Renounce self in order to find God. The lover seeks his Beloved.

genesis/48.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca,p.30
genesis/48.bible_quote: And Jacob said...God appeared to me at Luz and blessed me.
genesis/48.note: Jacob received heavenly confirmation and with all his power held on to that force and power, Jacob knew its value.

genesis/49.bible_quote: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah...until Shiloh come; and unto Him shall the gathering of the people be.
genesis/49.note: the Jews rejected Jesus Christ because of literal interpretation.

exodus/1.bible_quote: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun and Benjamin, Dan, Naphtali, Gad, Asher and Joseph.
exodus/1.note: In each cycle the guardians and holy souls have been twelve.  So Jacob had twelve sons.

exodus/1.bible_quote: the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour...
exodus/1.note: The children of Israel were a race of slaves under oppression; under Moses they were to achieve the highest plane of nationhood.

exodus/1.bible_quote: the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour...
exodus/1.note: His Holiness Moses rescued these degraded people of Israel from bondage.  Sending of prophets has been for the training of humanity.

exodus/1.bible_quote: And the Egyptians made the children of Israel to serve with rigour...
exodus/1.note: Moses rescued the children of Israel from bondage through the power of religious discipline.

exodus/2.bible_quote: And the child grew and she brought him unto Pharaoh's daughter and he became her son.  And she caled his name Moses.
exodus/2.note: He had been reared in the home of Pharaoh and been nourished at his table.

exodus/2.bible_quote: Moses went unto his brethren and looked on their burdens and he spied an Egyptian smiting one of his brethren and he slew the Egyptian...Now when Pharaoh heard about this he sought to slay Moses but M
exodus/2.note: To prevent an act of cruelty Moses struck down an Egyptian and became known as a murderer.  He fled and after that he was raised to the rank of Prophet.

exodus/2.bible_quote: Now when Pharaoh heard this thing he sought to slay Moses.  But Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh...
exodus/2.note: Pharaoh spoke insultingly saying...What a deed is that which thou hast done?  I fled from you when I feared you but my Lord hath given me wisdom and hath made me one of His apostles.

exodus/2.bible_quote: Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh and dwelt in the land of Midian.
exodus/2.note: In consequence of his exile [Abraham's] descendants became powerful...a Moses and a being like Christ were manifested from his posterity.

exodus/2.ref: Star of the West, XIV:3, p. 87
exodus/2.bible_quote: Moses fled from the face of Pharaoh...and the angel of the Lord appeared unto Him in a flame of fire out of the bush...
exodus/2.note: the flight of Moses, the Prophet of Sinai, revealed the flame of the Lord's burning Fire...Exile and banishment are blessed by the Divine favor...the banishment of the Sacred Beauty led to the diffusion of light of His Divine Revelation throughout all

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro, his father-in-law...
exodus/3.note: though but a shepherd Moses, through the power of religious training and discipline rescued Israel from bondage and founded a great civilization.

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro, his father-in-law...
exodus/3.note: Moses was but a simple shepherd but He was able to develop a whole nation which had been in a state of demoralization...Universal education can be accomplished only through the Holy Spirit.

exodus/3.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca,p.21
exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro, his father-in-law...
exodus/3.note: Moses was apparently a shepherd, but through the divine power, he overcame Pharaoh and his armies.

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro, his father-in-law...
exodus/3.note: Moses was a shepherd, but He became under God's direction, a divine shepherd.  He freed the children of Israel from bondage and captivity.

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro...
exodus/3.note: It is evident that His Holiness was a divine shepherd.  Religion is meant to be the quickening life of the body-politic...

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro...
exodus/3.note: Moses lived in Midian when Israel was in bondage in Egypt.  He was a shepherd in Midian, divine shepherd of Israel.

exodus/3.bible_quote: Moses kept the flock of Jethro...
exodus/3.note: It was such a man as this that freed a great nation from the chains of captivity...they attained a position which entitled them to honor among all nations...

deuteronomy/1.bible_quote: Also the Lord was angry with Me for your sakes, saying, Thou shalt no in thither.
deuteronomy/1.note: Rebuke addressed through Moses to the Israelites.

deuteronomy/3.note: Reproach really refers to the children of Israel who for having rebelled were held captive until the time of Joshua.  Reproofs addressed to the Prophets in reality are directed to the people.

deuteronomy/5.ref: Star of the West, VIII:4, p. 41 (*)

deuteronomy/5.note: What is prayer?  It is conversation with God...man sees himself in the presence of God.  One's heart must be sensitive to the music of prayer...Moses the Interlocutor. (*DMAS, 3/15/1914)

deuteronomy/10.bible_quote: and I stayed in the mount, according to the first time forty days and forty nights.
deuteronomy/10.note: For the purpose of awakening and admonishing the people of Israel, fasting was enjoined upon them.

deuteronomy/18.ref: Star of the West, XVI:2, p. 434
deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: The Lord will raise unto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto Me.  Unto Him ye shall hearken.
deuteronomy/18.note: Every Prophet predicted the coming of a successor and every successor acknowledged the truth of the predecessor.  Moses prophecied the coming of Christ.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: the Lord thy God will raise up unto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like unto Me; unto him ye shall hearken...
deuteronomy/18.note: How many awaited the Manifestation of the Messiah, yet they did not recognize Him?  Their literal interpretation veiled Christ from the Jews.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words into His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Israel, still wrapt in veils of false imaginings, is still expectant.  Becuase Jesus abrogated Moses' laws of divorce and the Sabbath, they rejected Him.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren like unto thee; and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: How many Pharisees were waiting to behold Him...yet they turned aside from Him and disputed with Him.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Moses announced the coming of Christ.  Israelites were waiting for Him but when He came they called Him Beelzebub.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee and will put My words in His mouth and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: The Sun of Reality shone from the Messianic mirror through the wonderful channel of Jesus Christ.  The Jews are holding to the Mosaic mirror, bereft of witnessing the lights in Jesus.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee and will put My words in His mouth and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Similarly the Jews were believers in His Holiness Moses, awaiting the coming of the Messiah.  The virtues and perfections of Moses became apparent in His Holiness Jesus Christ most effulgently but the Jews held to the name of Moses, not adoring the virts

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Two thousand years ago the Israelites expected a Messiah but when Christ appeared they turned away.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: the Lord said unto Moses I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren like unto thee and I will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: In the Bible the Jews have the promise of the Lord of Hosts and the Messiah.  All peoples awaiting the Two Manifestations.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me, I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee and I will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Jews kept away from the Light of Christ though He was the greatest friend of Moses and made Moses great in the eyes of the world.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Jews were awaiting the Manifestation of Christ...when He came, the Jews rejected Him.  The same is the case of the Manifestation of Baha'u'llah.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: The Jews were expecting the appearance of the Messiah but because of imitations they did not believe in Christ when He appeared.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: And the Lord said unto Me...I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in his mouth; and he shall speak unto them all that I shall command him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Christ said, I am the Promised One in your Books.  He spread His teachings.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: The Promised One, the Messiah must uphold and fulfill the laws of Moses, the Jews declared.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: The Messiah was promised in Hebrew Scriptures but when He came they rejected Him--so with all the prophets.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee, and will put My words in His mouth; and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: Moses foretold of Jesus Christ.  The foundations of all religious systems all proceed from God.

deuteronomy/18.bible_quote: I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren like unto thee and will put My words in His mouth and He shall speak unto them all that I shall command Him.
deuteronomy/18.note: The Jews prayed for the Messiah to come but because of literal interpretations they were veiled from Him.

deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: When Christ came He changed the old axion, "an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth."  It was converted into "love your enemies, do good to them that hate you"...one of love, mercy and forebearance.

deuteronomy/19.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 4
deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: Unimportant conditions underwent transformation but essential teachings were revoiced by Jesus Christ.

deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: then shall ye do unto him as he had thought to have done unto his brother...an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: Everyone of the divine religions contains essential ordinances which are not subject to change, and material ordinances which are abrogated according to the exigencies of time.  Christ abrogated the commands of Moses.

deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: These laws were applicable to the period; in the time of Christ they were not applicable, therefore abrogated.  Through truth all would be united.

deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: Laws have to be changed according to the needs and conditions of times.  His Holiness changed laws.

deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.
deuteronomy/19.note: Social laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of time and place.  Manifestations changed laws.

deuteronomy/19.ref: Star of the West, VIII:4, p. 47 (*)
deuteronomy/19.bible_quote: an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth.

deuteronomy/24.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce the people rose in protest against Him.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: then let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: Divorce had become so universal that there remained no fixed laws of marriage, therefore Jesus Christ forbade divorce.

deuteronomy/24.ref: Gleanings from the Writings of Baha'u'llah, p. 20

deuteronomy/24.note: Jesus Christ annulled the law of divorce.  The Jews are still waiting for the Manifestation.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: then let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: In the time of Moses this was necessary.  Jesus annulled the law of divorce.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: then let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: Christ proclaimed and completed the laws of Moses.  Becuase of the need and conditions of the times laws were changed.  Because of their adherence to imitations of ancient forms, they rejected Jesus Christ.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: the let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: In the time of Moses divorce was comformable to the needs and conditions.  In the time of Christ they were not suitable, so Jesus abrogated them.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: then let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: These outer laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of time, place and conditions.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: When a man hath taken a wife and married her and it come to pass that she find no favour in his eyes...let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: According to the exigencies of the time of Moses divorce was permissible.  During the time of Christ it was necessary to abrogate Moses' law of divorce.

deuteronomy/24.bible_quote: let him write her a bill of divorcement...
deuteronomy/24.note: In the days of Christ conditions had changed; Mosaic law was not adapted to needs therefore it was abrogated.

joshua/1.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 52

joshua/1.note: Promised Land is a spiritual state.  Moses viewed the Promised Land but died before he reached it.  Joshua became Israel's leader.

joshua/10.bible_quote: And there was no day like that before it or after it that the Lord hearkened unto the voice of a man; for the Lord fought for Israel.
joshua/10.note: The prophethood of Muhammad was the same as that of Moses and the power of 'Ali was the same as that of Joshua.

joshua/18.note: Holy Land honored by the lives of the patriarchs was occupied by Baha'u'llah.

judges/3.bible_quote: When the children of Israel cried unto the Lord, the Lord raised up a deliverer...In those days there was no king...
judges/3.note: Before the time of the kings, Israel was ruled by judges.  "The Holy Land was honored by the lives and labors of the Hebrew patriarchs, judges, kings..." Fulfilment of prophecy when Baha'u'llah occupied the Holy Land.

judges/21.bible_quote: And the children of Israel departed...every man to his tribe and inheritance.  In those days there was no king.

2-samuel/7.note: The Promised One was to be seated on the throne of David, whereas Jesus Christ had no home.  Literal interpretation veiled the reality of Jesus Christ.

2-samuel/7.bible_quote: I will establish the throne of his kingdom forever...

1-kings/10.bible_quote: I believed not the words until I came and beheld, the half was not told me; thy wisdom and prosperity exceedeth the fame which I heard.
1-kings/10.note: Moses was a simple shepherd...through his influence a whole nation which had been in a state of demoralization, reached a very enlightened civilization.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: Howbeit I believed not the words until I came, and mine eyes have seen it; and, behold the half was not told me; thy wisdom and prosperity exceedeth the fame which I heard.
1-kings/10.note: they rose to such a zenith that the splendor of the new sovereignty of Solomon was made possible, Moses brought unity to Israel--their religion was the quickening life of the body-politic.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: Howbeit I believed not the words until I came, and mine eyes have seen it; and behold the half was not told me; thy wisdom and prosperity exceedeth the fame which I heard.
1-kings/10.note: Moses gathered the wandering children of Israel and led them as a shepherd to political and religious unity.  Moses, a divine shepherd.

1-kings/10.note: It is evident therefore that Moses was an educator and teacher, culminating in the great power of Solomon's reign.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Moses civilized the children of Israel, brought them from bondage to the powerful nationhood during Solomon's reign.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Moses through the power of religion led Israel from bondage to a great renowned civilization and the splendor of Solomon's reign...Through the divine benediction the Holy Land was conquered and the glory of the Solomonic sovereignty dawned.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: In all lines of advancement they achieved distinction.  The Law of [Moses], the Word became the foundation of the laws of nations.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Moses disciplined the children of Israel and laid the foundations of nationhood and the glory of Solomon's reign.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Moses appeared with heavenly radiance and saved the children of Israel.  Moses' leadership culminated in the nationhood of Israel and in the glory of Solomon's reign.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: As the result of the institutions that Moses gave them they attained a position which entitled them to honor among all nations and their fame spread to all lands.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Even the Greek philosophers journeyed to Jerusalem to acquire the basis of Israelitish law.  Though but a shepherd Moses, through the power of religious training, rescued Israel from bondage and founded a great civilization.

1-kings/10.bible_quote: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom.  And all the earth sought to Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart.
1-kings/10.note: Moses trained the children of Israel and led them from bondage.  Even the philosophers of Greece went to Palestine to sit at the feet of sages...

psalms/2.bible_quote: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron...

psalms/2.bible_quote: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron...
psalms/2.note: The Messiah's sword was to be a sword of iron.  This signifies that His tongue would separate the true from the false.  He did not conquer by the power of an iron rod but by the sword of utterance.

psalms/2.bible_quote: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron...
psalms/2.note: This means that He would wield a sword.  It was the sword of His tongue that separated the right from wrong.  Jews were blind to spiritual symbolism.

isaiah/40.bible_quote: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth, but the Word of our God shall stand forever.
isaiah/40.note: The City is none other than the Word of God revealed in every age and dispensation.  In the days of Moses it was the Pentateuch; in the days of Jesus Christ, the Gospel...

isaiah/40.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 144-45

isaiah/40.note: This Day all the signs have appeared.

isaiah/40.bible_quote: Get thee up into a high mountain...lift up thy voice...say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God.  Behold the Lord God will come with a strong hand.
isaiah/40.note: Baha'u'llah was alluded to by Isaiah as One Who would come with a strong hand.

isaiah/40.bible_quote: O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain...lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid, say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God.  Behold the Lord God
isaiah/40.note: Baha'u'llah assures us that such a consummation had been prophesied.

isaiah/40.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 79
isaiah/40.bible_quote: O Zion that bringeth good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength...Behold the Lord God will come with strong hand, and His
isaiah/40.note: Addressing the Jewish people, Baha'u'llah revealed the strong city is Akka.

isaiah/41.bible_quote: thou, Israel, art My servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham, My Friend.
isaiah/41.note: Abraham's sacrifice was to demonstrate His detachment from all but God--so also was that of Jesus and Muhammad.

isaiah/43.bible_quote: I will bring thy seed from the east and gather thee from the west...let the people be assembled, let them bring forth their witnesses that they may be justified...
isaiah/43.note: The Jews did not understand, so were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.

isaiah/43.bible_quote: But now, thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and He that formed thee, O Israel...I have redeemed thee..
isaiah/43.note: Sacrificing themselves, Manifestations and servants of God are detached from material advantages and labour to give virtues to mankind.

isaiah/47.bible_quote: As for our redeemer, the Lord of Hosts is His name, the Holy One of Israel.
isaiah/47.note: These two the Jews are still looking for.  All the peoples of the world are awaiting two Manifestations.

isaiah/48.bible_quote: Hearken unto me, O Jacob and Israel, my called; I am he; I am the first, I also am the last.
isaiah/48.note: Sacrificing themselves, Manifestations and servants of God are detached from material advantages and labour to give virtues to mankind.

isaiah/49.bible_quote: And He made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of His hand hath He hid me; and made me a polished shaft in His quiver hath He hid me...
isaiah/49.note: It has been demonstrated that through the Revelations of the Manifestations, their attributes are made manifest, God's Revelation, attributes and names.

isaiah/49.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 35
isaiah/49.bible_quote: and He hath made my mouth like a sharp sword...
isaiah/49.note: The spiritual sword can separate the truth from error.  The tongue should proclaim truth, teach what is right and what is wrong.

isaiah/53.bible_quote: Who hath believed our report?  and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?  ...He is despised and rejected of men.
isaiah/53.note: Divine powers are focused in the spirit of the Messengers.  Hearers who are attuned received the Word; to others it was a foolishness, to be "despised and rejected".

isaiah/53.bible_quote: when we shall see Him there is no beauty that we should desire Him...He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief.  Surely He hath borne our griefs and carried our so
isaiah/53.note: Christ endured a life of sorrows, pain and grief to bring a perfect example of love into the world; and in spite of this we act in a contrary spirit one towards another.

isaiah/54.bible_quote: For thy Maker is thine husband; The Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.  For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsake
isaiah/54.note: This chapter refers to the mother of 'Abdu'l-Baha.  For the calamities and afflictions mentioned in the whole chapter are such which she suffered...which she endured with patience and thanked God therefore...

isaiah/54.bible_quote: For thy Maker is thine husband; The Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.  For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsake
isaiah/54.note: This chapter refers to the Exalted Leaf, the mother of 'Abdu'l-Baha.  For the calamities and afflictions mentioned in the whole chapter are such [as] she suffered in the path of God.

isaiah/54.bible_quote: For thy Maker is thine husband; The Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.  For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsake
isaiah/54.note: This chapter refers to the Exalted Leaf, the mother of 'Abdu'l-Baha...the humiliation and reproach...she suffered in the path of God no one can refute.

isaiah/54.bible_quote: For thy Maker is thine husband; The Lord of Hosts is his name; and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel; The God of the whole earth shall he be called.  For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsake
isaiah/54.note: This refers to Navvab, Baha'u'llah's wife.

isaiah/55.bible_quote: come, buy...without money and without price...
isaiah/55.note: Since Baha'u'llah offered holy things "without price" the fanatics, fearing loss of prestige, decided to renew their attacks on Baha'u'llah.

isaiah/55.bible_quote: So shall My Word be that goeth forth out of My mouth; it shall not return unto Me void; but it shall accomplish that which I please, and it shall prosper in the thing whereunto I sent it.
isaiah/55.note: The first thing to do is to acquire a thirst for spirituality, the life, the life!...the way to acquire this thirst is to meditate on the future life.  Study the Holy Words, read your Bible, read the Holy Books...

isaiah/55.bible_quote: So shall My Word be that goeth forth out of My mouth it shall noot return unto Me void...
isaiah/55.note: When in London 'Abdu'l-Baha wrote in the old Bible in the City Temple...This Book is the Holy Book of God and of celestial inspiration.  It is the Bible of salvation, the noble Gospel. It is the mystery of the Kingdom and its Light.

isaiah/55.bible_quote: So shall My Word be that goeth forth out of My mouth; it shaoll not return unto Me void; but is shall accomplish that which I please...
isaiah/55.note: The friends and maid-servants should know the value of the Bible, for they are cognizant of the hidden mystery of the Holy Book...The Bibleand the Holy Books of other religions must be studied and read in Baha'i meetings.  This study will widen

isaiah/55.bible_quote: So shall My Word be that goeth forth out of My mouth; it shall not return unto Me void; but it shall accomplish that which I please...
isaiah/55.note: The Word of God penetrates into the core of all things and cannot be resisted...

isaiah/55.bible_quote: the mountains and the hills shall break forth before you into singing and the trees of the field shall clap their hands.
isaiah/55.note: Many passages in the Scriptures are symbolic.  The above expresses rejoicing.  Literal interpretation veiled Christ's Reality from the Jews.

isaiah/61.bible_quote: The Spirit of the Lord God is upon me; because the Lord hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and t
isaiah/61.note: That which was prophesied was not the cause of the Manifestation of Christ.  The record of a thing in the Book does not become the cause of its existence.

isaiah/65.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 79

isaiah/65.note: Addressing the Jewish people, Baha'u'llah revealed "the Strong City is Akka".

isaiah/65.bible_quote: behold I create a new heavens and a new earth.
isaiah/65.note: Alluded to in the Kitab-i-Iqan, the repository of that law which Isaiah anticipated.

isaiah/65.bible_quote: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together...
isaiah/65.note: Symbolic unity of mankind.  How well Isaiah expressed their lack of understanding the real meaning...

isaiah/65.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose-Garden of Acca, p. 5
isaiah/65.bible_quote: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together, and the lion shall eat straw like the bullock...
isaiah/65.note: All the prophetic statements of the coming of the Kingdom will take place in the days of 'Abdu'l-Baha.

isaiah/65.bible_quote: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together...
isaiah/65.note: The reference indicates the unity of mankind.  Jesus Christ did not bring peace, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

isaiah/65.bible_quote: The wolf and the lamb shall feed together...
The wolf and the lamb shall feed together...
isaiah/65.note: This symbolizes the unity and agreement of races, nations and peoples who will come together...spiritually.

daniel/2.bible_quote: And in the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom which shall never be destroyed; it shall stand forever.
daniel/2.note: In Baghdad the Tabernacle of the promised Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long-awaited Kingdom of the Father were unassailably established.

daniel/7.bible_quote: I saw one like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven...

daniel/7.ref: Star of the West, IX:1, p. 7 (*)
daniel/7.bible_quote: I saw in the night visions one like the Son of man...and there was given to Him dominion and glory and a Kingdom.  His dominion is an everlasting dominion which shall not pass away and His Kingdom tha
daniel/7.note: The coming of the Kingdom is foretold in all the Holy Books...especially in Isaiah and Daniel. (*Notes of Mrs. Herrin, 1900)

daniel/8.bible_quote: How long shall be the vision concerning the daily sacrifice and the transgression of desolation...
daniel/8.note: Let us prove the manifestations of the Bab and Baha'u'llah...we shall speak of traditional proofs...

daniel/8.bible_quote: then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.
daniel/8.note: By cleansing of the sanctuary prophesied by Daniel and confirmed by Jesus Christ in His reference to the abomination of desolation  (Mt 24:15) had been accomplished by the coming of the Bab.

daniel/8.bible_quote: unto two thousand, three hundred days then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.
daniel/8.note: Edict of Artaxerxes to rebuild Jerusalem until the birth of Jesus Christ are 456 years and from Jesus Christ's birth until the Manifestation of the Bab there are 1844 years.  1844 plus 456 equals 2300 years.

daniel/8.bible_quote: at the time of the end shall be the vision.
daniel/8.note: Consider how clearly the Manifestation of the Bab is spoken of in the Old and New Testaments...

daniel/9.bible_quote: Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy Holy City.
daniel/9.note: Seventy weeks make 490 years.  The third edict of Artaxerxes in 457 BC plus 33 years of Christ's age makes 490, the time announced by Daniel for the Manifestation of Christ.

daniel/9.bible_quote: from the going forth of the commandment to restore and build Jerusalem, unto the Messiah the Prince, shall be seven weeks, and threescore and two weeks...and after the threescore and two weeks shall t
daniel/9.note: after the termination of rebuilding Jerusalem until the ascension of Christ there will be 62 weeks. The seven weeks are the duration of building Jerusalem which took 49 years; add 7 weeks to 62 it makes 69 weeks, and in the last week (69-70)

daniel/9.bible_quote: and for the overspreading of abominations He shall make it desolate...
daniel/9.note: Ended at the time of the Bab's coming.

daniel/11.bible_quote: and shall take away the daily sacrifice and they shall place the abomination that maketh desolate.
even to the time of the end because it is yet for a time appointed.
daniel/11.note: Explained by Baha'u'llah in Kitab-i-Iqan, p. 161-72, as ushered in by the Bab.

daniel/11.bible_quote: even to the time of the end because it is yet for a time appointed.

daniel/12.bible_quote: And at that time shall Michael stand up, the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people...
daniel/12.note: A third seven year plan must culminate in marking the centennial of the formal assumption by Baha'u'llah of the prophetic office associated with Daniel's prophecy...

daniel/12.bible_quote: O Daniel, shut up the words and seal the book even to the time of the end...
daniel/12.note: Revealed on the even of the Declaration of Baha'u'llah which broke the seals of the Book disclosed the meanings destined to remain closed until the end of time.

daniel/12.bible_quote: Daniel, shut up the words and seal the Book even to the time of the end...

daniel/12.bible_quote: Daniel, shut up the words and seal the Book even to the time of the end...
daniel/12.note: All the sacred mysteries were sealed but now Baha'u'llah has broken the seals revealed the meanings and we can understand the Realities.

daniel/12.bible_quote: sware by Him that liveth forever, that it shall be for a time, times and a half...
daniel/12.note: The Bab, precursor of Baha'u'llah appeared in the year 1260.

daniel/12.bible_quote: Daniel, for the words are closed up and sealed till the time of the end.
daniel/12.note: Baha'u'llah unseals the Book in the Kitab-i-Iqan.

daniel/12.bible_quote: Daniel, for the words are closed up and sealed till the time of the end.
Daniel, for the words are closed up and sealed till the time of the end.
And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be a thousand two hundred and ninety days.
daniel/12.note: No verse with the day of the Lord in Daniel, but this verse gives the date of Baha'u'llah's declaration, as 1290 days from the proclamation of the mission of Humannad was the year 1280 of the Hijra, 1863-64 of the Gregorian calendar.

daniel/12.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose-Garden at Acca,p. 5
daniel/12.bible_quote: And from the time of that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be one thousand two hundred and ninety days.
daniel/12.note: 1290 years after Muhammad was the date that Baha'u'llah announced His mission to the world as the Promised One. (from talk by Mirza Assad'u'llah on 11/7/1908)

daniel/12.bible_quote: And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be one thousand two hundred and ninety days.
daniel/12.note: This is the year 1863-64 in which Baha'u'llah made His Declaration.

daniel/12.bible_quote: And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away, and the abomination that maketh desolate set up there shall be one thousand two hundred and ninety days.
daniel/12.note: The year 1290 from the proclamation of the mission of Muhammad was the year 1280 AH and 1863 AD when Baha'u'llah made His declaration.

daniel/12.bible_quote: And from the time that the daily sacrifice shall be taken away and the abomination that maketh desolate set up, there shall be 1290 days.
daniel/12.note: 1290 was the year Hijra 1280 or 1863-64 Gregorian era.  This refers to Baha'u'llah's announcement to His disciples at Baghdad.

daniel/12.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose-Garden at Acca, p. 5
daniel/12.bible_quote: Blessed is he that waiteth and cometh to the 1335 days.
daniel/12.note: In the year 1335 you shall see the great unfoldment.  We have nine years (i.e., 1917) to witness the outcome of these events.  The climax is in the days of 'Abdu'l-Baha. (From talk by Mirza Assad'u'llah on 11/7/1908)

daniel/12.bible_quote: Blessed is he that waiteth, and cometh to the 1335 days.
daniel/12.note: The hundred lunar years destined to immediately preceed that consummation.

daniel/12.bible_quote: Blessed is he that waiteth and cometh to the 1335 days.
daniel/12.note: What greater reward than that the consummation of the third seven year plan should synchronize with the greatest of Jubilees related to the year 1335, mentioned by Daniel.

daniel/12.ref: Star of the West, XI:1, p. 17 (*)
daniel/12.bible_quote: Blessed is he that cometh to the 1335 days.
daniel/12.note: This year should be taken as a solar year and not a lunar year. (*Tablet to Faraz 'Allah Zaki al-Kurdi)

jeremiah/23.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74

jeremiah/23.note: We have gathered under the shadow of Baha'u'llah, this is the supper of the Lord.  The Heavenly Books prophesy that they shall come from the East and the West and sit down in the Kingdom of God.

jeremiah/23.bible_quote: the days will come, that I will raise unto David a righteous Branch...He shall be called the Lord of Righteousness.
jeremiah/23.note: The Jews prayed for the coming of the Messiah but literal interpretation of the Scriptures veiled them.

jeremiah/30.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 144

jeremiah/30.note: hearken...unto the voice of Jeremiah...Great is this day and it hath no equal--Wert thou to observe with the eye of fairness, thou wouldst perceive the greatness of this Day.

ezekiel/4.ref: Star of the West, XIII:6, p. 144
ezekiel/4.bible_quote: have appointed thee each day for a year.
ezekiel/4.note: In the Bible in the days of the Lord, one day equals one year.

ezekiel/30.bible_quote: Son of man prophecy and say, Thus saith the Lord God; Howl ye, Woe worth the day!  For the day is near, even the day of the Lord is near, a cloudy day...
ezekiel/30.note: The day of woe is the Day of the Lord...woe for the neglectful...sinners, the ignorant...

ezekiel/30.bible_quote: For the day is near, even the day of the Lord.
ezekiel/30.note: Prophecy of trouble in the Day of Baha'u'llah.

ezekiel/30.bible_quote: For the day is near, even the Day of the Lord is near...
ezekiel/30.note: Therefore we must strive that the material world may be reformed...When this reformation affects every degree then will come the Day of the Lord.

ezekiel/30.bible_quote: For the day is near, even the Day of the Lord is near...
ezekiel/30.note: The day of God, great indeed is this day!  Allusions to it in all the sacred Scriptures as the Day of God, the Day in which mankind can behold the Face and hear the Voice of the Promised One...

ezekiel/34.note: We have gathered under the shadow of Baha'u'llah, this is the supper of the Lord.  The Heavenly Books prophecy that they shall come from the East and the West and sit down in the Kingodm of God.

ezekiel/34.bible_quote: I will bring them from the countries to their own land...I will feed My flock upon the high mountains of Israel shall their fold be...
ezekiel/34.note: Revolutionary changes enabled the remnant and outcasts of Israel to assemble in their own land...upon the mountain.

ezekiel/35.note: The forgiving Lord revealed Himself at Mt. Paran or Mt. Sinai or Mt. Seir.

My tabernacle shall be with them...
ezekiel/37.note: Tablernacle was first erected in Baghdad symbolized in the law of Baha'u'llah.

hosea/2.bible_quote: I will give her the valley of Achor for a door of hope.

hosea/11.note: Jesus lived in Egypt as prophecied.  There were fifty Gospels and all but 4 were non-canonical.

joel/1.bible_quote: Alas for the day! for the day of the Lord is at hand and as a destruction from the Almighty shall it come.
joel/1.note: The advent of Baha'u'llah is the day of woe.

joel/2.bible_quote: let all the inhabitants of the land tremble; for the Day of the Lord cometh for it is nigh at hand...a Day of darkness and of gloominess, a Day of clouds and thick darkness...
joel/2.note: Baha'u'llah's Day is this to all Christians.  To Baha'u'llah's Day Joel and Zephaniah had both referred to as the Day of Jehovah.

joel/2.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 142-43
joel/2.bible_quote: for the Day of the Lord is great and very terrible...

joel/2.bible_quote: And I will shew wonders in the heavens and in the earth...the sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and terrible day of the Lord come.
joel/2.note: Jews believe Messiah is yet to come.  According to the Jews, Jesus Christ fulfilled none of these conditions, therefore they rejected Him.

joel/2.bible_quote: the great and terrible Day of the Lord.
joel/2.note: To Baha'u'llah's Day Joel and Zephaniah had both referred as the Day of Jehovah.

joel/3.bible_quote: For the Day of the Lord is near...but the Lord will be the hope of His people, and the strength of the children of Israel...
joel/3.note: When this reformation affects every degree then will come the Day of the Lord.

amos/1.bible_quote: The Lord will roar from Zion and utter His voice from Jerusalem; and the habitations of the shepherds shall mourn, and the top of Carmel shall wither.
amos/1.note: O Shaykh, reflect upon these words addressed by Him Who is the Desire of the world to Amos.

amos/1.bible_quote: The Lord will roar from Zion and utter His voice from Jerusalem, and the habitations of the shepherds shall mourn and the top of Carmel shall wither.
amos/1.note: Amos foretold the advent of Baha'u'llah.

jonah/1.note: Explanation of miracles, analogy to resurrection of Jesus Christ, and the three Dispensations.

micah/3.bible_quote: And they shall be mine, saith the Lord of Hosts in that day when I make up my jewels...
micah/3.note: Some of the prophets two or three thousand years before prophesied the Lord of Hosts would be manifested in the Holy Land, fulfilled by Baha'u'llah.

micah/4.note: The time fore-ordained unto the peoples of the earth is now come, promises fulfilled.  Out of Zion hath gone forth the Law of God and Jerusalem and the hills and the land thereof are filled with the glory of His Revelation.

micah/4.bible_quote: and He will teach us His ways and we will walk in His paths...they shall sit everyone under his vine and under his fig tree...
micah/4.note: Prophecy of the unity of mankind in the teachings of Baha'u'llah.

micah/4.bible_quote: and they shall beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks; nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
micah/4.note: How could Christ be the promised Christ?  In the human kingdom warfare was to cease.  Jews blind to the Reality of Christ.  Purpose of the prophetic words was the inner symbolic significance.

micah/4.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46
micah/4.bible_quote: they shall beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning-hooks; nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.
micah/4.note: A world catastrophe, a fiery ordeal, can alone usher in the promised kingdom of the promised Heavenly Father.

micah/4.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205

micah/4.note: Baha'u'llah brought the plan for the unity of mankind and universal peace.

micah/7.note: One of the prophecies fulfilled in the enforced travels of Baha'u'llah.  This is what we may call a miracle.

habakkuk/3.note: The forgiving Lord hath revealed Himself on Mt. Paran.  Explanation of the Trinity.

zephaniah/1.note: These refer to the century of the Blessed Beauty. (* Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)

zephaniah/3.ref: Star of the West, XI:18, p. 302 (*)
zephaniah/3.bible_quote: For then will I turn to the people a pure language that they may all call upon the name of the Lord, to serve Him with one consent.
zephaniah/3.note: Universal language will hasten the coming of the millennial day when contesting races, warring nations, inimical religions will come together in love and unity. (*Address in Edinburgh, Scotland, 1/7/1913)

haggai/2.bible_quote: And I will shake all nations, and the desire of all nations shall come.
haggai/2.note: Baha'u'llah is the One to whom Haggai referred as the desire of all nations.

haggai/2.bible_quote: and the desire of all nations shall come.
haggai/2.note: Baha'u'llah was the One whom Haggai called the desire of all nations.

zechariah/2.bible_quote: many nations shall be joined and I will dwell in the midst...and thou shalt know that the Lord of Hosts hath sent me unto thee...for the Lord shall inherit Judah.
zechariah/2.note: Good news that the Lord of Hosts should be manifested in the Holy Land, fulfilled by Baha'u'llah.

zechariah/2.bible_quote: lo, I come, and I will dwell in the midst of thee, saith the Lord.
zechariah/2.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His Mission.

zechariah/2.bible_quote: Rejoice, O daughter of Zion! for lo I come and I will dwell in the midst of thee, saith the Lord.  And many nations shall be joined to the Lord in that day and shall be My people...
zechariah/2.note: When Baha'u'llah came to this prison in the Holy Land the wise men realized that the glad tidings which God gave through theprophets were manifested and that God was faithful to His promise.

zechariah/8.ref: Star of the West, VIII:14, p. 191 (*)
zechariah/8.bible_quote: Yea, many people and strong nations shall come to seek the Lord of Hosts in Jerusalem and to pray before the Lord.
zechariah/8.note: This is the Day in whch the Lord of Hosts has come down from heaven on the Clouds of Glory!  In which nations and governments will enter into an eternal bond of amity... (*Centennial celebration of the birth of Baha'u'llah)

zechariah/9.bible_quote: Behold the King cometh unto thee; he is just, and having salvation, lowly and riding upon an ass...
zechariah/9.note: This prophecy was fulfilled in Jesus Christ riding on the colt and making His triumphal entry into Jerusalem.  Literal interpretation veiled the truth of the Messiah from the Jews.

zechariah/6.bible_quote: And speak unto him saying...Behold the Branch and He shall build the Temple of the Lord.
zechariah/6.note: The Surih of the Temple order to be written in the shape of a pentacle, symbolizing the Temple of man, identified with the Temple mentioned by Zechariah.

zechariah/6.bible_quote: Thus speaketh the Lord of Hosts, saying, Behold the man whose name is the Branch...he shall build the Temple of the Lord.
zechariah/6.note: To Baha'u'llah Zechariah referred as the Branch who shall grow out of His place and shall build the Temple of the Lord.

zechariah/6.bible_quote: Thus speaketh the Lord of Hosts, saying, Behold the man whose name is the Branch; He shall grow up out of His place and he shall build the Temple of the Lord.
zechariah/6.note: In Baghdad the Tabernacle of the promised Lord of Hosts was first erected and foundations of the long-awaited Kingdom of the Father were unassailably established.  Within its walls the Most Great House of God, the shrine around which will circle

malachi/3.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
malachi/3.bible_quote: Then they that feared the Lord spake often one to another; and the Lord hearkened and heard it and a book of remembrance was written...they shall be mine, saith the Lord of Hosts in that day when I ma
malachi/3.note: This refers to the friends of God. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)

malachi/3.bible_quote: And they shall be mine, saith the Lord of Hosts, is that day when I make up my jewels...
malachi/3.note: Some of the two or three thousand years before prophecied the Lord of Hosts would be manifested in the Holy Land, fulfilled in Baha'u'llah.

malachi/4.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
malachi/4.bible_quote: But unto you that fear My name shall the Sun of righteousness arise with healing in His wings.
malachi/4.note: Great is this Day!  The allusions to it in all the sacred Scriptures as the Day of God attest its greatness.

malachi/4.bible_quote: and I will send you Elijah before the coming of the great and dreadful Day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: Elijah was expected to be sent from heaven, and Christ said Elijah is none other than John the Baptist (Mt 11:14).

malachi/4.bible_quote: I will send you Elijah before the coming of the day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: The Bab was the return of John the Baptist expected by the Christians and the return of Elijah anticipated by the Jews.

malachi/4.bible_quote: the great and dreadful day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: Advent of Baha'u'llah called the Day of God.

malachi/4.bible_quote: I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful Day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: oneness of power, of bestowal of revelation of the prophets.

malachi/4.bible_quote: Behold I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: Bab's advent marks the return of Elijah to Mt. Carmel.

malachi/4.bible_quote: Behold I will send you Elijah the Prophet, before the coming of the great and dreadful Day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: In the Bible the Jews have the promise of the Lord of Hosts and the Messiah; in the Gospel the return of Christ and Elijah is promised.

malachi/4.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
malachi/4.bible_quote: Behold, I will send you Elijah the Prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord.
malachi/4.note: These tidings are the same in all the Holy Books expressed in different ways, two successive Manifestations.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Geneology of Jesus.
matthew/1.note: His Holiness Christ was a unique and single personage born of the lineage of Israel...All Christians believe that His Holiness Moses was a Prophet.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Geneology of Jesus.
matthew/1.note: In consequence of Abraham's exile a Moses and a being like Chirst were manifested from his posterity.

matthew/1.bible_quote: When as [Jesus'] mother, Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Baha'u'llah upholds the purity of the Virgin Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: When as [Jesus'] mother, Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child by the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Baha'u'llah upholds the purity of the Virgin Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Were a myriad voices to be raised, no ear would listen if upon a fatherless Child hath been conferred prophethood...

matthew/1.bible_quote: the Lord appeared unto him in a dream saying, Joseph fear not to take unto thee Mary, thy wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Jesus came from Heaven, though outwardly He came from the womb of Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: the Lord, appeared unto him in a dream saying, Joseph, fear not to take unto thee Mary, thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Physically [Jesus] was born of a woman but the Christ reality is from the city of eternity.

matthew/1.bible_quote: that which is conceived in Mary is of the Holy Ghost.  And she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call His name Jesus.
matthew/1.note: The body of Christ was born of Mary of Nazareth but the Spirit was of God.  The human capacities were limited but the strength of His Spirit was immeasurable.

matthew/1.bible_quote: fear not to take Mary, thy wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Jesus came to the world through the power of God, born of the Holy Spirit and of the blessed Virgin Mary.  Mary His mother was a saint from Heaven...God made Mary to be exalted above all other women.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Joseph, fear not to take Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: The people of Islam were taught to realize how Jesus came from God and was born of the Spirit, and that He must be glorified of all men.

matthew/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
matthew/1.bible_quote: fear not to take Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Baha'u'llah asserted that the mystery of the immaculate conception of the Virgin Mary is confessed.

matthew/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 119
matthew/1.bible_quote: Joseph, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Baha'u'llah designated Jesus Christ as One Who appeared out of the breath of the Holy Ghost.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Joseph, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha pleaded with the Muhammadans to investigate the truth concerning Jesus, in the Qur'an and the bible.  If the Muhammadans and Christians would investigate truth, the outcome would be unity.

matthew/1.bible_quote: thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary, thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Explanation: How was Christ born of the Holy Spirit?

matthew/1.bible_quote: thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
matthew/1.note: Though Jesus said He came from heaven, many knew that he was born of Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Joseph...fear not to take unto thee Mary, thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Jesus had a two-fold nature, physically He was born of Mary but His spirit descended from heaven.  The second coming will be similar.
