matthew/1.bible_quote: that which is conceived in her [Mary] is of the Holy Ghost.  And she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call His name Jesus.
matthew/1.note: The Christ is born of the Holy Spirit; He is raised up by the Holy Spirit; He is a descendant of the Holy Spirit--the Reality of Christ does not descend from Adam--Christ is a quickening spirit.

matthew/1.ref: Star of the West, VIII:8, p. 98
matthew/1.bible_quote: But while he thought on these things, behold the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a dream saying, Joseph, thou son of David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for that which is conceived
matthew/1.note: Christ was born of the Holy Spirit.  He was the sun and His rays shone upon His disciples through His teachings.

matthew/1.ref: Star of the West, XVII:10, p. 328
matthew/1.bible_quote: the Lord appeared unto Joseph saying, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: In the Qur'an it states that Jesus came into the world through the quickening breaths of the Holy Spirit...O Arabians, you must believe in Jesus Christ as the product of the Holy Spirit.

matthew/1.bible_quote: the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a dream saying...fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/1.note: Christ came from heaven, though outwardly He came from the womb of Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Joseph, fear not to take unto thee Mary, thy wife; for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost...she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call His name Jesus...
matthew/1.note: In reality Jesus did descend from heaven although He was born of Mary.  He was a heavenly man.

matthew/1.bible_quote: And she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call His name Jesus...
matthew/1.note: The reality, perfections and Spirit of Christ came from God but He was born of Mary.

matthew/1.bible_quote: And she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call His name Jesus...
matthew/1.note: Jesus' reality from heaven; his body earthly.  Literal interpretation veiled the truth from the Jews.

matthew/1.bible_quote: Go quickly and tell His disciples...He goeth before you into Galilee--there ye shall see Him.
matthew/1.note: It was a woman, Mary Magdalene who confirmed the wavering disciples in their faith...Mary Magdalene was a mere villager, a peasant woman yet she became the means of consolation and confirmation to the disciples of Christ.

matthew/2.bible_quote: there came wise men from the East saying, Where is He that is born king of the Jews?  For we have seen His star in the East and are come to worship Him...
matthew/2.note: Special stars to herald coming of Abraham, Moses, Jesus.  This was a sign that was manifested in the visible heaven.
The Holy Land was the cradle of Christianity.

His Holiness Christ appeared in Palestine...the people of the West embraced and promulgated Christianity.  I have great hopes that the lights of Baha'u'llah may also find the fullest manifestation in these Western regions.

matthew/2.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 23
matthew/2.bible_quote: there came wise men from the East...when they saw the star they rejoiced...they fell down and worshipped [Christ]...
matthew/2.note: Orientals came to see Baha'u'llah as He walked up and down in the garden.  They kept faces turned to Him, reminding 'Abdu'l-Baha of pictures of the Wise Men worshipping the Christ.

matthew/2.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 58
matthew/2.bible_quote: the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph saying...take the young child and His mother, and flee into Egypt...and was there until the death of Herod.
matthew/2.note: Baha'u'llah recalls the care of Jesus by God, and expects this protection.

matthew/2.bible_quote: the Lord appeared to Joseph...take the young child and His mother into Egypt...and he took the young child and His mother into Egypt...
matthew/2.note: The enforced departure of Baha'u'llah to Baghdad recalled the flight of Joseph to Egypt.

matthew/2.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 7
matthew/2.bible_quote: [Joseph] took the young child and departed into Egypt and was there until the death of Herod that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Prophet (Hosea XI:1).
matthew/2.note: The fifth Gospel gave other history of Jesus--it wasn't considered canonical.  There were fifty Gospels.

matthew/2.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 115
matthew/2.bible_quote: And He came and dwelt in a city called Nazareth.
matthew/2.note: I will show you the meaning thereof--although He came from Nazareth, He also came from heaven. (John 16:28)

matthew/3.bible_quote: In those days John the Baptist preaching in the wilderness of Judaea and saying, Repent ye; for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
matthew/3.note: Yahya, John the Baptist, the herald of the Word, Jesus.

matthew/3.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 36
matthew/3.bible_quote: In those days came John the Baptist preaching in the wilderness...prepare ye the way of the Lord...
matthew/3.note: John the Baptist came before the Christ.  The Forerunner announces the Manifestation.  Two forerunners, Ahmad and Kazim before the Manifestation of this Day.

matthew/3.bible_quote: For this is He that was spoken of by the Prophet Isaias.
matthew/3.note: Return explained, and references to Elias in John 1:20,21.

matthew/3.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
matthew/3.bible_quote: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord...
matthew/3.note: Elijah came but was not recognized in John...not the entity of Elijah but his qualities that John embodied.

matthew/3.bible_quote: He that cometh after Me is mightier than I...He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
matthew/3.note: Christ says that you must be baptized with the spirit...This spirit of faith is the flame of reality...cause of eternal illumination.

matthew/3.bible_quote: He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
matthew/3.note: Baptism and fire are one.  By the fire of the love of God the veil is burnt which separates us from Heavenly realities.

matthew/3.bible_quote: He that cometh after Me is mightier than I...He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.
matthew/3.note: He who is baptized by the Holy Spirit is a ...manifestation of divine mercy to mankind.

matthew/3.bible_quote: I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance; but He that cometh after me shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
matthew/3.note: Christ appeared in order to illumine the world of humanity...to unite hearts.

matthew/3.bible_quote: He that cometh after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not worthy to bear; He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
matthew/3.note: Through Christ they were intended to be baptized by the Holy Spirit, attain a new spirit and realize life everlasting.  No relation obtains among us--yet ideal bonds blend us.

matthew/3.bible_quote: I indeed baptize you with water unto repentance...
matthew/3.note: Baptism has become a form, ritual, but had a spiritual meaning.

matthew/3.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38
matthew/3.bible_quote: but He that cometh after me is mightier than I...He shall baptize you with water and with fire.
matthew/3.note: The Spirit is conducive to everlasting glory; it is the cause of exultation of the world of humanity.  May each one of you become the recipient of the Spirit.  This is My hope.

matthew/3.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 103
matthew/3.bible_quote: I, John baptize you with water unto repentance...but He that cometh after me shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
matthew/3.note: These ceremonies have spiritual meaning but no spiritual effect upon the soul.  It is not true that if a child dies without the baptism that his soul is lost--the child has not sinned and goes from this world free from faults and defects.

matthew/3.bible_quote: Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to Jordan unto John to be baptized of him.  But john forbade Him saying, I have need to be baptized of Thee and comest Thou to me?  And Jesus answering said unto him, Su
matthew/3.note: Explanation of "to complete the law of the former religion".  He confirmed the past law.  Christ desired this institution to be used at that time.

matthew/3.bible_quote: And Jesus when He was baptized went up--out of the water and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting upon Him.  And lo, a voice from heaven saying, This is My beloved Son in whom
matthew/3.note: God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit, Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

matthew/3.bible_quote: And Jesus, when He was baptized went up out of the water, and lo, the heavens opened unto Him and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove, and lighting upon Him...
matthew/3.note: The call of Baha'u'llah surpassed the experience of Jesus Christ at His baptism.

matthew/3.bible_quote: and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting upon Him...
matthew/3.note: Most Great Spirit designated by Baha'u'llah symbolized in Christian Dispensation as the dove.

matthew/3.bible_quote: and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove from heaven...
matthew/3.note: The descent of the dove upon Jesus Christ at His baptism symbolic of the annunciation of the angel to Baha'u'llah regarding His mission.

matthew/3.bible_quote: and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove from heaven...
matthew/3.note: This was not a material dove..it was s spiritual state expressed by a figure.  Symbolism abounds in Scriptures.

matthew/3.bible_quote: and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting upon Him...
matthew/3.note: Before this the Holy Spirit did not manifest Himself in Him.  Humanly the Manifestations are unaware of certain things, but their heavenly condition embraces all mysteries.

matthew/3.ref: Star of the West, XIV:9, p. 274
matthew/3.bible_quote: and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and lighting upon Him...
matthew/3.note: The story about the dove is simply a metaphor.  No dove came down.  The Holy Spirit was always with Christ.  He knew about His mission from early childhood.

matthew/3.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
matthew/3.bible_quote: And lo a voice from heaven saying, This is My beloved Son in whom I am well pleased.
matthew/3.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.

matthew/3.bible_quote: And lo a voice from heaven saying, This is My beloved Son in whom I am well pleased.
matthew/3.note: The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ...meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.

matthew/3.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
matthew/3.bible_quote: And lo a voice from heaven saying, This is My beloved Son in whom I am well pleased.
matthew/3.note: Manifestations are expected but when they come they are rejected because of the blindness of the people.

matthew/3.bible_quote: And when He saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to His baptism, He said unto them, O generation of vipers, who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
matthew/3.note: These same Scriptures, however, unanimously condemn the people that inhabit this land.  They have, at one time, been stigmatized as the "generation of vipers."  Behold how this Wronged One is now, whilst surrounded by a "generation of vipers,"

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, IV:18, p. 305
matthew/4.bible_quote: Then was Jesus led up of the Spirit into the wilderness...and when He had fasted fourty days and fourty nights...
matthew/4.note: In the beginning the disciples and Christians fasted.  Later they changed fasting into lenten observances.

matthew/4.bible_quote: Temptation of Jesus Christ by Satan in the wilderness.
matthew/4.note: Explanation; the Holy Spirit in Christ refused to worship earthly glories...were as nothing to Him in comparison with doing the Will of the Father.

matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus saw Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea; for they were fishers.
matthew/4.note: Why were these disciples able to do what philosophers and scientists failed to accomplish?  You have the example in Peter who was assisted by the Holy Spirit, as have been all enlightened humanity...universal education...only through the Holy Spirit

matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus saw Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea for they were fishers...and He saith unto them, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: The two great Apostles, St. Peter and St. John the Evangelist, were once simple humble workmen.  By the power of the Holy Spirit their souls were illumined. (Acts 2:1)

matthew/4.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus saw Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea for they were fishers...and they immediately left the ship and followed Him.
matthew/4.note: Baha'u'llah called the attention of the Pope to the analogy of the acceptance of simple fishermen and rejection of the learned.

matthew/4.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus saith unto them, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.  And they straightway left their nets and followed Him.
matthew/4.note: Jesus said, 'Come ye after Me and I will make you fishers of men.'  In this day We say, 'Come ye after Me that We may make you to become quickeners of mankind.'

matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus, walking by the Sea of Galilee saw Simon Peter and Andrew...they were fishers.  Jesus saith unto them, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Through the aid and assistances of God they became mighty ones of God.  Peter was transformed.

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 6 (*)
matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus saw Simon Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea; for they were fishers.
matthew/4.note: Simple men filled with the Holy Spirit influenced humanity to such an extent that thousands would die to uphold their teachings. (* Discourse delivered in London, Paris)

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 6-7 (*)
matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus saw Simon Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea, for they were fishers...Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Peter had no education.  He was transformed by the power of the Spirit. (Acts 2)  He who was educated by the Holy Spirit can in his turn educate others. (Acts 2:42)  (* Discourse in Paris or London)

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39
matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus saw Simon Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea, for they were fishers.
matthew/4.note: Do not look at present condition; contemplate the future.  Insignificant persons but how great they became!  Bounties of Baha'u'llah shall in the future be made manifest.

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232
matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus walked by the Sea of Galilee, called Simon Peter and Andrew his brother, casting a net for they were fishers.
matthew/4.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit (Acts 2:41), Peter became a great preacher.  This is what is meant by everlasting life, by baptism with the Holy spirit and by being born again.

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, XI:15, p. 258 (*)
matthew/4.bible_quote: Jesus called Simon Peter and Andrew casting a net...for they were fishers...These twelve Jesus sent forth, As ye go preach, heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils...
matthew/4.note: The Apostles were fishers.  Consider to what a high station they did rise!  Their sovereignty runs to eternity.  (* Tablet to Roy C. Williams)

matthew/4.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus called Simon Peter and Andrew casting a net into the sea, for they were fishers...Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: The disciples were humble fishermen.  All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit.

matthew/4.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 38
matthew/4.bible_quote: And [Jesus] saith unto them, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Peter was the first Apostle to be called.  Observe how this crude and helpless instrument was transformed by the power of C hrist.  How much greater the power of the Father, in Baha'u'llah.

matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus called Simon Peter and Andrew...Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Baha'u'llah gives a greater promise to His followers than that of Jesus to Peter.

matthew/4.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 124
matthew/4.bible_quote: And He saith...Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Fishers of men is an individual call; quickeners of mankind, universal, Baha'u'llah asserts.

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, IX:10, p. 112 (Talk 6/13/1914)
matthew/4.bible_quote: And Jesus saith unto them, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Many common souls and the most degraded, advanced and followed the divine Manifestations.  Everyone testifies that all the learned philosophers and nobles of every nation were humble in the presence of the Blessed Beauty...became believers

matthew/4.ref: Star of the West, XIII:7, p. 173 (*)
matthew/4.bible_quote: And He saith, Follow Me and I will make you fishers of men.
matthew/4.note: Unity and concord is their bait; service and self-sacrifice is their sport...Let the Baha'is be the fishermen of the sea of spirituality.  Let them not lose their courage. (* DMAS)

matthew/7.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
matthew/7.bible_quote: And why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye?  Thou hypocrite, first cast out the beam that is in thine own eye...
matthew/7.note: Love that melts all opposition, conquers all foes, sweeps away all barriers; a love that triumphs over all obstacles...

matthew/7.ref: Star of the West, II:2, p. 7
matthew/7.bible_quote: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them...
matthew/7.note: Each one can choose between justice and injustice.  Be just.  Consider the rights of others before your own.

matthew/7.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose-Garden at Acca, p. 20
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: Each Prophet left after Him some sign by which He meant some significance.  In this day we are commanded to love mankind...

matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: If his morals become spiritual, his aspirations heavenly, his actions conformable to the will of God, man has attained the image of His Creator.

matthew/7.ref: Star of the West, VIII:6, p. 68
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: By belief we mean that the reality of man will be characterized by divine characteristics.

matthew/7.ref: Star of the West, XV:6, p. 163
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: He will seek to be one of the Kingdom...until this station be attained by man his life will be utterly devoid of results.

matthew/7.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: Fruits are the spiritual grapes.  The fruits of the Tree should be our desire.

matthew/7.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: Those who are born of the Spirit have all the Divine qualities of growth.  Without these qualities they are dead.

matthew/7.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 59
matthew/7.bible_quote: Ye shall know them by their fruits...
matthew/7.note: If a believer shows forth divine qualities we know the true faith is in his heart.  We should give evidence in our actions.  The real quality is revealed in his words and deeds.

matthew/7.bible_quote: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down and cast into the fire...by their fruits ye shall know them.
matthew/7.note: God requires each person to produce something towards the building of the Kingdom of heaven on earth.

matthew/7.bible_quote: Every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down and cast into the fire.  Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them...he that doeth the will of My father shall enter into the Kingdom of h
matthew/7.note: His Holiness Jesus Christ summoned all to the Most Great Peace through the acquisition of pure morals.

matthew/7.ref: Star of the West, XV:8, p. 229
matthew/7.bible_quote: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
matthew/7.note: If a believer shows forth divine qualities we know the true Faith is in his heart.  Three essentials to Faith: to confess with the tongue; to believe in the heart; to give evidence in our actions.

matthew/7.bible_quote: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
matthew/7.note: Therefore we must strive with life and heart that our deeds may be better...cultivate love for all the world.

matthew/7.bible_quote: Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them.
matthew/7.note: Thou has said aright, that verily 'Abdu'l-Baha looketh to deeds and not to words

matthew/7.bible_quote: the people were astonished at His doctrine, for He taught them as one having authority.
matthew/7.note: Christ's intention was to promote a Cause which was to educate the human world...to enlighten all mankind.

matthew/8.bible_quote: And behold there came a leper and worshipped Him...and Jesus touched him saying...be thou clean.
matthew/8.note: Through Jesus Christ the leper received healing from the leprosy of ignorance.

matthew/8.bible_quote: Except ye be converted and become as little children ye shall not enter into the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/8.note: Although thou art small thou wilt become great in the Kingdom.  Be obediant...add to thy faith...

matthew/8.bible_quote: See thou tell no man...
matthew/8.note: If you give the same teaching to all some might be retarded by them instead of helped.

matthew/8.bible_quote: Many shall come from the East and the West...and shall sit down..in the Kingdom...But the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out...and there shall be weeping...
matthew/8.note: The Divine Gardener cuts off the dry or weak branch.  Separate yourselves from the evil--it is like a disease, contagious.

matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down with Abraham...in the Kingdom of heaven.  But the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness.
matthew/8.note: They did not see these conditions fulfilled.  Romand tyranny enveloped the world.  They rejected Jesus Christ, did not see the Kingdom of heaven.

matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and the West and shall sit down with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, in the Kingdom of heaven.  But the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out into utter darkness...
matthew/8.note: Some of the nearest are cut off, while those from a distance receive their gifts.

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, VIII:14, p. 191 (*)
matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down...in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: This is the Day when the East and the West shall embrace each other like unto two lovers...war and contention shall be forgotten... (* Centennial celebration of the birth of Baha'u'llah)

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, VIII:17, p. 225
matthew/8.bible_quote: And I say unto you that many shall come from the East and West and sit down with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: Know of a certainty that the question of the gathering of the descendants of Abraham in Jerusalem is a fact.

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 25
matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and the West and shall sit down...in the Kingdom of heaven but the children of the Kingdom shall be cast out into utter darkness...
matthew/8.note: The Divine Gardener cuts off the weak branch from the good tree and grafts it to the branch from another tree.  Persons through their acts and words deprived themselves from the bestowals of the Blessed Beauty.

matthew/8.bible_quote: I say unto you that many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: For the brilliancy of the Kingdom of Abha will shine and the spirituality of 'Abdu'l-Baha become manifest.  Divine food served in loving friendship.

matthew/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 2
matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down...in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: It is a heavenly privilege to know the Truth and become a child of the Kingdom.

matthew/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 72
matthew/8.bible_quote: many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down...in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: May all believers in the world be so joined...nations be as one family.

matthew/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74-75
matthew/8.bible_quote: and many shall come from the East and West and shall sit down...in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/8.note: This is the blessed supper of the Lord...The heavenly Books prophesy that they shall come from the East and West to sit down in the Kingdom of God...in spiritual unity.

matthew/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 103
matthew/8.bible_quote: they brought unto Him many that were possessed with devils, and He cast out the spirits with His word, and healed all that were sick...
matthew/8.note: The effect of a Manifestation is to drive out all that is evil and replace the natural growht of virtues.

matthew/8.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 49
matthew/8.bible_quote: The foxes have holes and the birds have nests but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head...
matthew/8.note: Baha'u'llah's reason for Jesus' celibacy as He urged monks to marry.

matthew/8.bible_quote: The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: So fierce was the opposition to Jesus that He wandered from place to place.  All Manifestations were fiercely opposed.

matthew/8.bible_quote: but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: He will be seated on the throne of David; behold this man has not so much as a mat whereon to sit.  The Jews did not see the reality of Christ.

matthew/8.bible_quote: but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: Manifestations met with fierce opposition by divines.

matthew/8.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 49; Paris Talks, p. 55
matthew/8.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: he did not sit on the throne of David, He was a poor man.  The Jews said, This is not the One for whom we wait.

matthew/8.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 83
matthew/8.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: Treatment of Jesus by Jewish leaders analogous to that of Baha'u'llah by the divines.

matthew/8.bible_quote: The foxes have their holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: Jesus Christ was an Exemplar, detached from all that would bind Him to the material, detached from desire.

matthew/8.bible_quote: but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: Let us compare the lives of some of the Popes with the religion of Christ.  Christ, hungry...Pope passes his time in utmost splendor.  Christ hurt no one--some of the Popes killed innocent people--refer to history.

matthew/8.bible_quote: The foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
matthew/8.note: Remember the poverty of Jesus Christ and the Jews' persecution...That poverty was a light which hath illumined heaven and earth.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: This is explained by Isaiah (VI:9), Hear ye indeed but understand not; and see ye indeed but perceive not.  Dead if their hearts have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: The spirit of faith is the flame of reality...cause of eternal illumination.  Though the souls of humanity are living, yet if they are deprived of contact with the Spirit, they are as dead.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Without the protection and quickening of the Holy Spirit the human world would be extinguished.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Souls alive with animal and rational soul...Christ declared that they are dead in that they were devoid of the spirit of Faith.  Spiritual deadness, bestowal of the Holy Spirit.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: The alive are those souls in whom the spirit of life is breathed from the Presence of God...all besides them are dead.

matthew/8.ref: Daily Lessons delivered at Acca, p. 42
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: When souls that are not god die they go entirely away from this earth and so cannot influence anyone.  They are spiritually dead.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: If the souls of humanity are living and yet have not received a portion of the spirit they are as dead.

matthew/8.bible_quote: Follow Me, and let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Souls deprived of the divine spirit accounted as dead.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Body in need of physical and mental energy but his spirit requires fortification of the Holy Spirit.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Spiritually dead because they did not believe in Christ, only physically alive.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Without illumination from God the physically alive would be spiritually dead.  Were it not for the love of God the pathway of the Kingdom would be closed.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: The human soul is in need of divine vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit, without it man would be an animal.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: It is important to give perception and eternal life, that is, the spiritual and divine life; for physical life is not immortal and its existence is equivalent to non-existence.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Physical life in comparison with the life in the Kingdom of God is considered as death.

matthew/8.bible_quote: Follow Me; let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Those heedless of God are dead compared with the people of faith...

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38 (*)
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Whosoever hath not received a portion of the Spirit is as dead.  The Spirit is the life of humanity, the cause of eternal illumination. (* Address at rue St. Didier, Paris, 1/30/1913)

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, VII:16, p. 150 (*)
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: It is evident that life, in its true sense, is the life of the spirit and that life is the love of God, divine inspiration, spiritual joys and glad tidings of God. (* Early Tablet)

matthew/8.ref: Star of the West, p. XVIII:7, p. 224 (*)
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: The body of man is in need of physical and mental energy; the human spirit if not fortified by the Holy Spirit is dead and in need of resurrection by divine power. (*Tablet)

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: He Who would bury these dead was alive with a rational human soul, yet did Christ declare such dead;  this person was devoid of the Spirit of Faith...Without the Spirit of Faith a man is dead spiritually.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: You have found eternal life from among the dead; that is, you have heard the call of the divine Kingdom.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: souls who are not vivified by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead.

matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Those souls who are not vivified and attracted by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead.

matthew/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Those born of the Spirit have divine qualities; without these they are dead.  Those born of the Spirit have the power of growth.

matthew/8.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 18
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: Those who lived merely a physical life were to Christ as dead; real life is the life of the spirit, life eternal.

matthew/8.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 39
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: At death the dust of man is non-existent but in its own station it has existence, the human being may be spiritually dead while still alive.

matthew/8.ref: World Order through World Faith, p. 31
matthew/8.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
matthew/8.note: The soul that is portionless and bereft is dead...the human soul is in need of Divine vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit.

matthew/8.bible_quote: Why are y fearful, O ye of little faith?
matthew/8.note: The disciples of Christ were weak and apparently vanquished at first, but later they were victorious (Acts 2:42).

matthew/9.bible_quote: And behold certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
matthew/9.note: Observe how that persecution and scorn were changed into glory.  The Jews scoffed, saying, 'This man is naught but a sorcerer, blasphemeth and is possessed of a devil.'

matthew/9.bible_quote: For whether it is easier to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise and walk.  But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, then saith He to the sick of the p
matthew/9.note: This is the real sovereignty and such is the power of God's Chosen Ones.

matthew/9.bible_quote: For whether it is easier to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise and walk.  But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins...
matthew/9.note: His Holiness Jesus Christ was a real physician.  Prophets of God are real physicians, they prescribe for human conditions...whoever is healed by that remedy finds eternal health.

matthew/9.ref: Star of the West, VIII:18, p. 231
matthew/9.bible_quote: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins...Arise, take up thy bed and go unto thine house.
matthew/9.note: He did not use prayers or mental suggestion; He used spiritual authority in the command, which was the power of the Holy Spirit.  His Word was creative.

matthew/9.bible_quote: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins...Arise, take up thy bed and go unto thine house...I am not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance.
matthew/9.note: Through Jesus Christ the unchaste and the wayward were healed.

matthew/10.bible_quote: when He had called unto Him His twelve disciples He gave them power...These Jesus sent forth...
matthew/10.note: Always with their Master, they learned through His Teachings.  These carried the Gospel to different parts of the world.

matthew/10.bible_quote: when He had called unto Him His twelve disciples, He gave them power...
matthew/10.note: The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215
matthew/10.bible_quote: When Jesus had called unto Him His twelve disciples He gave them power...These twelve Jesus sent forth...
matthew/10.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps.

matthew/10.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/10.bible_quote: when He had called unto Him His twelve disciples, He gave them power...
matthew/10.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him, others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.

matthew/10.bible_quote: When He had called unto Him His twelve disciples, He gave them power...These twelve Jesus sent forth...
matthew/10.note: Let them that are men of action follow in their footsteps.

matthew/10.bible_quote: The twelve disciples...and Judas Iscariot who betrayed Him.
matthew/10.note: Jesus Christ really only had eleven disciples as Judas betrayed Him and was the cause of His crucifixion.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Now the names of the twelve Apostles are these...
matthew/10.note: Only eleven became Apostles of Jesus Christ, they illumined the world...similar conditions in Paris...little by little the Cause grew.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples.
matthew/10.note: Consider the days of Christ...how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts...The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.

matthew/10.bible_quote: The twelve disciples and Judas Iscariot who betrayed Him.
matthew/10.note: The superstition about the number thirteen had its origin in the fact that it was the thirteenth disciple who betrayed Jesus...Superstitions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Names of twelve disciples...
matthew/10.note: Christ in reality only educated eleven men, of whom one thrice denied Him.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls...The Cause of Christ permeated the whole world.  Then reflect what will take place in the future.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Names of the twelve apostles.
matthew/10.note: In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve apostles.

matthew/10.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/10.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples of Christ.
matthew/10.note: Only twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him...others but they were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.

matthew/10.bible_quote: These twelve Jesus sent forth and commanded them saying, Go...
matthew/10.note: The Bab admonished the Letters of the Living to ponder the words of Jesus addressed to His disciples as He sent them forth.

matthew/10.bible_quote: These twelve Jesus sent forth and commanded them saying, Go...
matthew/10.note: The charge which the Bab gave the Letters of the Living analogous to that given to Christ's disciples.  He recalled the words addressed by Jesus to His disciples and emphasized the superlative greatness of the New Day.

matthew/10.bible_quote: as ye go, preaching saying, the Kingdom of heaven is at hand.  Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, raise the dead, cast out devils...
matthew/10.note: Always with their Master, the Divine Master, they learned through His Teachings.

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, VII:14, p. 131
matthew/10.bible_quote: As ye go, preach saying, The Kingdom of heaven is at hand--I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves...be ye therefore wise.
matthew/10.note: Consider with what severance, attraction and enkindlement the Apostles arose in the promotion of the Word of God.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Freely ye have received, freely give.
matthew/10.note: You will be prospered in your teaching.  You have taken the gifts of God, so freely give.

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, VIII:5, p. 55 (*)
matthew/10.bible_quote: Freely ye have received, freely give.
matthew/10.note: Praise be to God that thou hast become specialized with divine favor and bounty. (* Tablet from Haifa 10/28/1916)

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, XX:10, p. 299
matthew/10.bible_quote: Heal the sick, freely ye have receied, freely give.
matthew/10.note: Those who say they are healing through the power of God should accept no pay, for they are dispensers of God's bounty, channels of His grace.

matthew/10.bible_quote: when ye depart out of that city, shake off the dust of your feet.
matthew/10.note: Instructions to traveling teachers.

matthew/10.bible_quote: And whosoever shall not receive you nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
matthew/10.note: The Bab repeated the words of Jesus in His charge to His disciples, to shake the dust from the feet in departing from a city which refused to receive him.

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, VII:13, p. 122 (*)
matthew/10.bible_quote: Whosever shall not receive you--when you depart, shake off the dust of your feet.
matthew/10.note: Man must live in the utmost sanctity and purity and he must ever think to assist others and not beg assistance from them. (* Talk in Haifa, 10/15/1914)

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, XI:2, p. 34 (*)
matthew/10.bible_quote: Whosever shall not receive you or hear your words shake the dust off your feet.
matthew/10.note: Teachers must travel to all parts of the world like 'Abdu'l-Baha.  He was sanctified and free from every attachment and in the utmost severance. (* Unveiling of the Divine Plan for the world)

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
matthew/10.bible_quote: And whosoever shall not receive you nor hear your words when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
matthew/10.note: The Covenant is like unto the ocean which preserves Baha'i unity.  These souls are like unto the foam upon it.  I saw that secretly and for varied motives unworthy actions were being manifested. (* Tablet to Howard and Mrs. MacNutt, Brooklyn, NY)

matthew/10.bible_quote: whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.
matthew/10.note: They should seek no reward...freely should they give...utter with eloquence...guide the people to the manifest light in great joy.

matthew/10.bible_quote: I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves...for they will deliver you up to the councils and they will scourge you...and ye shall be brought before kings...
matthew/10.note: The Apostles when they followed Jesus Christ, their ignorance gave place to understanding...they became spiritual...they became sons of God...they became saints.  Pray to God that you may be as angels and beacons of light.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Fear not them which kill the body but are not able to kill the soul.
matthew/10.note: Let your spirit be free so that you may soar towards sanctity.  Physical diseases cause man to suffer...they contribute towards the divine purpose...spiritual susceptibilities will be created in his heart.

matthew/10.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to send peace on earth; I came not to send peace but a sword...
matthew/10.note: He brought no reign of peace, for during His lifetime injustice and cruelty reached such a height that even He fell victom to it and died a shameful death on the cross.

matthew/10.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 185 (*)
matthew/10.bible_quote: Why callest thou Me good?  There is none good but One, that is God.
matthew/10.note: absolute perfection is unattainable by man.  However sucyh a man advances yet he is imperfect, because there is always a point ahead of him...he becomes disatisfied with his own condition and aspires to attain to that. (*DMAS 6/1/1914)

matthew/11.bible_quote: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias which is for to come. (Malachi 4:5)
matthew/11.note: John said when asked, What then, Art thou Elias?  and he saith, I am not.  Explanation of contradiction, reality of return.

matthew/11.bible_quote: And if ye will receive it, This is Elias which was for to come.
matthew/11.note: It is essential to have Divine perception in order to see the truth.  Holy Books must never be taken literally.

matthew/11.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 50
matthew/11.bible_quote: All things are delivered unto Me of My Father; and no man knoweth the Son but the Father; neither knoweth any man the Father, save the Son.
matthew/11.note: Baha'u'llah declared that the divines did not know the Station of Jesus Christ.

matthew/12.bible_quote: At that time Jesus went on the Sabbath Day through the corn and His disciples began to pluck ears of corn and to eat.  But when the Pharisees saw it they said, Behold Thy disciples do that which is no
matthew/12.note: The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.  They were deprived of the Beauty of Christ.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Jesus went on the Sabbath Day through the corn; and His disciples were ahungered and began to pluck corn and to eat.  But when the Pharisees saw it they said unto Him, Behold Thy disciples do that whi
matthew/12.note: The Jews regarded Jesus as the breaker of the Sabbath Day--they did not understand the significance, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.  They sentenced Him to death.

matthew/12.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 239 (Tablet to*)
matthew/12.bible_quote: Jesus went on the Sabbath Day through the corn; and His disciples were ahungered and began to pluck corn and to eat.  But when the Pharisees saw it they said unto Him, Behold thy disciples do that whi
matthew/12.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath.  Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was to manifest error.  Violators flatter, seduce and show insincere love. (* MacNutt, Brooklyn)

matthew/12.bible_quote: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto Him, Behold thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Because they were blindly following imitations the Jews rejected Jesus, they refused to investigate the truth.

matthew/12.bible_quote: when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto Him, Behold Thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Manifestations changed some of the laws of the Jews--which they called the law of Moses.  Altered according to the exigencies of the times.

matthew/12.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241(Tablet to MacNutt)
matthew/12.bible_quote: When the Pharisees saw it, they said unto Him, Behold, Thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath.  Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was to manifest error.  Violators flatter, seduce and show insincere love.

matthew/12.bible_quote: But if ye had known what this meaneth...ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
matthew/12.note: Because Jesus dared to rebuke the Pharisees the Jews rejected Him.  The Jews knew by heart the letter, of the Spirit they did not understand, they did not understand the Scriptures.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the laws of the Sabbath, the people rose in protest against Him.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Jews were veiled from the truth by literal interpretation; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Manifestations change the material laws in conformance with needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Then the Pharisees said unto Him, Thy disciples do that which is not lawful upon the Sabbath Day...For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: He broke the Sabbath Day.  He reformed the law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

matthew/12.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 96; Paris Talks, p. 105
matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Thou Jesus abrogated the Law of Moses, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.  The Pharisees said of Christ that He had broken the Sabbath.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Jesus Christ gave the spiritual truth regarding the Sabbath, the Jews were veiled from the truth by narrow interpretation.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practice led them to reject Jesus Christ.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Because of the needs and conditions of the time laws were changed.  The Jews were unwilling to change from old forms and teachings.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: Jesus Christ changed the laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the time.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: In the days of Christ conditions had changed, Mosaic Law not adapted to the needs, therefore Christ abrogated the law of the Sabbath.

matthew/12.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 31
matthew/12.bible_quote: For the Son of man is Lord even of the Sabbath Day.
matthew/12.note: The Sabbath of the Jews was changed by Christ.  The outward rules may be changed but the reality is unchanging.

matthew/12.ref: Utterances in letter from Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
matthew/12.bible_quote: there was a man which had his hand withered...then saith [Jesus] to the man, Stretch forth thine hand, and he stretched it forth, and it was restored whole [on the Sabbath].  Then the Pharisees went o
matthew/12.note: The Promised One must promote the laws of the Bible and this man broke the Sabbath, said the Jewish clergy.  The clergy created doubts in the minds of the people. (*Brittingham, 1/1914)

matthew/12.bible_quote: Then the Pharisees went out and held a council against Him, how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: God however aided Him and protected Him.  Thy Lord preserveth whom He willeth.

matthew/12.bible_quote: The Pharisees held council how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: Adversaries of Jesus were rabbis...whereas those of the Bab represented civil and ecclesiastical powers who conspired against upholders and Revelation.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Pharisees went out and held council how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: As in the days of Jesus Christ, Baha'u'llah was exposed to the perversity of the divines.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Then the Pharisees went and they held council against him, how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: The Jews rejected Jesus.  They were blind to the reality of Christ.  Literal interpretation of prophecies veiled them.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Then the Pharisees went out and held council against Him, how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: The enemies of His Holiness Christ were the Jews, the Pharisees but the enemies of Baha'u'llah were kings representing some 15,000,000 of people.  At last His banner was upraised...

matthew/12.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
matthew/12.bible_quote: Then the Pharisees went out and held a council against Him, how they might destroy Him.
matthew/12.note: In the time of Jesus the Jewish clergy created so many doubts that there were very few who believed, in reality there were only twelve and one of those denied Him.  Why?  Because tests were very great at that time.

matthew/12.bible_quote: when the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils but by Beelzebub.
matthew/12.note: Moses announced the coming of Jesus Christ.  Israelites were waiting impatiently for Him but when He came they called Him Beelzebub.

matthew/12.bible_quote: and when the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth cast out devils but by Beelzebub.
matthew/12.note: In His day Christ was called Satan, Beelzebub, but hear the bells ringing for Him!  Although men may rise against the Kingdom, the dominion and sovereignty of God will be set up.

matthew/12.bible_quote: and when the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth cast out devils but by Beelzebub.
matthew/12.note: In His day Christ was called Satan, Beelzebub but hear the bells now ringing for Him!  Although men rise against the Kingdom, the dominion and sovereignty of God will be set up.

matthew/12.bible_quote: when the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils but by Beelzebub the prince of devils.
matthew/12.note: The Jews hated Jesus.  They rejected and tortured Him, but Christianity advanced daily.

matthew/12.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 241 (*)
matthew/12.bible_quote: When the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils but by Beelzebub the prince of the devils.
matthew/12.note: Cause of Christ had no importance; the importance of the Covenant is not known yet. (* Tablet for Roy Wilhelm)

matthew/12.bible_quote: But when the Pharisees heard it they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by Beelzebub the prince of devils.
matthew/12.note: Pharisees called Jesus His Holiness the divine Christ, Beelzebub.  The deriding enemy addeth joy to the heart...becometh the means of spirituality.

matthew/12.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
matthew/12.bible_quote: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils but by Beelzebub the prince of devils.
matthew/12.note: When Jesus came the Jews called Him Beelzebub.  I have always said that the believers will be blamed for this Cause.  The Jews proved from the Bible that Jesus was a false prophet--It was always so. (* Brittingham, 1/1914)

matthew/12.bible_quote: All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men but the blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall not be forgiven him; but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be forgiven h
matthew/12.note: Explanation of why blasphemy against the Holy Ghost is unforgivable.

matthew/12.bible_quote: for the tree is known by its fruits.
matthew/12.note: Every soul is known by his conduct...Strive to be beatific in character.

matthew/12.bible_quote: O generation of vipers!
matthew/12.note: Behold how this Wronged One is now surrounded by a generation of vipers.  He is summoning all men to Him warning the heedless. (also see Mt 3:7; 23:33)

matthew/12.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 57
matthew/12.bible_quote: For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale's belly, so shall the Son of man be in the heart of the earth.
matthew/12.note: This refers to the episode in Jonah I.  This meant three Dispensations: Christ, Muhammad and Baha'u'llah.  Christ meant that He would be resurrected or reappear during the third cycle.

matthew/12.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 47
matthew/12.bible_quote: [Jesus] stretched forth His hand towards His disciples, and said, Behold, My mother and My brethren, for whoever shall do the will of the Father, the same is My brother, and sister and mother.
matthew/12.note: Spiritual relationship is the true familyhood of God's children.

matthew/12.bible_quote: For whosoever shall do the will of My Father...the same is My brother, and sister and mother.
matthew/12.note: Christ said His brothers were those who believed in God.

matthew/12.bible_quote: Whosoever shall do the will of My Father which is in heaven, the same is My brother, and sister and mother.
matthew/12.note: You are My spiritual children; spiritual children are dearer than physical children--you are the most beloved.

matthew/12.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 24
matthew/12.bible_quote: Whosoever shall do the will of My Father which is in heaven, the same is My brother, and sister and mother.
matthew/12.note: His Holiness Christ answered that His brothers were those who believed in God, and refused to associate with His own brothers.  It is impossible for a man to associate with a leper and not be infected.

matthew/13.bible_quote: But he that receiveth seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word and understandeth it--which also beareth fruit and bringeth forth some a hundred, some sixty and some thirty.
matthew/13.note: Attainment is dependent upon capacity.  Unless capacity is developed the summons of the Kingdom cannot reach his ears.

matthew/13.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
matthew/13.bible_quote: Parable of the Sower, with explanation.
matthew/13.note: The seed is contained in the trunk.  Some behold in a seed only a hard, black substance, while others see in it the life principle.

matthew/13.ref: Star of the West, XII:11, p. 182 (*)
matthew/13.bible_quote: Some fell upon stony places...had no deepness of earth...
matthew/13.note: Some have no capacity; others hear and understand but forget; others have great capacity and live accordingly. (* a week in 'Abdu'l-Baha's home in 'Akka)

matthew/13.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 180
matthew/13.bible_quote: And the disciples came and said, Why speakest Thou in parables?  He said, Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of Heaven but to them it is not given.

matthew/13.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 37
matthew/13.bible_quote: Therefore speak I to them in parables because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
matthew/13.note: The Call of the Kingdom regenerates some; but others like those Christ mentioned do not comprehend.

matthew/13.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 18
matthew/13.bible_quote: Therefore speak I to them in parables because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.
matthew/13.note: Because of their spiritual deadness they do not understand.  Miracles were spiritual not physical.
The good seed needs rain, ploughing, sunshine...the heart is made ready by all experience for the seed of life.

matthew/13.bible_quote: Parable of the Sower and the tares.
matthew/13.note: We must strive for capacity and seek readiness.  We must make the soil of our hearts receptive.

matthew/13.bible_quote: Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the Kingdom of their Father.
matthew/13.note: It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.

matthew/14.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 122 (*)
matthew/14.bible_quote: And the king sent for and beheaded John in the prison.
matthew/14.note: Recalled some of Jewish history and presented proofs for the validity of Christ.  You beheaded John the son of Zachariah. (*Talk with rabbi)

matthew/14.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106 (*)
matthew/14.bible_quote: And He commanded the multitude to sit down...and took the five loaves and two fishes and looking up to heaven, He blessed and brake.  They did all eat...they took up the fragments that remained twelve
matthew/14.note: Miracle and symbolism of the loaves and fishes explained. (* Talk with Mrs. von Lilienthal and Mrs. Beede)

matthew/14.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 34
matthew/14.bible_quote: And He commanded the multitude to sit down...and took the five loaves and two fishes, and looking up to heaven, blessed and brake.  They did all eat...they took up the fragments that remained twelve b
matthew/14.note: Interpretation of five loaves as words of Christ which satisfied the multitude, two fishes as the spiritual meaning, basketsful as super-abundance of teachings for others.

matthew/14.ref: Star of the West, VIII:9, p. 114 (*)
matthew/14.bible_quote: In the fourth watch of the night Jesus went to them walking on the Sea.
matthew/14.note: Symbolism of this miracle: the sea represents the ocean of creation, the two shores are the earthly truth and spiritual truth, boat stands for arguments and reasons... (* Talk to Ethel Rosenberg, 1901)

matthew/15.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
matthew/15.bible_quote: But in vain they do worship Me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men...but their heart is far from Me.
matthew/15.note: when the Jews became fettered by empty forms and imitations, the wrath of God became manifest.

matthew/15.bible_quote: I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
matthew/15.note: Jesus shepherded the flock of Israel but brought together people of many nations.  The establishing of religion is for peace.  Divine teachings are to create a bond of unity and fellowship.

matthew/15.bible_quote: multitudes came unto [Jesus] with them lame, blind, dumb, maimed and many others...and He healed them.
matthew/15.note: He shed the splendor of His glory upon all created things.  Lepers received healing from ignorance and perversity.  Eyes of the blind opened and the soul of the sinner was sanctified.

matthew/15.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106
matthew/15.bible_quote: And He took the seven loaves and the fishes and gave thanks...they did all eat and were filled; and they took up that was left seven basketsful.

matthew/16.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 28
matthew/16.bible_quote: And Peter said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God...then He charged His disciples that they should tell no man that He was Jesus the Christ.
matthew/16.note: This was a few days before His crucifixion.  Baha'u'llah sent Tablets to crowned heads telling them openly, I am the Father.  Then they exiled and banished Him to Acca.

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, VII:14, p. 131
matthew/16.bible_quote: Peter answered, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God...Blessed art thou for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee but My Father which is in heaven..Then Peter began to swear saying,
matthew/16.note: This movement is stupendous and of paramount importance.

matthew/16.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 38
matthew/16.bible_quote: Jesus said, Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: According to the history of the Church, Peter was rendered fit to become Jesus' successor and founder of the Church.

matthew/16.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 62
matthew/16.bible_quote: And I say unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Peter had said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.  Upon this faith is the cause of the unity of nations.

matthew/16.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 157
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock will I build My church.
matthew/16.note: The church is the law of God.

matthew/16.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 380
matthew/16.bible_quote: thou art Peter and upon this rock will I build My church.
matthew/16.note: meaning that the Faith of Peter was the true Faith.  Christ did not say that all should turn to Peter.

matthew/16.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 154
matthew/16.bible_quote: And I say also unto thee, that thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Peter's belief will be the foundation of the religion of God...the foundation of the church of God, the law of God.

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 237
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Peter means rock.  This was a sanction of Peter's faith--not indicative of his being an expounder of the Book.

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 235 (*)
matthew/16.bible_quote: thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: This is only a tradition transmitted by the Apostles--this is not written down by the pen of Christ but has exacted obediance from Christians.  Baha'u'llah wrote with His own pen in the Book of the Covenant. (* Tablet to Martha Root)

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 269 (Tablet to*)
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: This declaration preserved for a thousand years Christian unity.  If this word of Christ has preserved the unity of Christians, consider what will be done by the Kitab-i-Ahd which has been revealed by Baha'u'llah's Pen. (*Mary Lesch, Chicago)

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Today the Lord of Hosts is the defender of the Covenant, the forces of the Kingdom protect it. (*Tablet to Howard and Mrs. MacNutt, Brooklyn)

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, XI:18, p. 307
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: This Word could not be resisted and it has been made manifest...Baha'u'llah through His sacred Pen established this Covenant in the utmost power, perfection and clearness.  Consider what power it yields... (*Tablet to Marie Watson, 1919)

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, XII:14, p. 227
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: This was a sanction of Peter's faith; it was not indicative of Peter's being the expounder of the Book.

matthew/16.ref: TAB:I, p. 88
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: This speech created in the heart of Judas envy, therefore denied after confessing, hated after loving.  Such has taken place and will occur in this Great Cause.

matthew/16.ref: TAB:II, p. 220
matthew/16.bible_quote: thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church...and I will unto thee the keys of the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/16.note: Judas turned away from Jesus Christ because of jealousy of Peter.  Consider the predecessors as a warning and purify thy heart.

matthew/16.ref: TAB:III, p. 528
matthew/16.bible_quote: thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: The great one among them was Peter but he denied Jesus Christ three times.  Jews thought they had extinguished the fire but it kindled the hearts of the righteous.

matthew/16.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Peter was the head appointed by Christ, yet he denied the Christ three times.  What happened afterwards?  The truth in Christ spread all over the world.

matthew/16.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: There is nothing so effective as the Covenant of God to effect unity.  All disciples followed Peter so there was no dissension.  Baha'u'llah wrote a Covenant.

matthew/16.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 62
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Jesus spoke to Peter.  Baha'u'llah wrote appointing the Center of the Covenant in the Kitab-i-Aqdas.  He left no possibility for anyone to misunderstand.

matthew/16.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 21
matthew/16.bible_quote: Thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build My church.
matthew/16.note: Difference between God-appointed Baha'i Faith and man-appointed ecclesiastical organizations.

matthew/16.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
matthew/16.bible_quote: If any man will come after Me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross and follow Me.
matthew/16.note: No one is able to follow Christ entirely except he give up all material things such as honor, wealth, etc.

matthew/16.ref: Baha'i World Faith, p. 53
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: The land of Syria hath been honored by the footsteps of its Lord.  Scriptures have been fulfilled.

matthew/16.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: We have said, He Who is the Father is come.  Blessed is he that draweth nigh unto Him, and woe betide them that are far away.

matthew/16.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Every Prophet hath announced the coming of this Day...allusions made to it in all the sacred Scriptures.

matthew/16.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 18
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of the Father.
matthew/16.note: All are invited to partake of the fruit of the Kingdom of the Father...discloses the mysteries of the Teachings of His Father, Baha'u'llah.

matthew/16.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 19
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Jesus Christ did not claim to have come in that station but that He would return in the Glory of the Father.  This glory was to be the unity of mankind.

matthew/16.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 32
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah is the trunk, the branches, the root--all except the fruit, which is 'Abdu'l-Baha.  The Teachings of Baha'u'llah have been spread throughout the world.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 95
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: To Him Jesus Christ referred as the Son of man who shall come in the glory of His Father...in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 110-111
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah arrived in Baghdad.  In it the Tabernacle of the Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long-awaited Kingdom of the Father were unassailably established.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 151
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Jesus Christ returned in the glory of the Father was about to ascend His throne.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 207
matthew/16.bible_quote: the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His mission.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 207
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah announced the advent of One with whose name the Evangel was adorned.

matthew/16.ref: God Passes By, p. 210
matthew/16.bible_quote: the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His mission.
