matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Baha'u'llah asserted that the clergy interposed between the flock and the returned Christ.

matthew/16.ref: Star of the West, IV:15, p. 258
matthew/16.bible_quote: the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: The Father, whose coming Christ foretold has come among us and opened the Greatest Door of Bounteous Gifts and Divine favors.

matthew/16.ref: TAB:I, p. 138
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Return of Christ signifies the One to come after Him, He shall come with the Kingdom of God.

matthew/16.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: Holy Scriptures announce the coming of two Manifestations.

matthew/16.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 139
matthew/16.bible_quote: For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father.
matthew/16.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha is not a Manifestation of God; He is not to be acclaimed the Return of Christ, the Son Who will come in the glory of the Father.  He reflects even as a clear mirror the rays of Baha'u'llah's glory.

matthew/17.bible_quote: Jesus was transfigured before them and His face did shine as the sun...
matthew/17.note: The faithful are ever sustained by the presence of the Supreme Concourse.  It is the one Holy Spirit that strengthens all men.

matthew/17.bible_quote: Jesus taketh Peter, James and John...and bringeth them to an high mountain apart and was transfigured before them; and His face did shine as the sun and His raiment was white as the light...And there
matthew/17.note: It was a spiritual condition expressed as a physical meeting...

matthew/17.bible_quote: While He yet spake behold a bright cloud overshadowed them; and behold a voice out of the cloud which said, This is My beloved Son in whom I am well pleased; hear ye Him.
matthew/17.note: God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit Who was known amongst the people as fatherless the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

matthew/17.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
matthew/17.bible_quote: While He yet spake, behold a bright cloud overshadowed them; and behold a voice out of the cloud which said, This is My beloved son in whom I am well pleased; hear ye Him.
matthew/17.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.

matthew/17.bible_quote: While He yet spake...behold a voice out of the cloud which said, This is My beloved son in whom I am well pleased; hear ye Him.
matthew/17.note: The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ...meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.

matthew/17.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
matthew/17.bible_quote: While He yet spake...a voice out of the cloud said, This is My beloved Son in whom I am well pleased; hear ye Him.
matthew/17.note: The proof of the Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself.  Jesus Christ was born in a manger (Luke 2:7) but He changed the whole world by His power and divinity.

matthew/17.bible_quote: And His disciples asked Him, Why then say the scribes that Elias must come first?  Jesus answered: Elias shall first come, But I say unto you Elias is come already...Then the disciples understood He s
matthew/17.note: This is as regards His virtues and not as regards identity.  This is our view of incarnation.

matthew/17.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
matthew/17.bible_quote: Elias truly shall first come, and restore all things.
matthew/17.note: These tidings are the same in all the Holy Books expressed in different ways...two successive Manifestations.

matthew/17.bible_quote: Elias has come already and they knew him not...
matthew/17.note: Return of the Prophets mean return of their perfections only.

matthew/17.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, n.p.
matthew/17.bible_quote: And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him.
matthew/17.note: An evil soul is like a stony field in which good seeds have been sown but no growth has followed.  You are in heaven when close to 'Abdu'l-Baha.

matthew/17.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 20
matthew/17.bible_quote: go to the sea and cast an hook and take up the fish that first cometh; and when thou hast opened his mouth thou should find a piece of money...give unto them for Me and thee.
matthew/17.note: Hook means teaching; fish means the man who received it; money is the teaching in his mouth.

matthew/18.bible_quote: Except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
matthew/18.note: The true Baha'i loves the children.  A simple pure heart is near to God.

matthew/18.bible_quote: Except ye be converted and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
matthew/18.note: Men must become pure in heart in order to know God.

matthew/18.bible_quote: Except ye be converted and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
matthew/18.note: Although thou art small thou wilt become great in the kingdom--be obediant...add to thy faith daily.

matthew/18.bible_quote: But whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in Me...woe to that man by whom the offence cometh...
matthew/18.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha warns us to avoid wicked persons.

matthew/18.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21
matthew/18.bible_quote: woe to that man by whom the offence cometh.
matthew/18.note: In the Holy Writings of His Holiness Baha'u'llah, in a thousand places at least, the violators of the Covenant are execrated and condemned.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Why did Moses command to give a writing of divorcement...Moses, because of the hardness of your heart suffered you to put away your wives.
matthew/19.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce the people rose in protest against Him.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Moses, because of the hardness of your heart, suffered you to put away your wives but I say unto you...
matthew/19.note: Material laws were changed by the Manifestations according to the needs and conditions of the times...In the time of Moses divorce was conformable to the needs and conditions but in the time of Jesus they were not suitable so Jesus abrogated the law.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives, but I say unto you...
matthew/19.note: Because of the needs and conditions of the times, laws were changed.  Because of their adherence to imitation of ancient forms, the Jews rejected Jesus.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives, but I say unto you...
matthew/19.note: outer laws are subject to change according to the exigencies of time, place and conditions.  Manifestations make such changes.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Why did Moses command to give a writing of divorcement...because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives, but I say unto you...
matthew/19.note: Jesus Christ abrogated this law because the exigency which existed in the time of Moses did not exist.

matthew/19.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 31
matthew/19.bible_quote: Moses...because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives; but from the beginning it was not...
matthew/19.note: Explanation and interpretation...Laws represent the element which alters, for instance divorce.  Ture religion is one.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto me, for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: The true Baha'i loves the children. A simple heart is near to God.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me for of such is the kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: Blessed are the children, of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such are the Kingdom of heaven...hearts are pure, we must become like them.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me, for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: As long as you are the children of the Kingdom no matter what language you use it is sweet.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me, for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: You are the children of whom Christ has said, Of such is the Kingdom of God.  You are the very lamps of mankind...you are near the Source.

matthew/19.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 155 (*)
matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto me for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: Because children are tender plants, their hearts are clear and transparent. (*Addresses in Stuttgart and Esslingen, Germany)

matthew/19.bible_quote: Suffer the little children...to come unto Me; for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: Daily grow in faith and assurance and develop Baha'i attributes, such as obediance and kindness.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Good Master, what good thing shall I do that I may have eternal life?...keep the commandments...All these things have I kept from my youth up...go and sell what thou hast, and give to the poor...
matthew/19.note: Material favors sometimes deprive us of spiritual favors...rich who are attracted through their hearts are like brilliant torches.

matthew/19.bible_quote: keep the commandments...All these things have I kept from my youth up...go and sell what thou hast and give to the poor...
matthew/19.note: Each soul who wishes to take a step in the path of God, must carry his own cross, devote his life to God's service and welcome sacrifice.

matthew/19.bible_quote: If thou wilt enter into life keep the commandments...
matthew/19.note: Jesus renewed the commandments which Moses had received from Jehovah.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Why callest thou Me good?  There is none good but One, that is God...
matthew/19.note: How often the Prophets of God and His Universal Manifestations in their prayers confess their sins...This is to teach men to confess their sins and faults.

matthew/19.bible_quote: Verily I say unto you that a rich man shall hardly enter into the Kingdom of heaven.
matthew/19.note: The true seeker will gladly renounce worldly desires and possessions if they interfere with spiritual growth.

matthew/19.bible_quote: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
matthew/19.note: Jesus said, Blessed are the poor. I love you all; you are children of the Kingdom and are accepted of God.

matthew/19.bible_quote: it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
matthew/19.note: one's heart must not be attached to the things of this world.

matthew/19.bible_quote: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
matthew/19.note: Earth's happiness does not depend upon wealth.  The rich depend on riches whereas the poor depend on God.

matthew/19.bible_quote: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
matthew/19.note: Christ said so; Baha'u'llah said so...We must be free from the insatiable desire for riches.  Poor are blessed.

matthew/19.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
matthew/19.bible_quote: I say unto you, That ye which have followed Me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of His glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
matthew/19.note: Christ promised the disciples this...explanation.

matthew/19.bible_quote: That ye who have followed Me in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of His glory, ye shall sit upon twelve thrones...
matthew/19.note: To the Day of Baha'u'llah Jesus Christ referred as the regeneration.

matthew/19.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 104 (*)
matthew/19.bible_quote: But many that are first shall be last; and the last shall be first.
matthew/19.note: Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God, of humanity.  The humbler man is, in the path of God, the more exalted is he. (*Talk to students on Mt. Carmel)

matthew/20.bible_quote: Parable of the laborers and the Lord in the Vineyard.
matthew/20.note: Lord of the Vineyard is Baha'u'llah.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100
matthew/20.bible_quote: So the last shall be first and the first last; for many be called but few chosen.
matthew/20.note: Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God, of the world of humanity.  The honor of man depends upon this.

matthew/20.bible_quote: for many be called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: Two kinds of Faith--objective and subjective--obediance of the outer man and obediance to the will of God.  There is very little of the faith that consists of the knowledge of God and divine Words.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: Violators are execrated and condemned.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: The chosen have heard and understood the call from the divine assembly.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called, but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: The doors of His generosity are wide open...but we must be attentive.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: It is from the bounty of God that man is selected for the highest degree.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 45
matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: Everyone must be tested as the time of the chosen ones to prove their worth is very short.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, VIII:16, p. 217
matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: O ye whom God hath chosen...ye must be sincere and faithful, ye must follow the ordinances which refer to the Covenant of God.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
matthew/20.bible_quote: for many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: in the holy Writings of His Holiness, Baha'u'llah in a thousand places at least, the violators are execrated and condemned.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, XIII:6, p. 143
matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: The transformation depends on the mercy of God.  In the realm of men transformation is possible only through another person.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: the friends of God have been endowed with ability and capacity on which account they become the chosen and not the called.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: That is, many have been invited to the faith, but few chosen for guidance.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: [God] appropriateth by His mercy those whom He liketh and chooseth for His love those of whom He approveth.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: Ye partook of the eternal table...This favor is one of the special gifts of the Merciful...Many are invited but few are signalized with grace of guidance.

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: the chosen: upon whom He caused evidensed Light to descend...guided into the right Path, and to whom He gave the Glad-tidings of the Great Success!...Ye must be sincere and faithful, ye must follow the ordinances which refer to the Covenant...

matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: This is a station which God giveth to whomsoever He pleaseth.

matthew/20.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 60
matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: We need to be strongly tested in order to proove our faith to ourselves and the world.  Be firm.

matthew/20.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 80
matthew/20.bible_quote: For many are called but few are chosen.
matthew/20.note: God's Mercy has descended upon us although we are not worthy...We are the blessed ones who know.

matthew/20.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 21,26
matthew/20.bible_quote: the Son of man came to give His life a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: Christ wished to become a sacrifice.  Christ came of His own will to be a sacrifice that people might become educated and progress.  He saved all who believed on Him.

matthew/20.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
matthew/20.bible_quote: the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed then obeyed, then found deliverance from sin.

matthew/20.bible_quote: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life as a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: When Jesus Christ appeared...He invited men to Eternal Life; the Light of Guidance was shed forth by that radiant Star and He at length gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.

matthew/20.bible_quote: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life as a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from the imperfections of the physical nature.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 4
matthew/20.bible_quote: the Son of man came...to give His life a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: Consider what His Holiness Christ endured...finally sacrificing His life so that a tent of love and concord might be raised over humanity.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156
matthew/20.bible_quote: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life as a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from the imperfections of the physical nature.

matthew/20.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (*)
matthew/20.bible_quote: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: Let us have the life of the spirit...of sympathy, practical cooperation, celestial brotherhood... (*Talk 5/1914)

matthew/20.bible_quote: the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many.
matthew/20.note: It is for this purpose that the Manifestations unveil themselves, bear every ordeal to make ready souls dawnings of light...This is the real atonement Christ made.  He sacrificed Himself for the world.

matthew/21.bible_quote: the disciples went...and brought the ass...and they set Him thereon.  And a great multitude spread their garments in the way.
matthew/21.note: How many awaited the coming of the Messiah and yet when He appeared they followed the superstitions of the Pharisees.

matthew/21.bible_quote: the disciples...brought the ass...and set Him thereon...
matthew/21.note: But those who had a clear insight regarded even the ass on which He was mounted as a splendid throne...and the thorns which were upon His head as a brilliant diadem.

matthew/21.bible_quote: And the multitude said, This is Jesus the Prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
matthew/21.note: Men said, He is of Nazareth...born of Mary.  What can He mean...that He came from God?  The capacities of His human body were limited but the strength of His spirit was immeasurable.

matthew/21.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 18
matthew/21.bible_quote: Jesus went into the Temple of God and cast out them that sold and bought in the Temple and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers...And said, My house shall be called the House of Prayer...ye have
matthew/21.note: This is a most wonderful event, that His Holiness without opposition from the Jews was permitted to enter their most sacred place and proclaim the Word of God.

matthew/21.ref: Flowers culled from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 28
matthew/21.bible_quote: the chief priests and the elders...said, By what authority doeth thou these things?...Jesus answered...the baptism of John, whence was it from heaven or of men?...they answered, We cannot tell...Neith
matthew/21.note: The same power has appeared a hundred fold more powerful in Baha'u'llah in the station of the Father.

matthew/21.bible_quote: But when the husbandman saw the son...let us kill him and seize his inheritance.  And they caught him...and slew him.
matthew/21.note: This is an illustration of sedition and Covenant-breaking.  Covenant-breakers are execrated and condemned.

matthew/21.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21
matthew/21.note: In the holy Writings Baha'u'llah, in a thousand places at least, the violators of the Covenant are execrated and condemned.

matthew/21.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
matthew/21.note: Prophecy of the coming of One in the Station of the Father fulfilled in Baha'u'llah.
Prophecy of the coming of the Lord in the station of the Father fulfilled by Baha'u'llah.

matthew/21.bible_quote: Parable of the Vineyard and the Lord.
matthew/21.note: Jesus Christ referred to Baha'u'llah as the Lord of the Vineyard and as the Son of man who shall come in the glory of His Father.

matthew/21.bible_quote: Parable of the Vineyard and the Lord.
matthew/21.note: Baha'u'llah has come in the Station of the Father.

matthew/21.bible_quote: Parable of the husbandmen, Vineyard and Lord.

matthew/21.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 205
matthew/21.bible_quote: When the Lord therefore of the Vineyard cometh, what will He do unto those husbandmen?
matthew/21.note: Baha'u'llah comes in the station of the Judge; His warning to evil doers.

matthew/21.bible_quote: The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner.
matthew/21.note: Christ said that the stone which the builders rejected became the headstone of the corner.  This means...spiritually great men and women..have been rejected, now they will become the chief stones.  The wise man works for the good results of the future.

matthew/22.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 9
matthew/22.bible_quote: Parable of the King's Marriage Feast.
matthew/22.note: The invited guests, leaders of the day made excuses to stay away.  By this the religious leaders are indicated...the simple ones came instead.

matthew/22.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 41
matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle Him in His talk.
matthew/22.note: Analogy between the doctors disputing and rejecting Jesus, with the divines rejecting Baha'u'llah.

matthew/22.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 48
matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees and took counsel how they might entanble Him in His talk.
matthew/22.note: When I came to them in My glory, they turned aside...The Jewish doctors disputed with Jesus Christ and perpetrated what hath made the Holy Spirit lament.

matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might entangle Him in His talk...But Jesus perceived their wickedness and said, Why tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?
matthew/22.note: Certain persons, like the Pharisees...will exert themselves to...deprive souls of the Glad-tidings of the Holy Spirit.

matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees and took counsel how they might entangle Him in His talk.
matthew/22.note: The Jews expected the Messiah but when He came they rejected Him--they were bereft of sight.

matthew/22.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 30
matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees and took counsel how they might entangle Him in His talk.
matthew/22.note: Warning to Pope Pius IX not to dispute with Baha'u'llah as Jewish doctors did with Jesus.

matthew/22.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p . 89
matthew/22.bible_quote: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar?...Render unto Caesar wthe things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.
matthew/22.note: Baha'u'llah explains that obey God by obeying rulers.

matthew/22.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 73
matthew/22.bible_quote: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar?...Render unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's, and unto God the things that are God's.

matthew/22.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 81
matthew/22.bible_quote: Render unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's and unto God the things that are God's.
matthew/22.note: Obey rulers is to obey God.

matthew/22.bible_quote: Render unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's and unto God the things that are God's.
matthew/22.note: Live so that the world may see your loyalty to God and your government.  This is Justice.

matthew/22.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 7
matthew/22.bible_quote: Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's and unto God the things that are God's.
matthew/22.note: Religious interests should not be brought into politics.  Reliigions should treat of morals; politics, of material circumstances.

matthew/22.bible_quote: the Pharisees...asked Him a question, tempting Him...No man was able to answer Him a word.
matthew/22.note: Baha'u'llah warns the Pope not to dispute with Him as the Pharisees did with Jesus.

matthew/22.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
matthew/22.bible_quote: Then went the Pharisees and took counsel how they might entangle Him in His talk.
matthew/22.note: Baha'u'llah warned the Pope, Guard thyself lest darkness spread its veils over thee.  Consider those who oppressed Jesus and disputed with Him.

matthew/22.bible_quote: Jesus said unto them, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy mind.  This is the first and great commandment.
matthew/22.note: This is the original and principal teaching that Jesus Christ spread in the world.

matthew/22.bible_quote: Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.
matthew/22.note: Consider the foundation of the brotherhood laid by His Holiness Christ.  Galen wrote, They live together in utmost love and fellowship.

matthew/22.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
matthew/22.bible_quote: Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.
matthew/22.note: All the Prophets were sent, all the holy books were revealed, so that the law of love might be promoted.

matthew/23.bible_quote: Then spake Jesus to the multitudes, and to the disciples...the Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat: all therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do...
matthew/23.note: I hope that it is proven to you that Moses had no better friend than His Holiness the Christ.

matthew/23.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 4
matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: Jesus Christ proclaimed and ratified the prophethood of Moses.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works, for they say and do not...
matthew/23.note: By promoting the laws of the Bible, the law of Moses was meant.  Christ promulgated Sinaitic law and gave it spiritual expression.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works...
matthew/23.note: Manifestations testify to the Oneness therefore Jesus upheld the teachings and commandments of Moses.  Jews had become blind to the truth through bigoted imitation.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works...
matthew/23.note: Christ ratified and proclaimed the Law of Moses.  All prophets have revoiced that same foundation of reality.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do...
matthew/23.note: Christ declared Moses to have been the prophet of God.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: We have stated the valid proofs of the Prophethood of Moses...Jesus Christ was instrumental in publishing the name and greatness of the Israelitish Prophets, and proved to the world that the Israelites constituted the people of God.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: Christ caused the name and prestige of Moses to become widespread.  Through Christ Moses was proclaimed a prophet of God.

matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  And therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: The greatest friend of Moses was Jesus but the Jews felt He was taking away some of Moses' glory.  They were veiled from the truth and kept away from His bounties.

matthew/23.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 3-4
matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat. All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: Jesus Christ revoiced and confirmed essential teachings of Moses.  Christ ratified and proclaimed the foundation of the law of Moses.

matthew/23.ref: Star of the West, III:17, p. 8 (*)
matthew/23.bible_quote: The Scribes and Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.  All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do.
matthew/23.note: Christ spread the name of Moses throughout the world, and promulgated the Old Testament throughout the nations. (*Address in London)

matthew/23.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100
matthew/23.bible_quote: he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.  And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
matthew/23.note: The humbler a man is in the path of God, the more exalted is he; the greater his meekness and submissiveness the more beloved is he...

matthew/23.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 104
matthew/23.bible_quote: But he that is greatest among you shall be your servant.  And whoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
matthew/23.note: The humbler man is, in the path of God, the more exalted is he.  Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God.  In the path of God humility is honor.  Whoever is the real servant of the believers of God is their helper.

matthew/23.bible_quote: And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
matthew/23.note: Divines, rejection of the truth fell into depths of ignorance; how many ignorant by acceptance of the Faith, have soared aloft...

matthew/23.bible_quote: And whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased; and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted.
matthew/23.note: The learned men arose against Muhammad while the unlettered recognized and embraced Him.

matthew/23.bible_quote: Ye serpents, ye generation of vipers, how can ye escape the damnation of hell?
matthew/23.note: These same Scriptures, however, unanimously condemn the people that inhabit this land.  They have, at one time, been stigmatized as the "generation of vipers."  Behold how this wronged One is now, whilst surrounded by a "generation of vipers," calling

matthew/23.ref: The Unfoldment of World Civilization, p. 16
matthew/23.bible_quote: O Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee...
matthew/23.note: Baha'u'llah compares the evidence of Divine visitation which befell the persecutors of Jesus and Himself.  Baha'u'llah's apostrophe to Constantinople.

matthew/23.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 176
matthew/23.bible_quote: O Jerusalem...thou that killest the prophets and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings...
matthew/23.note: Compare Jesus Christ's words with Baha'u'llah's apostrophe to Constantinople as He lay in prison.
Time of the end ushered in by the Bab.

matthew/24.bible_quote: And Jesus went out and departed from the Temple...And Jesus said unto [the disciples), See ye not all these things?  Verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another that sha
matthew/24.note: We see in these days the fulfilment of divine prophecy.  Jerusalem has disappeared...now it is rebuilt.

matthew/24.ref: Star of the West, p. V:1, p. 8 (*)
matthew/24.bible_quote: And Jesus said unto them, see ye not all these things?  Verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down.
matthew/24.note: significant, no doubt, of the culmination of the old order of things. (*Tablet with NawRuz greetings)

matthew/24.bible_quote: the disciples came to Him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be?  And what shall be the sign of Thy coming, and of the end of the world?
matthew/24.note: Every time He made reply, He set forth a special sign that should herald the promised dispensation.

matthew/24.bible_quote: And He sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto Him privately, saying, Tell us when shall these things be?  And what shall be the sign of Thy coming, and of the end of the world?
matthew/24.note: He referred to prophecy of Daniel XII.  Jesus answered in the form of allegory.  Jesus prophecied of Baha'u'llah. (* 11/7/1908, Mirza Assad'u'llah)

matthew/24.bible_quote: When shall these things be?...When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place...
matthew/24.note: In this answer Jesus referred them to Daniel 8:8 which prophecied the coming of the Bab

matthew/24.bible_quote: When shall these things be?...When ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet,  stand in the holy place...
matthew/24.note: Announces the time of the appearance of Muhammad, the Bab and Baha'u'llah. (*11/7/1908, Mirza Assad'u'llah)

matthew/24.bible_quote: when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place...
matthew/24.note: Prophecied by Daniel and confirmed by Jesus Christ accomplished by the coming of the Bab.

matthew/24.bible_quote: When ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the holy place...
matthew/24.note: Ended in the coming of the Bab.

matthew/24.ref: Star of the West, II:13, p. 3
matthew/24.bible_quote: For as the lightning cometh out of the East and shineth even into the West, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be...
matthew/24.note: Give a thousand thanks to God that as the Great Sun arose in the East, His brilliant Light shone upon the West and brightened the Western world--that though you were apparently far off, in reality you were near.

matthew/24.bible_quote: For as the lightning cometh out of the East and shineth even unto the West; so shall the coming of the Son of man be.
matthew/24.note: It was many years after His ascension before they knew who He really was--so in this generation.  Do not be discouraged.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Immediately after the tribulation of those days shall the stars fall from heaven.
matthew/24.note: The stars of the heaven of knowledge have fallen.  That is what Jesus prophecied.  They adduced the proof in order to demonstrate the truth of Baha'u'llah's Cause.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Immediately the stars shall fall from the heavens...and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/24.note: Christians are expecting the stars to fall and Christ to appear in the clouds yet these are but symbols.  Jews awaiting Christ from a heaven which does not exist.

matthew/24.bible_quote: after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened and the moon shall not give her light...stars fall from heaven...tribes of the earth mourn...
matthew/24.note: The revelation of Jesus to His disciples analagous to the statement "commotions shall appear and voice of lamentation shall be raised and evidences of mischief shall be revealed."

matthew/24.bible_quote: and the stars shall fall from heaven and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken...
matthew/24.note: Baha'u'llah proclaims the fall of the stars of the firmament of knowledge who have turned aside from Him in a Tablet to Napoleon III who had cast behind his back the Tablet revealed to him by Baha'u'llah.

matthew/24.bible_quote: the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall from heaven...
matthew/24.note: Rendered into the Persian, the purport of these words is, When oppression and afflictions which befall mankind, will have come to pass, then shall the sun be withheld from shining...Explanation of symbolic language, sun, moon, stars...

matthew/24.ref: Some Answered Questions, Chapter XXVI, p. 35-43
matthew/24.bible_quote: The sun will be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall from heaven...and then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven and then shall all the tribes of the ea
matthew/24.note: His coming depends upon the fulfilment of certain signs...Baha'u'llah has explained these verses in the Kitab-i-Iqan.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Immediately after the tribulation of those days...then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven...and then they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/24.note: Jesus said similar signs would appear in the last day, when Christ will come the second time.

matthew/24.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
matthew/24.bible_quote: the stars shall fall from heaven...
matthew/24.note: Science proves that nearly all the stars are much larger than the earth.  Kingdom of heaven is within your soul...symbolism of stars, heaven...

matthew/24.bible_quote: Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/24.note: Explanation of meaning of clouds of heaven.

matthew/24.bible_quote: they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
matthew/24.note: O Pope! He Who is the Lord of Lords is come overshadowed with clouds.  He verily hath come down again...

matthew/24.bible_quote: and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/24.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha reminds us that we are veiled from the truth by literal interpretations...

matthew/24.bible_quote: and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/24.note: Jesus Christ referred to Baha'u'llah as the Son of man.

matthew/24.ref: Star of the west, X:12, p. 232 (*)
matthew/24.bible_quote: after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened and the moon shall not give her light...and they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: All these things refer to the century of the Blessed Beauty. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)

matthew/24.ref: Star of the West, XIII:6, p. 148
matthew/24.bible_quote: then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: The cloud mentioned in the Holy Scriptures is the human body because it is a veil...which, like a cloud prevents them from seeing the Sun of Truth...Christ's second coming...

matthew/24.bible_quote: they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: the clouds are vapors which rise from the earth...The cloud mentioned in the Holy Scriptures is the human body, a veil, preventing them from seeing Christ.

matthew/24.bible_quote: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven...
matthew/24.note: The Sun is ever the same but the place whence the Sun hath appeared hath changed.  The Sun means the Manifestations.  At Christ's time the sun appeared in the sign of Aquarius, but now from the sign of Cancer.  Do not become veiled by the places from

matthew/24.bible_quote: they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: There is a difference among believers as to the Second Coming of Christ.  The Promised Christ is Baha'u'llah and the Bab, not 'Abdu'l-Baha.

matthew/24.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
matthew/24.bible_quote: they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: Christ has come already...some are waiting for His coming.  Some are veiled by clouds of unbelief.  He has already come in the heart if you believe.

matthew/24.bible_quote: they shall see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory...
matthew/24.note: They see the body but not the spirit...they hear the words but are unable to understand the meaning...

matthew/24.bible_quote: And he shall send his angels...and they shall gather his elect from the four winds...
matthew/24.note: Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne--Baha'u'llah.

matthew/24.bible_quote: And He shall send His angels...and they shall gather together His elect from the four winds, from one end of the heaven to ther other.
matthew/24.note: I hope you may be quickened and vivified by the breath of the Holy Spirit.  Then shall ye become the angels of heaven whom Christ promised would appear to gather the harvest of divine planting...This is My hope and My prayer.

matthew/24.ref: 'Abdu'l-Baha to the American visitors at Acca,1900
matthew/24.bible_quote: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass till all these things be fulfilled.
matthew/24.note: The meaning of this--there are two generations.  Spiritual and physical generations.  Christ was speaking of the spiritual generation.

matthew/24.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 53
matthew/24.bible_quote: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall not pass till all these things be fulfilled.
matthew/24.note: Generation has a different meaning in different places.  In this verse it means dispensation, the Christian Dispensation.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Heaven and earth shall pass away but My words shall not pass away.
matthew/24.note: Christian understanding is that law of the Gospel shall never be annulled.  The Promised One must reaffirm and establish the law proclaimed in the Gospel--no faith but His Faith.  True meaning explained.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Heaven and earth shall pass away but My words shall not pass away.
matthew/24.note: The Word of God penetrates into the core of all things and cannot be resisted.

matthew/24.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 143
matthew/24.bible_quote: But of that day and hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels of heaven, but My Father only.
matthew/24.note: Baha'u'llah declares this is the great and terrible Day foretold in the Gospel.

matthew/24.bible_quote: ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.  But know this...if the good man of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched...
matthew/24.note: Meaning explained in the Kitab-i-Iqan.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Watch, therefore, for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come...
matthew/24.note: Thou wast awake, therefore thou didst advance and engage in the service of the Word of God.

matthew/24.bible_quote: Now he [Judas] that betrayed Him gave them a sign saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is He; hold Him fast.
matthew/24.note: There were people like Judas...who by their own deeds separated themselves from Christ.  Similarity to violators who turned against 'Abdu'l-Baha.

matthew/25.bible_quote: Well done, thou good and faithful servant.
matthew/25.note: There are some who are as lighted torches...others whose influence breeds discord.

matthew/25.bible_quote: When the Son of man shall come in His glory, and all the holy angels with him...

matthew/25.ref: Star of the West, I:12, p. 232 (*)
matthew/25.bible_quote: When the Son of man shall come in His glory, and all the holy angels with Him, then shall He sit upon the throne of His glory.  And before Him shall be gathered all nations...and He shall set the shee
matthew/25.note: This refers to Baha'u'llah (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)

matthew/26.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 58
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then assembled together the chief priests and scribes and elders of the people and consulted that they might take Jesus by subtlety...
matthew/26.note: Analogy between the rejection of Jesus Christ and the divines' rejection of Baha'u'llah.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then assembled the chief priests and scribes and elders of the people...and consulted that they might take Jesus by subtlety and kill Him.
matthew/26.note: The Jews clamored that Jesus Christ abrogated the Law of Moses regarding divorce.  They are still waiting for the manifestation.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then assembled the chief priests and scribes and elders of the people...and consulted that they might take Jesus by subtlety and kill Him.
matthew/26.note: The scribes and Pharisees spread slanders and caused Him to be crucified.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then assembled the chief priests and the scribes and the elders of the people...and consulted that they might take Jesus by subtlety and kill Him.
matthew/26.note: The Jews were entreating for the coming of the Promised One, but when He came with manifest power, they turned away from Him.  Hidden because of His name was Baha'u'llah. (*Lawh-i-Aqdas of Baha'u'llah)

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 43
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then assembled together the chief priests and scribes and the elders of the people...and consulted that they might take Jesus by sutlety and kill Him.
matthew/26.note: They held strictly to the law--they were the cause of the condemnation and crucifixion of Jesus...holding to the letter of the law.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, VIII:11, p. 144
matthew/26.bible_quote: There came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment and she broke the box and poured it on His head...Why was this waste?
matthew/26.note: Symbolism: the bottle is Mary's heart; the ointment is the love of God in Mary's heart.  All this love she poured forth at the feet of Jesus.  We must give all our heart's love to God.

matthew/26.bible_quote: There came unto Him a woman having an alabaster box of ointment and she broke the box and poured it on His head...this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor...she did it for My
matthew/26.note: They did not understand the wisdom of what He did.

matthew/26.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 42
matthew/26.bible_quote: For in that [Mary] hath poured this ointment on My body, she did it for My burial.
matthew/26.note: In embalming they washed and perfumed the dead.  Christ was to be crucified so she annointed Him and dried it with her hair.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XII:9, p. 157 (*)
matthew/26.bible_quote: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this that this woman hath done be told for a memorial of her.
matthew/26.note: There was one name that always brought joy to the face of Baha'u'llah.  His expression would change at the mention of it...it was Mary Magdalene.  (*Diary of Juliet Thompson)

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353 (*)
matthew/26.bible_quote: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this Gospel shall be preached in the whole world there shall this that this woman hath done be told for a memorial of her.
matthew/26.note: Were it not for the tests, the face of Mary, the Magdalene would not glisten with the light of firmness and certainty unto all horizons. (*Tablet to Mrs. I.D. Brittingham)

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 269
matthew/26.bible_quote: Judas Iscariot went unto the chief priests and said, What will ye give me and I will deliver [Jesus] unto you?  And they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver.
matthew/26.note: Violators like Judas Iscariot have for financial motives deviated from the Covenant.  Baha'i unity cannot be preserved save through the Covenant of God.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242 (*Tablet to)
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then one of the twelve called Judas Iscariot went unto the chief priests and said unto them, What will ye give me and I will deliver Him unto you...
matthew/26.note: Covenant is the ocean; violators are the foam on it.  Violators manifest temporary activity...but soon, like Judas Iscariot and his associates are those who approved of his conduct, they shall be completely forgotten.(*MacNutt, Brooklyn)

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then Judas Iscariot went unto the priests and said unto them, What will ye give me and I will deliver [Jesus] unto you?
matthew/26.note: Judas separated himself from Jesus.  Any person who has left the Cause did so because of his own actions, intrigue and evil plots.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
matthew/26.bible_quote: They made ready the Passover...when evening was come He sat down with the twelve...
matthew/26.note: It was not in a church.  On that night they had the Last Supper, it was in a room.  The idea is the heart should be attracted, the spirit filled with divine Glad Tidings and the soul turned to God...This is the Object--other things are secondary...

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:9, p. 284
matthew/26.bible_quote: And the disciples made ready the Passover.  Now when even was come He sat down with the twelve.
matthew/26.note: Because there was excessive love among the members who convened there, that table, the Lord's Table, surpassed the royal tables.  Even now it is known as such.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 275 (Tablet to*)
matthew/26.bible_quote: When even was come He sat down with the twelve...and they did eat.
matthew/26.note: The manna which came from heaven to the disciples was bounty, knowledge, faith and assurance, love and attraction, attachment and enkindlement...My hope is that that gathering may also eat from this manna and these bounties. (*Mr. Hall)

matthew/26.bible_quote: when the even was come [Jesus] sat down with the twelve...took bread and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples and said, Take, eat, this is My body.  And He took the cup...saying, Dri
matthew/26.note: The Unity Feast is that Divine Table, the Lord's Supper.

matthew/26.bible_quote: when even was come [Jesus] sat down with the twelve...And they did eat; Jesus took break...and said, Take, eat, this is My body.
matthew/26.note: The Supper of the Lord which Christ ate with the Apostles was a heavenly supper.  The food was the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowals of God.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then Judas, which betrayed Him answered...Master, is it I?
matthew/26.note: Baha'u'llah has left no room for discussion--some wish to create dissension as did Judas.  Violators must be sent away.

matthew/26.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/26.bible_quote: The Judas, which betrayed Him, answered and said, Master, is it I?
matthew/26.note: Judas, one of the twelve disciples deserted Jesus Christ...others were not strong.  God assists the firm.

matthew/26.bible_quote: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples...Take, eat, this is My body...
matthew/26.note: Designated the Lord's Supper, they ate heavenly food at that supper...the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowals of God.

matthew/26.bible_quote: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread and blessed it and brake it and gave it to His disciples, and said, Take, eat, this is My body.  And He took the cup...This is My blood of the new testament.
matthew/26.note: because there was excessive love among them...that Table surpassed the royal tables...

matthew/26.bible_quote: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread and blessed it and brake it and gave it to the disciples and said, Take, eat, this is My body.
matthew/26.note: Make ye an effort in every meeting that the Lord's Supper may become realized and heavenly food--love, affinity, union of souls descend, then 'Abdu'l-Baha will be present also.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Jesus took bread and blessed it and brake it...Take, eat, this is My body.
matthew/26.note: Jesus was not transformed into bread and wine.  They were symbols.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 203 (*)
matthew/26.bible_quote: As they were eating Jesus took bread and blessed it and brake it...and He took the cup and gave thanks and gave it to them.
matthew/26.note: Inasmuch as there was present the material bread and likewise heavenly Manna which was descending upon them, it was verily the Lord's Supper. (*Address at Nineteen-day Feast at Mrs. Helen Goodall's home 10/16/1912)

matthew/26.bible_quote: For this is My blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
matthew/26.note: Christ Who is the Word of God sacrificed Himself.  This has two meanings--an apparent one and an esoteric one.  He arose to teach and educate men--He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life--to quicken others.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258
matthew/26.bible_quote: Drink ye all of it; for this is My blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
matthew/26.note: [Jesus] came, people say, to redeem us through His blood.  He sacrificed Himself that He might educate and illumine the world.

matthew/26.bible_quote: For this is My blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
matthew/26.note: There is no love more perfect than to give one's life--Jesus proved His love--then His followers believed and were delivered from sins. (*11/11/1908, Mirza Haydar 'Ali)

matthew/26.bible_quote: For this is My blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
matthew/26.note: These verses have been interpreted by the churches in such a superstitious way that it is impossible for human reason to accept the explanation.

matthew/26.bible_quote: For this is My blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins.
matthew/26.note: I gave My blood for the life of the world...Those who turned towards Christ were saved from their attachment to sin.

matthew/26.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90
matthew/26.bible_quote: I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's Kingdom.
matthew/26.note: It is evident that Christ did not mean material grapes, but it was a spiritual state which He interpreted as fruit.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, II:15, p. 16
matthew/26.bible_quote: But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in My Father's Kingdom.
matthew/26.note: It is evident that Jesus Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual condition and a heavenly state which He interpreted as fruit.

matthew/26.bible_quote: I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in My Father's Kingdom.
matthew/26.note: The appearance of the Holy Manifestations is for the purpose of causing the heavenly table to descend.  This table means divine virtues and characteristics.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then cometh Jesus...unto a place called Gethsemane...began to be sorrowful...He fell on His face...
matthew/26.note: Death of Quddus exceeds the agony of Jesus Christ.

matthew/26.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 69
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane...
matthew/26.note: Some day the Rizwan will be visited by pilgrims from all over the world just as the Garden of Gethsemane is sacred with memories of Jesus Christ.

matthew/26.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
matthew/26.bible_quote: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation...
matthew/26.note: Fast and pray, that ye enter not into temptation...(Not found in Gospels as we know them)

matthew/26.bible_quote: the Spirit indeed is willing but the flesh is weak.
matthew/26.note: Whatever troubles we have on our way to the Kingdom are a test to the soul.  The way to God is strewn with troubles and difficulties;remember what Christ said...the Spirit is powerful.

matthew/26.bible_quote: the Spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
matthew/26.note: the spirit of man discovers the realities of all things.  However, the spirit of man is powerful and the body is weak.

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 270
matthew/26.bible_quote: Judas, one of the twelve, came and with him a multitude.  Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, the same is He; and forthwith he came to Jesus and kissed Him.
matthew/26.note: Judas Iscariot first believed in Christ then turned away from Him.  I hope that thou mayest daily increase in firmness and become the cause of guidance to other souls.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is He; hold Him fast.
matthew/26.note: There were people like Judas...who, by their own deeds separated themselves from Christ.  Similarity to violators who turned against 'Abdu'l-Baha.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign, saying, Whosoever I shall kiss the same is He; hold Him fast.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him...kissed Him.
matthew/26.note: Be pure, to be pure is to be selfless...Jesus had eleven disciples for the twelfth was the cause of His crucifixion.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss that is He; hold Him fast.
matthew/26.note: The betrayal of Baha'u'llah by Mirza Yahya is more shameful than the betrayal of Jesus Christ by Judas.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss that same is He; hold Him fast.
matthew/26.note: Judas began well but failed in the end.  Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants.  It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Address in London or Paris)

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now he that betrayed Him gave them a sign...
matthew/26.note: Consider the predecessors as a warning...and purify thy heart.  Judas turned away because of jealousy...

matthew/26.bible_quote: Put up again thy sword into his place...
matthew/26.note: Jesus Christ forbade warfare.  Baha'u'llah revoiced the teachings of the prophets.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Put up thy sword in his place...
matthew/26.note: When we hold fast to the foundation of religion, differences disappear.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Put up again thy sword into his place...
matthew/26.note: The purpose of the life of Jesus Christ and the glory of His death was to set mankind free from the sin of strife, war and bloodshed...These wars are anti-Christ.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Put up thy sword in his place...
matthew/26.note: The meaning is that warfare is abrogated, but consider the Christian wars...need to turn back to fundamental principles of Prophets.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Put up again thy sword in his place...
matthew/26.note: Jesus Christ taught, through this act, that war was abrogated.  The Jews rejected Jesus because of their literal interpretation, which veiled the truth.

matthew/26.bible_quote: And they laid hold on Jesus and led Him away to Caiaphas, the high priest...and the high priest said...Answerest Thou nothing?...But Jesus held His peace....And when they had bound Him they led Him aw
matthew/26.note: They led Him away, He Who was the Day-Star of the heaven of Divine Revelation.  Jesus held His peace and spake not.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Now the chief priests and elders and all the council sought false witness against Jesus, to put Him to death.
matthew/26.note: Jews were awaiting the Messiah...when He came Jews rejected Jesus.  The same is the case with Baha'u'llah.

matthew/26.bible_quote: I abjure thee by the living God that Thou tell us whether thou be the Christ, the Son of God.  Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast said.
matthew/26.note: Jesus Christ spoke of Himself as the Son of God. (*not quote from 'Abdu'l-Baha but from the pilgrims)

matthew/26.bible_quote: Jesus saith unto him, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power...
matthew/26.note: These were His words, yet consider how to outward seeming He was devoid of all power except that inner power which was of God.  How can I describe their heinous behaviour towards Him?

matthew/26.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 27
matthew/26.bible_quote: Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven...
matthew/26.note: Failure of kings of Christendom to draw near unto Baha'u'llah

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, VIII:13, p. 180 (*Tablet for)

matthew/26.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
matthew/26.bible_quote: Peter denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.
matthew/26.note: The tests made Peter the Apostle deny Christ.  Many a just soul did not endure the power of tests.

matthew/26.bible_quote: But [Peter] denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.
matthew/26.note: Peter denied that He knew Jesus--at that time he was weak for he had not yet received the Holy Spirit.  Afterward he became firm.  Be pure, to be pure is to be selfless.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Names of the twelve apostles.
matthew/26.note: Christ in reality only educated eleven men of whom one thrice denied Him.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls...

matthew/26.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33

matthew/26.note: Peter was the head appointed by Christ, yet he denied the Christ three times.  What happened afterwards?  The truth in Christ spread all over the world.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then began he to curse...saying, I know not the man.  And immediately the cock crew.  And Peter remembered the word of Jesus...
matthew/26.note: This was before the Holy Spirit had descended on Him at Pentecost

matthew/26.note: When Jesus Christ was crucified He had twelve disciples--one denied Him...another sold Him for money; notwithstanding this, consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would sacrifice their lives for Baha'u'llah

matthew/26.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353
matthew/26.bible_quote: Then began Peter to swear...saying, I know not the man.  And Peter remembered the words of Jesus...
matthew/26.note: Even the glorious Peter was not rescued from the flame of trials...and wavered.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then began he to swear...saying, I know not this man...
matthew/26.note: Christ only educated eleven men; the greatest of them was Peter who, when tested, thrice denied Christ.  In spite of this the Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Reflect what will take place in the future...

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then began Peter to swear...saying, I know not the man...
matthew/26.note: Peter who was the Rock was firm and solid...but the Rock quaked a little.  I hope you will become steadfast...firm...solid.

matthew/26.bible_quote: Then began he to swear, saying, I know not the man...
matthew/26.note: Though Peter was the great one among the disciples, he denied Jesus three times.  Jews thought they had extinguished the fire but it kindled the hearts of the righteous.

matthew/26.bible_quote: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus...
matthew/26.note: Comparison between Peter and Mary Magdalene, and example of the capability of woman.  Baha'u'llah emancipated women.

matthew/26.bible_quote: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus...
matthew/26.note: Comparison of the behaviour of Peter and that of Mary Magdalene at the time of the crucifixion of Jesus Christ...a proof that woman is equivalent and a complement of man.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And [Judas] cast down the pieces of silver in the Temple, and departed, and went and hanged himself.
matthew/27.note: Impossible to train another until he first conquers his own self and his desires...Judas was deprived of the Kingdom of God.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And he cast down the pieces of silver in the Temple and departed, and went and hanged himself...
matthew/27.note: if foot of man deviate as did that of Judas so that he became human after he had been divine...there is in that a great lesson to people of understanding.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they stripped Him and put on Him a scarlet robe...a crown of thorns...mocked Him...and spit upon Him...led Him away to be crucified.
matthew/27.note: Baha'u'llah recalls the sufferings of Jesus Christ and the earlier Prophets, caused by the divines.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they stripped Him and put on Him a scarlet robe...when they platted a crown of thorns they put it on His head...bowed their knee before Him...spit upon Him...smote Him on the head.
matthew/27.note: Manifestations met with fierce opposition by the divines.  Reflect how Jesus was treated by His enemies.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they stripped Him and put on Him a scarlet robe...a crown of thorns...mocked Him...spit upon Him...led Him away to be crucified.
matthew/27.note: Baha'u'llah recalled in anticipation of His own lot that of Jesus Christ.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they stripped Him and put on Him a scarlet robe...when they had platted a crown of thorns they put it on His head...bowed their knee before Him...spit upon Him...smote Him on the head.
matthew/27.note: Manifestations have been opposed by the embodiment of the Powers of Darkness.

matthew/27.bible_quote: They stripped Him...platted a crown of thorns and put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him, and took the reed and smote Him upon the head...
matthew/27.note: All through His life He suffered opposition and hardship.  Humanity was His enemy.  In spite of all this He was the embodiment of pity, supreme goodness and love.

matthew/27.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 31
matthew/27.bible_quote: when they had platted a crown of thorns they put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him and took the reed and smote Him on the head...
matthew/27.note: All Prophets of God were scorned and persecuted.  Men have worshipped the personality but were blind to the Reality, truth.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head...
matthew/27.note: Jesus Christ knew that all earthly diadems were at His feet, therefore he said, All power is given Me in Heaven and on earth.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...
matthew/27.note: His abasement was His glorification; His crown of thorns is a heavenly diadem.  When they pressed it upon His blessed head...they laid the foundation of His everlasting Kingdom.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they platted a crown of thorns and put it on His head...spit on Him...smote Him on the head...mocked Him.
matthew/27.note: There was no abuse and persecution which they did not heap upon Him.  They did not investigate reality.  When man investigates truth and finds it, he must hold to it.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...they mocked Him...they spit upon Him and took the reed and smote Him on the head.
matthew/27.note: Jews rose against Jesus but He is now glorified; in spite of the hatred of men God's dominion will be set up, eternal.

matthew/27.bible_quote: and when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...spit upon Him and took the reed and smote Him on the head...and led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: The prophets of God were persecuted.  Jesus Christ accepted martyrdom in order to summon mankind to unity and love.  It is the divine purpose in religion.

matthew/27.bible_quote: when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him and took the reed and smote Him on the head...and led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: All Prophets of God were scorned and persecuted.  Jesus was rejected, tortured.

matthew/27.bible_quote: when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...and led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: To all outward appearances they overcame Him...but all the kings of the earth have not been able to withstand Him.

matthew/27.note: He endured all things so that the world might unite and that He might cement the hearts of men through His love. (*Address in Stuttgart, 4/6/1913)

matthew/27.ref: Star of the West, IX:15, p. 171 (*)

matthew/27.note: Later on it became manifest that He was the Light of Truth and was bestowing the spirit of eternal life.  No Prophet cometh unto them but they laugh Him to scorn. (*Tablet to Juanita Storch)

matthew/27.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633
matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him...smote Him upon the head...
matthew/27.note: The Jews cried, O God, hasten the day when the Promised One will manifest Himself.  He claims to be from Nazareth; He was to come with a sword, sit on the throne of David, conquer East and West, teach the law of Moses.  They could not see Reality

matthew/27.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258
matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him...smote Him on the head...
matthew/27.note: He was mocked, scorned and laughed at, yet His Mission was to uplift the very world that refused Him.  Christ is always Christ.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns they put it upon His head...mocked Him...spit upon Him...
matthew/27.note: Reflect how the Pharisees persecuted Christ.  The result was that His lamp became ignited, His light began to shine...

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...
matthew/27.note: Literal interpretation blinded the eyesight of the Jews...Pharisees had evil opinions of Jesus Christ.  But the radiance of His illumination was diffused over the whole world; they regarded even the ass on which He was mounted as a throne

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns they put it upon His head...mocked Him...
matthew/27.note: Remember what the Pharisees published concerning Jesus...Of the same class are the Pharisees and priests in this manifest day.

matthew/27.bible_quote: when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...
matthew/27.note: Alas for the souls, because of what they committed against the Word of God, Christ.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...
matthew/27.note: Consider how they crowned Jesus with a crown of thorns...now how thousands of people adorn their heads in His name with most brilliant gems.  Woe! woe unto such heedless people.

matthew/27.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 13
matthew/27.bible_quote: and when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...mocked Him...
matthew/27.note: The world must come to know the Word of Christ...how He was mocked...Teaching truth is like building bridges by which humanity may cross over the current which threatens it.

matthew/27.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 19
matthew/27.bible_quote: And after they had mocked Him...led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: The enemies of Christ persecuted and crucified Him yet He loved them all.  If the love of God is shining in our hearts we will...love all alike.

matthew/27.bible_quote: when they had platted a crown of thorns, they put it upon His head...spit upon Him...smote Him upon the head...mocked Him...led Him away to be crucified.
matthew/27.note: The Jews did not know the meaning of the prophecies.  Literal interpretation veiled the true meaning.

matthew/27.bible_quote: and led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: All were shaken but Mary Magdalen.  She was a veritable lioness.  She gathered the others together and said, "Why do ye mourn?  Did not the Christ foretell His crucifixion?  Arise, and be assured.  they have killed but the body; the reality can never die

matthew/27.bible_quote: and led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: The Jews rose against Jesus Christ even to the shedding of His blood.  Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices the cause of hatred and war.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they led Him away to be crucified.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: The Talmud and its superstitions veiled His Holiness so completely that they crucified their promised Messiah.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: Blind imitations and hereditary prejudices have become the cause of hatred...therefore we must seek truth.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they led Him away to be crucified.
matthew/27.note: Consider how harmful is imitation.  We must investigate reality.

matthew/27.bible_quote: they led Him away to be crucified.
matthew/27.note: He was crucified for the love of mankind.  The Bab and Baha'u'llah suffered because they were aflame with love for mankind.

matthew/27.bible_quote: the led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: If the Jews had investigated the Reality of Christ they would have beheld His beauty and truth--they were withheld because of bigoted imitations.

matthew/27.bible_quote: and they led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: When Jesus Christ died upon the cross the disciples were shaken.  Peter, one of the greatest denied Him thrice.  Mary Magdalene confirmed their faith.  This is a proof that woman is the equivalent and complement of man.

matthew/27.bible_quote: the led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: The body of Jesus was crucified but the Spirit of Christ is always pouring upon the world.

matthew/27.bible_quote: and they led Him away to crucify Him.
matthew/27.note: In the time of Jesus, cruelty and tyranny were such that people even crucified Jesus...Literal interpretation veiled the truth from the Jews.  They had no knowledge of the symbology of the Scriptures.

matthew/27.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 100
matthew/27.bible_quote: and they crucified Him.
matthew/27.note: Suffering inflicted on Jesus Christ by the priests similar to that inflicted on other martyrs by divines.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: The Jews rose against Jesus Christ even to the shedding of His blood.  Had they investigated reality they would have accepted their promised Messiah, Jesus Christ.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: The Jews were blind to Reality.  They were veiled by literal interpretation.  They were deprived of the beauty of Christ...They rejected and crucified Him.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: No death of religious founder comparable to that of the Bab except in the Gospels.

matthew/27.bible_quote: and they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: A prayer revealed by Baha'u'llah exalts Mirza Mihdi's death to the rank of those acts of atonement associated with Abraham's intended sacrifice, crucifixion of Jesus Christ and the martyrdom of Imam Husayn.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: The Jews expected the Messiah to be the Prince of peace; because He did not meet the prophecied conditions, they crucified Him.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: Christ suffered.  He accepted all His trials because of the immortality of His Spirit.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: He gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.  His heart was full of love for all the world, His goodness was destined to reach each one--as they began to realize these things they repented--but He had been crucified.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: Christ suffered unto death to bring Light...all prophets of God came to promote unity.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him and parted His garments...
matthew/27.note: His Holiness Jesus Christ gave His life upon the cross for the unity of mankind.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...
matthew/27.note: may you offer yourselves in the threshold of sacrifice for the betterment of the world.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...
matthew/27.note: They sentenced Him to death.  Why did this happen?  Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus.  They refused to investigate truth.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...
matthew/27.note: Christ suffered.  He accepted all His trials because of the immortality of His Spirit...gave His blood in order to guide mankind...offered His life as a sacrifice to illumine humanity.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...
matthew/27.note: Before the appearance in human form the Word of God was in the utmost sanctity and glory...when it shone in the body it fell into the hands of the Jews and at last was crucified.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...
matthew/27.note: He arose to teach and educate men and so He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life.  He perished in body so as to quicken others by His spirit.

matthew/27.bible_quote: And they crucified Him...

matthew/28.ref: Star of the West, X:17, p. 307 (*)
matthew/28.bible_quote: In the end of the Sabbath came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre...and the angels said...go quickly and tell the disciples...and they did run to bring His disciples word...
matthew/28.note: Later on it became evident what crowns studded with brilliant jewels of guidance were placed on the heads of Mary Magdalene and the 'other Mary', the Mother of Jesus. (*Unveiling the Divine Plan for the Islands of the Sea)

matthew/28.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 62
matthew/28.bible_quote: Fear not ye; for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified...He is not here: for He is risen...
matthew/28.note: Because it is clear literal text, it is believed...Explanation of symbolism.

matthew/28.bible_quote: As Christ came from a spiritual heaven of the Divine Kingdom, therefore His disappearance under the earth for three days has an inner signification and is not an outward fact.
And the angel said...go and tell His disciples that He has risen...
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene was transformed and became the means through which the confirmation of God descended.

matthew/28.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/28.bible_quote: and go quickly, and tell His disciples that He is risen from the dead.
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene held fastly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.
