matthew/28.bible_quote: The Angel said to Mary Magdalene and the other Mary...Go quickly and tell His disciples that He is risen...
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene strengthened the disciples.  In the sight of God sex makes no difference.  He or she is greatest who is nearest God.

matthew/28.bible_quote: And go quickly and tell His disciples that He is risen...
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene was superior to all the men of her time.  She gathered the others together and said, Why do y mourn?  Her reality is shining from the horizon of Christ.

matthew/28.bible_quote: go quickly and tell His disciples that He is risen from the dead...
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene kept firm in the Cause; she surpassed men in depending the Faith against suspicions.  This is a manifest light.

matthew/28.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/28.bible_quote: and go and tell His disciples that He is risen...
matthew/28.note: Mary Magdalene held fastly to the Christ and made others firm.  God will assist all who are firm in the Cause.

matthew/28.bible_quote: All power is given unto Me in heaven and in earth.
matthew/28.note: Christ was a receiver of inspiration; but what a difference between the inspiration of the Word of God and the revelations of Isaiah, Jeremiah and Elijah!

matthew/28.bible_quote: Go ye therefore and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/28.note: Jesus lighted the torch...after Him the Apostles went far carrying the Gospel.

matthew/28.bible_quote: Go ye therefore and teach all nations, baptizing them in the aname of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/28.note: Consider the influence of Jesus Christ on His Apostles.  These simple men were enabled by the Power of the Holy Spirit to spread the Glad Tidings.

matthew/28.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 41 (*)
matthew/28.bible_quote: Jesus spake unto them saying...teach all nations...
matthew/28.note: We must be willing to give our lives...we must go among the peoples of the world...summoning them. (*Address at 309 W. 78th St, New York)

matthew/28.ref: Star of the West, V:1, p. 6
matthew/28.bible_quote: Go ye therefore and teach all nations...
matthew/28.note: Command to travel in every country...as the Apostles of Christ did.

matthew/28.ref: Star of the West, VII:10, p. 85 (*)
matthew/28.bible_quote: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Ghost.
matthew/28.note: Through this superhuman service the rays of peace and conciliation may illumine and enlighten all the regions, and the world of humanity may find peace. (*Tablet to the West)

matthew/5.bible_quote: Sermon on the Mount
matthew/5.note: Among [Jesus's) principles of spiritual civilization He came to proclaim is the Most Great Peace of mankind--the Oneness of the kingdom of humanity.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, X:15, p. 282 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Blessed are the poor in spirit; for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
matthew/5.note: Be severed from the physical world, become angels of heaven and travel and teach through all these regions...each one shall blow the breath of life in the souls of others. (*Unveiling the Divine Plan for the Western World)

matthew/5.bible_quote: Blessed are the pure in heart; for they shall see God.
matthew/5.note: Such must be the purity of character that people may be drawn closer to the Heavenly Father, the Source of purity and grace.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Blessed are the pure in heart for they shall see God.
matthew/5.note: The pure in heart shall see with the eyes of the Spirit and shall recognize God in His great Manifestation, Baha'u'llah.

matthew/5.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
matthew/5.bible_quote: Blessed are the pure in heart; for they shall see God.
matthew/5.note: Be of the Spirit, not of the body.  The Light of the body is the eye.  Spirtuality is the possession of a good pure heart.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye are the salt of the earth but if the salt have lost its savour, wherewith shall it be salted...Let your light so shine before men...
matthew/5.note: The Bab's admonition to His disciples to ponder the words of Jesus as He sent out His disciples.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye are the salt of the earth; but if the salt have lost its savour, wherewith shall it be salted?
matthew/5.note: oneness of God and of mankind...this is the bond of union among Baha'is all over the world.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye are the salt of the earth...
matthew/5.note: the friends of God are the salt of the earth.  [They] have been endowed with the ability and capacity on which account they became the "chosen" and not the "called".

matthew/5.bible_quote: Neither do men light a candle and put it under a bushel but on a candlestick and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.
matthew/5.note: Be upon your guard in order that none may assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llha's Cause.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets; I am not come to destroy but to fulfil.
matthew/5.note: All spiritual prophecies concerning the coming of Christ were fulfilled but the Divine Reality of Jesus was unrecognized.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to destroy the law or the prophets; I am come not to destroy but to fulfil...Whosoever shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called t
matthew/5.note: Rebuke of Christ to the Jews.  He promulgated the ten commandments. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 173 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to destroy the law or the prophets; I am not come to destroy but to fulfil.
matthew/5.note: The character of divine sovereignty has no change or transformation, but the organization and administration change continually.  Two kinds of divine teachings, the spiritual and the material. (*Article, International Psychic Gazette)

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to destroy the law or the prophets; I am not come to destroy but to fulfil...Whosoever shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called t
matthew/5.note: When Jesus Christ appeared among the Jews, the first thing He did was to proclaim the validity of the Mosaic Mission.  He declared that the Torah, the Old Testament, was the Word of God. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 636 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Think not that I am come to destroy the law or the prophets; I am come not to destroy but to fulfil...Whosoever shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called t
matthew/5.note: The foundation of the reality of the law of Moses is that of Christianity.  Had it not been for Christ you would not have heard the name of Moses. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 333)

matthew/5.bible_quote: Whosoever shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the Kingdom of heaven; but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called gre
matthew/5.note: Jesus Christ renewed the Commandments.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/5.note: Jesus spread the name of Moses throughout the Orient and the Occident.  We must investigate reality...not accept ancestral imitations or hearsay.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the Kingdom of heaven but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be c
matthew/5.note: Jesus renewed the commandments which Moses had received from Jehovah. He said the Commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

matthew/5.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 77
matthew/5.bible_quote: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the Kingdom of heaven...
matthew/5.note: Christ taught obedience.  Muhammad taught resignation.  Baha'u'llah taught renunciation.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, an eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you...love your enemies.
matthew/5.note: This was according to the spirit of the [Mosaic] age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, But I say unto you...Love your enemies...
matthew/5.note: The stern old law changed into one of love by Manifestations.

matthew/5.ref: The Principles of Religious Unity, p. 4
matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you, that ye resist not evil...turn to him the other cheek...
matthew/5.note: Jesus Christ abrogated laws because conditions had changed requiring the laws.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you...love your enemies...
matthew/5.note: Every one of the divine religions contains essential ordinances which are not subject to change, and material ordinances which are abrogated according to the exigencies of time.  Christ abrogated the commands of Moses...

matthew/5.bible_quote: It hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you...love your enemies...
matthew/5.note: These laws were applicable to the period, but in the time of Jesus not applicable...therefore abrogated.  Through truth all would be united and agreed.

matthew/5.bible_quote: it hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you that ye resist not evil...
matthew/5.note: His Holiness Christ changed laws...non-essentials are changed according to the exigencies of the time and conditions.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, an eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you...
matthew/5.note: Social laws changed in accordance with the exigencies of the time, place and conditions.  Manifestations changed such laws.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, VIII:4, p. 47 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Ye have heard that it hath been said, An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth, but I say unto you resist not evil.
matthew/5.note: Different teachings are needed for different people. (*Talk with Mrs. Dreyfus-Barney)

matthew/5.bible_quote: resist not evil; but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek turn to him the other also...
matthew/5.note: When we hold fast to the foundation of religion, difference will disappear.  We should not resist evil doers.

matthew/5.bible_quote: But I say unto you, that you resist not evil; but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
matthew/5.note: allegiance to the Word of God alone establishes unity and love.

matthew/5.bible_quote: whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other...
matthew/5.note: Explanation of justice and mercy of God.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
matthew/5.bible_quote: Whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
matthew/5.note: No connection between the holy teachings and war. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 116
matthew/5.bible_quote: I say unto you, that ye resist not evil...
matthew/5.note: Remarks about disarmament and universal peace.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, VI, p. 61
matthew/5.bible_quote: whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
matthew/5.note: All religions have two departments, spiritual and material...investigation of truth.  Religion is one.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, IV:6, p. 104
matthew/5.bible_quote: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you and pray for them which despitefully use you and persecute you.
matthew/5.note: Welcome persecution, hostility and bitterness as the means of testing and increasing your supreme faith in God.

matthew/5.bible_quote: love your enemies, pray for them which despitefully use you...That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven; for He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain
matthew/5.note: There is need of turning back to the original foundation.  Baha'u'llah has re-established the teachings of the Prophets.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you and persecute you.
matthew/5.note: Consider how much the principles of the religion of Christ have been forgotten...and many heresies appeared.

matthew/5.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (DMAS, 5/23/1914)
matthew/5.bible_quote: But I say unto you, love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you and pray for them which despitefully use and persecute you...
matthew/5.note: Admonitions regarding true brotherhood.  The Baha'is must fulfil to the letter the requirements of these holy principles.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Love your enemies...pray for them that despitefully use you...sendeth rain on the just and the unjust.
matthew/5.note: Hatred and persecution cannot be reconciled with Christ's teaching...God's love and care for all mankind.

matthew/5.bible_quote: He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good; and He sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
matthew/5.note: The bounty of God is transcendent, unrestricted, universal.

matthew/5.bible_quote: He maketh His sun to rise on the evil and on the good and He sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
matthew/5.note: the mercy of God is universal.

matthew/5.bible_quote: for He maketh the sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and He sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
matthew/5.note: Mercy of God encircles all of mankind.  No soul is deprived.

matthew/5.ref: World Order through World Faith,  p. 17
matthew/5.bible_quote: for He maketh the sun to rise on the evil and on the good and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
matthew/5.note: All are sheltered beneath the tree of His providence and mercy--the mercy of God is universal.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven in perfect.
matthew/5.note: It necessitates the focalization of all heavenly virtues...Be the image and likeness of God.

matthew/5.bible_quote: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.
matthew/5.note: The world of humanity must...be the recipient of bounties from the eternal.  Man must learn...from God Himself.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: Strive that your ideals may be realized in the Kingdom of God on earth...Use your knowledge so war may cease and the glorious edifice of Peace be raised.

matthew/6.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46
matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come.  Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: A world catastrophe must preceed and usher in the Kingdom...as anticipated by Jesus Christ.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come.  Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: That prayer is also a prophecy...depends on your service.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come.  Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: If people of the entire globe are welded in one great commonwealth the prayer "Thy will be done" will be a reality.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come.  Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: This is the time of which Jesus Christ spoke when He gave the Lord's Prayer...I hope that this is your expectation and great desire.

matthew/6.bible_quote: But seek ye first the kingdom of God and His righteousness...
matthew/6.note: It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Seek ye first the Kingdom of God and His righteousness and all these things shall be added unto you.
matthew/6.note: Can this be done by human power?  No, in the name of God!  For example, Christ alone...upraised the standard of peace and righteousness.

matthew/6.bible_quote: But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth.
matthew/6.note: Some days He ['Abdu'l-Baha] hardly saw His own family, so pressed was He by those who crowded to the Birubi for some kind of help--following the above teaching of Christ.

matthew/6.bible_quote: But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy closet, and when thou hast shut the door pray to thy Father which is in secret...
matthew/6.note: With the language of the heart man prays to God.
Reconstruction of the world must take place before the Will of God can be done on earth--signs of awakening since Baha'u'llah's Revelation.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come.  Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha made plans to carry Divine Plans for establishing the Kingdom of God in the Western world.

matthew/6.bible_quote: Thy kingdom come...on earth as it is in heaven.
matthew/6.note: Baha'u'llah is the establisher of the Kingdom which Jesus Christ promised.

mark/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 36
mark/1.bible_quote: The voice of one crying in the wilderness...
mark/1.note: John the Baptist came before Christ.  Each Manifestation came after a Herald.  Ahmad and Kazim were forerunners before the Manifestation of this Day.

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 151
mark/1.bible_quote: As it is written in the prophets, Behold I send my messenger...the voice of one crying in the wilderness...John did baptize in the wilderness and preach the baptism of repentance...
mark/1.note: Explanation that return of Elias is not of the personality but the reality of the perfections.

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 103
mark/1.bible_quote: If, indeed have baptized you with water; but He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
mark/1.note: Water symbolizes the knowledge of God which gives eternal life because all life had its beginning in water.  Knowledge and purity is water, love is fire...Ceremonies have spiritual meaning...It is not true that if a child dies without the baptism

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38 (*)
mark/1.bible_quote: I indeed have baptized you with water; but He shall baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
mark/1.note: The Spirit is conducive to everlasting glory; it is the cause of the exultation of the world of humanity.  May each one of you become the recipient of the Spirit.  (*Talk in Paris, 2/14/1913)

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 97
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw the heavens opened and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him.
mark/1.note: This is not a material dove...it was a spiritual state expressed by a figure.  Symbolism abounds in Scriptures.

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 104
mark/1.bible_quote: And it came to pass in those days that Jesus came and was baptized of John in the Jordan.
mark/1.note: to complete the law of the former religion He confirmed the past law.  Christ desired this institution to be used at that time.

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 254
mark/1.bible_quote: Jesus was baptized of John in Jordan.  And coming out of the wwater He saw the heavens opened and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him.
mark/1.note: Two-fold nature of the Manifestations--bodily and Oneness with God.  It had not been revealed unto Him before His baptism that His station was that of the Manifestation of God.

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, XIV:9, p. 274
mark/1.bible_quote: Jesus was baptized of John in Jordan...and coming up out of the water He saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him.
mark/1.note: The story about the dove is simply a metaphor.  No dove came down...the Holy Spirit was always with Christ.  He knew about His Mission from early childhood.

mark/1.ref: God Passes By, p. 93
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him.  And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
mark/1.note: The Call of Baha'u'llah surpassed the experience of Jesus Christ at His baptism.

mark/1.ref: God Passes By, p. 101
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw the heavens opened and the Spirit...like a dove descending upon Him...and a voice saying, Thou art My beloved Son.
mark/1.note: Most Great Spirit designated by Baha'u'llah symbolized in Christian Dispensation as the Dove.

mark/1.ref: Messages to America, 1932-46, p. 100
mark/1.bible_quote: Jesus was baptized of John...and coming out of the water He saw...the Spirit like a dove descending upon Him and there came a voice out of heaven saying, Thou art My beloved Son.
mark/1.note: The descent of the dove upon Jesus Christ symbolic of the Annunciation to Baha'u'llah of His Mission.

mark/1.ref: Kitab-i-Iqan, p. 57
mark/1.bible_quote: And there came a voice from heaven saying, Thou art My belove Son in whom I am well pleased.
mark/1.note: God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit, Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood, and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

mark/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
mark/1.bible_quote: there came a voice from heaven saying, Thou art My beloved Son.
mark/1.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 131
mark/1.bible_quote: And there came a voice out of heaven saying, Thou art My beloved Son.
mark/1.note: The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holly Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.

mark/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
mark/1.bible_quote: there came a voice from heaven saying, Thou art My beloved Son.
mark/1.note: Manifestations are expected but when They come, they are rejected because of the blindness of people.

mark/1.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 38
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw Simon and Andrew and they were fishers.  And Jesus said, Come ye after Me and I will make you fishers of men...
mark/1.note: Peter was the first Apostle called.  Observe how this crude and helpless instrument was transformed by the power of the Son Jesus Christ; how much more is that of the Father revealed in Baha'u'llah.

mark/1.ref: 'Abdu'l-Baha on Divine Philosophy, p. 89
mark/1.bible_quote: Jesus saw Simon and Andrew casting a net...for they were fishers...Come ye after Me and I will make you to become the fishers of men.
mark/1.note: these disciples able to do what philosophers and scientists failed to accomplish through the Holy Spirit.

mark/1.ref: Kitab-i-Iqan, p. 64
mark/1.bible_quote: John did baptize in the wilderness and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
mark/1.note: Yahya, John the Baptist was Herald of the Word.

mark/1.ref: Some Answered Questions, p. 109
mark/1.bible_quote: John did baptize and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
mark/1.note: Remarks concerning baptism.

mark/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 45
mark/1.bible_quote: John did baptize in the wilderness and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
mark/1.note: Elijah came but was not recognized in John...not the entity of Elijah but his qualities that John embodied.

mark/1.ref: 'Abdu'l-Baha on Divine Philosophy, p. 122
mark/1.bible_quote: I have baptized you with water; but He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
mark/1.note: This Spirit of faith is the flame of reality, the life of humanity and the cause of eternal illumination.

mark/1.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 38
mark/1.bible_quote: I indeed have baptized you with water; but He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
mark/1.note: He who is baptized by the Holy Spirit is a Manifestation of divine mercy to mankind.

mark/1.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 438
mark/1.bible_quote: I have baptized you with water; but He shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
mark/1.note: Christ appeared in order to illumine the world of humanity...to unite hearts...Through Christ they were intended to be baptized by the Holy Spirit, attain a new spirit and realize life everlasting.

mark/1.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 53;Paris Talks, p. 59
mark/1.bible_quote: As Jesus walked by the sea He saw Simon and Andrew casting a net...for they were fishers.  And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after Me and I will make you become fishers of men.
mark/1.note: The two great Apostles, St. Peter and St. John the Evangelist were once simple humble workmen.  By the power of the Holy Spirit their souls were illumined.

mark/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
mark/1.bible_quote: Jesus saw Simon and Andrew casting a net...for they were fishers.  And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me...
mark/1.note: Baha'u'llah asked the Pope to consider how the great leader pronounced judgment against Jesus while a lowly fisherman recognized His station, and said, Take heed then, ye men of understanding!

mark/1.ref: Promulgation of Universal Peace, p. 415
mark/1.bible_quote: as He walked by the sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew his brother...they were fishers...Come ye after Me...
mark/1.note: Through the aid and assistance of God they became mighty ones of God.  Peter was transformed.

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 6-7 (*)
mark/1.bible_quote: As Jesus walked by the sea of Galilee He saw Simon and Andrew...for they were fishers.  And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after Me...
mark/1.note: Simple men filled with the Holy Spirit influenced humanity to such an extent that thousands would die to uphold their teachings...Peter had no education. He who is educated by the Holy Spirit can educate others (*Discourses in London and Paris)

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39 (*Tablet to Agnes)
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw Simon and Andrew his brother...for they were fishers...Come ye after Me...
mark/1.note: The Apostles of Christ were insignificant persons; at present consider what change has taken place.  Do not look at present condition, contemplate the future.  Bounties of Baha'u'llah shall in the future become manifest. (*Parsons,1/8/1919)

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw Simon and Andrew his brother...for they were fishers.
mark/1.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit, Peter became a great preacher.  This is what is meant by everlasting life, by baptism with the Holy Spirit and by being born again. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie Parmerton, Cincinnati)

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, XI:15, p. 258 (*)
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw Simon and Andrew...for they were fishers...cast out devils, annointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.
mark/1.note: The Apostles were fishers.  Consider to what a high station they did rise!  Their sovereignty runs to eternity. (*Tablet to Roy C. Williams [Wilhelm?])

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, XIII:7, p. 173
mark/1.bible_quote: Come unto Me and I will make you to become fishers of men.
mark/1.note: Unity and concord is their bait...service and self-sacrifice is their support...Let the Baha'is be the fishermen of the sea of spirituality.  Let them not lose their courage.

mark/1.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
mark/1.bible_quote: He saw Simon and Andrew...for they were fishers...Come ye after Me and I will make you fishers of men.
mark/1.note: The disciples were humble fishermen...All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit.

mark/1.ref: God Passes By, p. 210
mark/1.bible_quote: Come ye after Me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
mark/1.note: Baha'u'llah gave a greater promise to followers than Jesus to Peter.  He assures them that if they choose to follow Him He will make them become Quickeners of Mankind.

mark/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 124
mark/1.bible_quote: Come ye after Me and I will make you become fishers of men.
mark/1.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that while fishers of men was individual, quickeners of mankind is universal.

mark/1.ref: Star of the West, IX:10, p. 112 (*Talk, 6/13/1914)
mark/1.bible_quote: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after Me and I will make you fishers of men.
mark/1.note: Many common souls have advanced to the divine Manifestations.  Ignorant people accepted Muhammad.  However, everyone testifies that all the learned philosophers and nobles of every nation were humble in the presence of the Blessed Beauty, and great numb

mark/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
mark/1.bible_quote: Come ye after Me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
mark/1.note: In this day, however, We say, Come ye after Me that We may make you to become quickeners of mankind.

mark/1.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
mark/1.bible_quote: And the angel appeared and said unto her, The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee and the power of the Highest shall overshadow thee; therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be ca
mark/1.note: Baha'u'llah asserted that the mystery of the immaculate conception of the Virgin Mary is confessed.

mark/1.ref: Gleanings, p. 86
mark/1.bible_quote: And there came a leper to Him, beseeching Him...Jesus touched him and saith unto him...be thou clean.
mark/1.note: Through Jesus Christ the leper received healing from the leprosy of ignorance.

mark/2.bible_quote: When Jesus saw their faith, He said unto the sick of the palsy, son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
mark/2.note: Forgiveness was granted through Jesus Christ.

mark/2.bible_quote: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies?  Why can forgive sins but God only...Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed an
mark/2.note: This is the real sovereignty and such is God's power in His chosen Ones.

mark/2.ref: Star of the West, VIII:18, p. 231
mark/2.bible_quote: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins I say unto thee arise and take up thy bed and go thy way into thine house.
mark/2.note: He did not use prayers or mental suggestion.  He used spiritual authority in the command, which was the power of the Holy Spirit.  His Word was creative.

mark/2.bible_quote: went through the corn fields on the Sabbath Day; and His disciples began to pluck...Pharisees said, Behold, why do they on the Sabbath Day that which is not lawful?
mark/2.note: The Jews regarded Jesus as a breaker of the Sabbath...they did not understand the significance, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.

mark/2.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 239 (*Tablet to Mr.&)
mark/2.bible_quote: And the Pharisees said unto Him, Behold, why do they on the Sabbath day that which is not lawful?
mark/2.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath.  Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was to manifest error. Violators flatter, seduce and show insincere love.(*Mrs. MacNutt)

mark/2.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs.(*)
mark/2.bible_quote: And the Pharisees said unto Him...Why do they on the Sabbath day that which is not lawful?
mark/2.note: The Jewish clergy said, the Promised One must promote the laws of the Bible and this man broke the Sabbath.  They created doubts in the minds of the people. (*Brittingham, 1/1914)

mark/2.bible_quote: And the Pharisees said...Why do they on the Sabbath day that which is not lawful?
mark/2.note: The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.

mark/2.bible_quote: He went through the corn fields on the Sabbath day and the disciples began to pluck the ears of corn.  And the Pharisees said unto Him, Behold, why do they on the Sabbath day that which is not lawful?
mark/2.note: Because they were blindly following imitations, the Jews rejected Jesus...they refused to investigate the truth.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Manifestations change the material laws in conformance with the needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Though Jesus Christ defied the law of Moses, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Jesus Christ gave the spiritual truth regarding the Sabbath--the Jews were veiled from the truth by narrow literal interpretation.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Jesus Christ changed the laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the time.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: In the days of Christ, conditions had changed--Mosaic law was not adapted to the needs--therefore Christ abrogated the law of the Sabbath.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the laws of the Sabbath the people rose in protest against Him.

mark/2.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 49; Paris Talks, p. 55
mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: He broke the Sabbath Day.  He reformed the Law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to the Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practice led them to reject Jesus Christ.

mark/2.bible_quote: The Sabbath was made for man and not man for the Sabbath.  Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: Because of the needs and conditions of the times, laws were changed.  The Jews were unwilling to change from old forms and teachings.

mark/2.bible_quote: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
mark/2.note: The Jews were veiled from truth by literal interpretation; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.

mark/3.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 58
mark/3.bible_quote: And the Pharisees went forth and straighway took counsel with the Herodians how they might destroy Him.
mark/3.note: Analogy between the rejection of Jesus Christ and the divines' rejection of Baha'u'llah.

mark/3.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
mark/3.bible_quote: And He ordained twelve, that they should be with Him...
mark/3.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him--others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.

mark/3.bible_quote: And He ordained twelve, that they should be with Him, and that He might send them forth to preach, and to have power...
mark/3.note: Always with their teacher, the Divine Master, they learned through His teachings.

mark/3.bible_quote: And He ordained twelve, that they should be with Him, and that He might send them forth to preach...
mark/3.note: The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

mark/3.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples.
mark/3.note: Christ in reality educated only eleven men of whom one thrice denied Him.  Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls.  Then reflect what will take place in the future.

mark/3.bible_quote: Twelve disciples of Jesus Christ.
mark/3.note: Consider the days of Christ, how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts...The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.

mark/3.bible_quote: Twelve disciples...And Judas Iscariot which also betrayed Him.
mark/3.note: The superstition about the number thirteen originated in the fact that it was the thirteenth disciple who betrayed Jesus.  Superstitions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.

mark/3.bible_quote: Names of twelve disciples...And He called unto Him the twelve and began to send them forth by two and two; and gave them power...
mark/3.note: Let them that are men of action follow in their footsteps.

mark/3.bible_quote: Twelve disciples named.
mark/3.note: Only eleven began Apostles of Jesus Christ--they illumined the world...similar conditions in Paris.  Little by little the Cause grew.

mark/3.bible_quote: Twelve disciples.
mark/3.note: In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve Apostles.

mark/3.bible_quote: Names of the eleven and of Judas Iscariot, which betrayed Him.
mark/3.note: Jesus really had only eleven for Judas betrayed Him.

mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth He out devils.
mark/3.note: Although men may arise against the Kingdom, God's dominion will be set up.  Truth will prevail.

mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub...
mark/3.note: The Jews rejected Jesus and tortured Him but Christianity advanced daily.

mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes saith He hath Beelzebub and by the prince of the devils casteth He out devils.
mark/3.note: The Pharisees called Jesus Beelzebub.  The deriding enemy addeth joy to the heart--becometh means of spirituality.

mark/3.ref: Utterance in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs.(*)
mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes said, He hath Beelzebub...
mark/3.note: When Jesus came the Jews called Him Beelzebub.  I have always said that the believers will be blamed for this Cause.  The Jews proved from the Bible that Jesus was a false Prophet--It is always so. (*Brittingham, 1/1914, Haifa)

mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes which came down from Jersulem said, He hath Beelzebub...
mark/3.note: Moses announced the coming of Christ.  The Jews were blind to Reality.  The real Christ Spirit has come again from the supreme apex in Baha'u'llah to illumine the world.

mark/3.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 241 (*)
mark/3.bible_quote: The scribes said, He hath Beelzebub...
mark/3.note: Cause of Christ had no importance then.  Analogy regarding the Covenant which is not yet known befittingly. (*Tablet to Roy C. Wilhelm)

mark/3.ref: Star of the West, XXV:?, p. 258
mark/3.bible_quote: And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub.
mark/3.note: Christ in His Day was called Satan, today He is as the Word of God.

mark/3.bible_quote: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is My brother, My sister and mother.
mark/3.note: You are My spiritual children; spiritual children are dearer than physical children--you are most beloved.

mark/3.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 47
mark/3.bible_quote: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is My brother, My sister and My mother.
mark/3.note: Spiritual relationship is the true familyhood of God's children.

mark/3.bible_quote: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is My brother, and My sister and My mother.
mark/3.note: Christ said His brothers were those who believed in God...

mark/3.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 24
mark/3.bible_quote: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is My brother, My sister and My mother.
mark/3.note: This Holiness the Christ said that His brothers were those who believed in God, and refused to associate with His own brothers.  It is impossible for a man to associate with a leper and not be infected.

mark/4.bible_quote: Parable of the sower...And these are they that are sown upon good ground such as hear the Word and receive it and bring forth fruit...
mark/4.note: Attainment is dependent upon capacity.  Unless capacity is developed the summons of the Kingdom cannot reach the ear, the light of the Sun of truth will not be observed...

mark/4.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
mark/4.bible_quote: Parable of the sower and the tares.
mark/4.note: The seed contained the Truth.  Some behold in the seed only a hard substance while others see in it the life principle.

mark/4.ref: Star of the West, XII:10, p. 182
mark/4.bible_quote: Parable of the sower and the tares.
mark/4.note: Some have no capacity; others hear and understand but forget; others have great capacity and live accordingly.

mark/4.bible_quote: That seeing they may see and not perceive; and hearing they may hear and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted.
mark/4.note: In all Scriptures the sentence of want of understanding hath been pronounced against those whose hearts have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

mark/4.ref: Flower...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 37 (*)
mark/4.bible_quote: That seeing they may see and not perceive and hearing they may hear and not understand.
mark/4.note: The Kingdom is like a spirit--the second birth will be produced, but other souls do not comprehend. (*Tablet, translated 11/12/1908)

mark/4.bible_quote: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand.
mark/4.note: Bounties of Christ transformed eye, ear and made the heart tender.  We must investigate the foundation of religion--spread its message.

mark/4.bible_quote: And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel?
mark/4.note: Be upon your guard in order that none assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llah's Cause.

mark/4.bible_quote: And He said unto them, Why are ye so fearful?  How is it that ye have no faith?
mark/4.note: The disciples of Christ were physically weak and apparently vanquished at first--yet in the end they were victorious.

mark/4.bible_quote: Parable of the sower and the tares.
mark/4.note: Symbolic of the seed of life which needs experiences.

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, VII:13, p. 122 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: And whosoever shall not receive you...when ye depart from that place, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them.
mark/6.note: Man must live in the utmost sanctity and purity and he must ever think to assist others and not beg assistance from them.  (*Talk in Haifa, 10/15/1914)

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, XI:2, p. 34 (*Unveiling the)
mark/6.bible_quote: And whosoever shall not receive you nor hear you, when ye depart shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them.
mark/6.note: Teachers must travel to all parts of the world like 'Abdu'l-Baha.  He was sanctified and free from every attachment. (*Divine Plan for the World)

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: And whosoever shall not receive you nor hear you, when ye depart, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them.
mark/6.note: The Covenant is like unto the ocean which preserves Baha'i unity; and these souls are like unto the foam upon it...I acted in accordance with Christ's admonition. (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: And they went out and preached...cast out devils and annointed with oil many that were sick and healed them...
mark/6.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps. (*Tablet called Trumpet call to the East and West)

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, XX:10, p. 299
mark/6.bible_quote: And they cast out many devils and annointed with oil many that were sick and healed them.
mark/6.note: Those who say they are healing through the power of God should accept no pay, for they are dispensers of God's bounty--channels of His grace.

mark/6.bible_quote: others said this it is Elias.
mark/6.note: Explanation of the return of the perfections rather than the person of Elias.

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 122 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: The king sent an executioner and commanded [John's] head to be brought in.
mark/6.note: Review of Jewish history, ending with the execution of John the Baptist. (*Talk with rabbi)

mark/6.bible_quote: And He called unto Him the twelve...
mark/6.note: The Bab admonished the Letters of the Living to ponder the words of Jesus as He sent them forth to propagate the Cause of God.  He bade them fulfill their mission.

mark/6.bible_quote: And He called unto Him the twelve, and began to send them forth two by two...
mark/6.note: Analogy to the Bab's sending forth of the Letters of the Living...He recalled the words addressed by Jesus to His disciples and emphasized the superlative greatness of the new Day.

mark/6.bible_quote: And when ye depart, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them.
And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them...
mark/6.note: They should seek no reward, freely they should give...utter with eloquence...guide the people to the manifest light, in great joy.

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: And when He had taken the five loaves and two fishes, He looked up to heaven and blessed and brake the loaves...And He took up twelve basketsful of fragments.
mark/6.note: The miracle and symbolism of the loaves and fishes explained. (*Talk with Mrs. von Lilienthal and Mrs. Beede)

mark/6.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 34
mark/6.bible_quote: And when He had taken the five loaves and two fishes, He looked up to heaven and blessed and brake them...He took up twelve basketsful of fragments.
mark/6.note: Loaves are the five words of Jesus which satisfied the multitude, the fish represents spiritual meaning, and basketsful the superabundance of this teaching for others to share.

mark/6.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
mark/6.bible_quote: Whosoever shall not receive you or hear your words... shake off the dust of your feet...
mark/6.note: I acted in accordance with the admonition of Christ...notwithstanding that unworthy behavior I was still patient...I paid their expenses, showed them the utmost consideration... (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the Commandment of God, that ye may keep your own traditions...making the Word of God of none effect through your tradition.
mark/7.note: I hope that it is proven to you that Moses had no better friend than His Holiness Christ.

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God ye hold the tradition of men...
mark/7.note: Jesus spread the name of Moses throughout the Orient and Occident.  We must investigate reality--not accept ancestral imitations.

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the Commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...full well ye reject the commandment of God.
mark/7.note: The Jews were rebuked by Jesus for observing the letter and rejecting the Revelation of God.  Jesus extolled Moses.  Manifestations testify to the oneness of the Prophets.

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men.  Full well ye reject the commandment of God.
mark/7.note: Christ ratified and proclaimed the Law of Moses.  All Prophets have revoiced that same foundation of reality.

mark/7.ref: Star of the West, IV:3, p. 54 (*)
mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition...making the Word of God of none effect through your tradition.
mark/7.note: Rebuke of Christ...He promulgated the ten commandments. (*Answers to theologians in Paris)

mark/7.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups...
mark/7.note: when the Jews became fettered by empty forms and imitations, the wrath of God became manifest.

mark/7.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 3-4
mark/7.bible_quote: for laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...
mark/7.note: Jesus Christ revoiced and confirmed the essential teachings of Moses...ratified and proclaimed the foundation of the law of Moses.

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...full well ye reject the commandment of God.
mark/7.note: The greatest friend of Moses was Jesus, but the Jews felt He was taking away some of Moses' glory.  They were veiled from the truth and kept away from His bounties.

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...Full well ye reject the commandment of God that ye may keep your own tradition.
mark/7.note: Jesus renewed the commandments Moses had received from Jehovah.  He said the commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

mark/7.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...Full well ye reject the commandment of God...
mark/7.note: When Jesus Christ appeared amongst the Jews, the first thing He did was to proclaim the validity of the Mosaic Mission.  He declared that the Torah, the Old Testament was the Word of God. (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729)

mark/7.bible_quote: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men...making the Word of God of none effect through your tradition...
mark/7.note: Jesus rebuked the Jews for their disobediance to the spirit of the commandments--observing requirements only.

mark/7.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 4
mark/7.bible_quote: And He said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God that ye may keep your own tradition.
mark/7.note: Jesus Christ proclaimed and ratified the prophethood of Moses.

mark/7.bible_quote: Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own traditions.
mark/7.note: Jesus Christ declares Moses to have been a Prophet of God.

mark/7.bible_quote: Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
mark/7.note: Christ caused the name and prestige of Moses to become widespread.  Through Christ Moses was proclaimed a Prophet of God.

mark/7.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 103
mark/7.bible_quote: For this saying go thy way; the devil has gone out of thy daughter.
mark/7.note: The effect of a Manifestation is to drive out all that is evil and replace the natural growth of virtues.

mark/8.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit to Acca, p. 18
mark/8.bible_quote: Having eyes, see ye not?  And having ears, hear ye not?
mark/8.note: Because of their spiritual deadness they do not understand.  Miracles were spiritual and not physical.

mark/8.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27 (*)
mark/8.bible_quote: Whosoever will come after Me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.
mark/8.note: No one is able to follow Christ entirely except he give up all material things, such as honor, wealth... (*Talk by Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908, Acca)

mark/8.bible_quote: He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: The land of Syria hath been honored by the footsteps of its Lord.  The Scriptures have been fulfilled.

mark/8.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
mark/8.bible_quote: also shall the Son of man be ashamed when He cometh in the glory of the Father.
mark/8.note: We have said "He Who is the Father is come...Blessed is he that draweth nigh unto Him and woe betide them that are far away."

mark/8.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: Every Prophet hath announced the coming of this Day; allusions were made to it in all the sacred Scriptures.

mark/8.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 18 (*)
mark/8.bible_quote: He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: All are invited to partake of the fruit of the Kingdom of the Father for He discloses mysteries of the teachings of Baha'u'llah. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar in Acca, 11/10/1908)

mark/8.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 19 (*)
mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: Jesus Christ did not claim to have come in that station but that He would return in the glory of the Father.  This glory was to be the unity of mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, Acca, 11/10/1908)

mark/8.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 32(*)
mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: Baha'u'llah is the trunk, the branches, the root--all except the fruit, which is 'Abdu'l-Baha.  The teachings of Baha'u'llah have been spread throughout the world. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, Acca, 11/12/1908)

mark/8.bible_quote: He cometh in the glory of the Father...
mark/8.note: Fulfilled in the coming of Baha'u'llah and attested to by His Name.

mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father with the holy angels...
mark/8.note: Jesus referred to Baha'u'llah as the One Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne.

mark/8.bible_quote: He cometh in the glory of His Father...
mark/8.note: In Baghdad the Tabernacle of the Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long awaited Kingdom of the Father unassailably established.

mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father...
mark/8.note: Jesus Christ, returned in the glory of the Father, was about to ascend His throne.

mark/8.bible_quote: when He shall come in the glory of His Father with the holy angels...
mark/8.note: Baha'u'llah announced the advent of One with whose name the Evangel was adorned.

mark/8.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
mark/8.bible_quote: He cometh in the glory of His Father...
mark/8.note: Baha'u'llah asserted the clergy interposed between the flock and the returned Christ who came in the glory of the Father.

mark/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father.
mark/8.note: These tidings are the same in all the Holy Books expressed in different ways.  Two successive Manifestations, Elias and Christ, the Bab and Baha'u'llah.

mark/8.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 137-38
mark/8.bible_quote: when He cometh in the glory of His Father...
mark/8.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha is not a Manifestation of God; He is not to be acclaimed the "Return of Christ", the Son Who will come in the glory of His Father.  He reflects even as a clear Mirror the rays of Baha'u'llah's glory...

mark/9.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 50
mark/9.bible_quote: And He was transfigured before them.  And His raiment became shining...white as snow...
mark/9.note: Baha'u'llah declared the divines did not know the station of Jesus Christ.

mark/9.bible_quote: and He was transfigured before them and His raiment became shining...
mark/9.note: The faithful are ever sustained by the presence of the Supreme Concourse.

mark/9.bible_quote: and He was transfigured before them...His raiment became shinig, exceeding white as snow...And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses...
mark/9.note: This was a spiritual condition expressed as a physical meeting...

mark/9.bible_quote: And a voice came out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son; hear ye Him.
mark/9.note: God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

mark/9.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
mark/9.bible_quote: and a voice came out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son; hear ye Him.
mark/9.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ asserted by Baha'u'llah.

mark/9.bible_quote: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them; and a voice came out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son; hear ye Him.
mark/9.note: meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.  Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ.

mark/9.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32]
mark/9.bible_quote: and a voice came out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son; hear ye Him.
mark/9.note: The Proof of the Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself.  Jesus was born in a manger but He changed the whole world by His power and Divinity.

mark/9.bible_quote: He charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen.
mark/9.note: If you give the same teachings to all some might be retarded by them instead of helped.

mark/9.bible_quote: Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?  And he answered, Elias verily cometh first...But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come...
mark/9.note: This is as regards its virtues and not as regards identity.  This is our view of reincarnation.

mark/9.ref: Star of the West, IX:?, p. 100 (*)
mark/9.bible_quote: and Jesus sat down and called the twelve and said unto the, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all and servant of all.
mark/9.note: The humbler a man is in the path of God, the more exalted is he; the greater his meekness and submissiveness, the more beloved is he.  (*Talk to believers and students, 7/1914)

mark/10.ref: Mary Lucas, Visit at Acca, p. 31
mark/10.bible_quote: And the Pharisees asked...Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife?
mark/10.note: Jesus Christ taught truth concerning divorce.  He abrogated the law of Moses.  Social laws change according to the needs of the time and conditions.  True religion is always one.  The spirit of religion is unchangeable.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: Material laws were changed by the Manifestations according to the needs and conditions of the times.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of divorce, the people rose in protest against Him.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: Material laws were changed by the Manifestations according to the needs and conditions of the times.

mark/10.bible_quote: For the hardness of your hearts he wrote you this precept.  But from the creation God made them male and female...For this cause shall man cleave to his wife.
mark/10.note: Law of Moses--divorce--changed because of needs and condition s of the times.  In the time of Jesus this law was the cause of corruption.  Their spiritual blindness led them to adherence to old forms.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: The exigency which existed in the time of Moses did not exist, therefore Christ abrogated this law of divorce.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: In the days of Christ conditions had changed--Mosaic Law not adapted to the needs--therefore Christ abrogated it.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept...What God hath joined together let no man put asunder.
mark/10.note: This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these lawswere not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...What therefore God hath joined together let no man put asunder.
mark/10.note: Jesus Christ annulled the law of divorce.  The Jews still waitng for the Manifestation.

mark/10.bible_quote: For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept...the creation God made them male and female.  For this cause shall man man cleave to his wife, and they twain shall be one flesh.
mark/10.note: In the time of Moses divorce was conformable to the needs and conditions but in the time of Jesus it was the cause of corruptiion, therefore He abrogated that law.

mark/10.bible_quote: Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement...For the hardness of your heart He wrote you this precept.
mark/10.note: Manifestations change outer laws according to the exigency of time, place and conditions.

mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: The true Baha'i loves the children.  A simple pure heart is near to God.

mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: Blessed are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such are the Kingdom of heaven...hearts are pure, we must become like them.

mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: As long as you are the children of the Kingdom, no matter what language you use, it is sweet.

mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: You are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such is the kingdom of God, and according to the word of Baha'u'llah, you are the lamps of humanity.  You are near the Source.

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 155 (*)
mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of heaven.
mark/10.note: children are tender plants, their hearts are clear and transparent. (*Letters recording talks in Stuttgart and Esslingen, Germany)

mark/10.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto me...for of such is the Kingdom of God...Whosoever shall not receive the Kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
mark/10.note: Daily grow in faith and assurance and develop Baha'i attributes, such as obediance and kindness.

mark/10.bible_quote: Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?  And Jesus said, Thou knowest the commandments...
mark/10.note: Jesus Christ was instrumental in publishing the name and greatness of the Israelitish prophets, and proved to the world that the Israelites constituted the people of God.  We have stated the valid proofs of the Prophethood of Moses.

mark/10.bible_quote: How hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: The true seeker will gladly renounce worldly desires and possessions if they interfere with spiritual growth.

mark/10.bible_quote: How hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: Earth's happiness does not depend upon wealth.  The rich depend on riches whereas the poor are dependent on God.

mark/10.bible_quote: How hard it is for them that trust in riches to enter the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: Blessed are the poor.  Christ said so.  Baha'u'llah said so.  We must be free from the insatiable desire for riches.  Poor are blessed.

mark/10.bible_quote: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: Jesus said, Blessed are the poor. I love you all; you are children of the Kingdom and are accepted of God.

mark/10.bible_quote: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
mark/10.note: one's heart must not be attached to the things of this world.

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100 (*)
mark/10.bible_quote: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.
mark/10.note: Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God...of the world of humanity.  The honor of man depends upon this. (*Talk with students, DMAS, 7/15/1914)

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, XIII:5, p. 104 (*)
mark/10.bible_quote: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be the servant of all.
mark/10.note: In the path of God humility is honor.  Whoever is the real servant of the believers of God is their helper.  (*Talk given to students on Mt. Carmel)

mark/10.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
mark/10.bible_quote: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many...
mark/10.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed then obeyed, they found deliverance from sin.

mark/10.bible_quote: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister and to give His life a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: When Christ appeared...He invited men to eternal life, the Light of Guidance was shed forth by that radiant Star and He at length gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.

mark/10.bible_quote: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from physical imperfections...to give the spirit of life...

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
mark/10.bible_quote: Even as the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life as a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: All the Prophets appeared that the oneness of man might be taught.  How much suffering these Prophets had to endure to unfold this illumination.  His Holiness offered His life. (*Talk in Stuttgart, 4/6/1913)

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (*)
mark/10.bible_quote: For even as the son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give His life a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: Let us have the life of the spirit...of sympathy, practical cooperation, celestial brotherhood... (*Talk, 5/1914)

mark/10.bible_quote: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: It is for this purpose that the Manifestations unveil themselves, bear every ordeal to make ready soul's dawning of light.  This is the real atonement of Christ.  He sacrificed Himself for the world.

mark/10.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 632
mark/10.bible_quote: Even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many.
mark/10.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from the imperfections of the physical nature...

mark/11.bible_quote: And when they came nigh Jerusalem, He sendeth forth two of His disciples.
mark/11.note: Jesus manifested Himself in Jerusalem and called men to the Kingdom of God.

mark/11.bible_quote: And they brought the colt to Jesus...and He sat upon him.
mark/11.note: Literal interpretation veiled Jesus from the Jews, even though they had been waiting for the Messiah.

mark/11.bible_quote: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him...
mark/11.note: God, however, aided Him and protected Him.  The Lord preserveth whom He willeth.

mark/11.bible_quote: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him...
mark/11.note: The enemies of His Holiness Christ were the Jews, the Pharisees but the enemies of Baha'u'llah were kings representing 15,000,000 people.  At last His banner was upraised...

mark/11.ref: Utterances in letter of Mrs. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
mark/11.bible_quote: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him...
mark/11.note: In the time of Jesus the Jewish clergy created so many doubts that there were very few who believed--in reality there were only twelve and one of those denied Him.  Why? Because tests were very great at that time. (*Brittingham, 1/1914)

mark/12.bible_quote: Parable of the husbandman and the Lord of the vineyard.
mark/12.note: Illustration of sedition and Covenant-breaking.  Covenant-breakers are execrated and condemned.

mark/12.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 110
mark/12.bible_quote: Parable of the husbandman and the Lord of the vineyard.
mark/12.note: Baha'u'llah came in the station of the Father, fulfilling this prophecy.

mark/12.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21
mark/12.bible_quote: Parable of the husbandman and the Lord of the vineyard.
mark/12.note: In the holy Writings of His Holiness Baha'u'llah in a thousand places at least, the violators of the Covenant are execrated and condemned.

mark/12.bible_quote: What shall the Lord of the vineyard do?  He will come and destroy the husbandman.
mark/12.note: Baha'u'llah came in the station and glory of the Father and the Lord of the vineyard.

mark/12.bible_quote: What shall therefore the Lord of the vineyard do? ...He will come and destroy the husbandman.
mark/12.note: Warning to evil doers about Baha'u'llah.

mark/12.bible_quote: What shall therefore the Lord of the vineyard do?  He will come and destroy the husbandman...
mark/12.note: Baha'u'llah identifies Himself with the Promised Father...

mark/12.bible_quote: The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner.
mark/12.note: This is the experience of the Manifestations.

mark/12.bible_quote: And they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Jesus in His words.
mark/12.note: Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees disputed with Jesus.  He Who is the Lord of Lords is come overshadowed with clouds.  He verily hath come down again.

mark/12.bible_quote: and they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: Certain persons, like the Pharisees...will exert themselves to deprive souls of the glad-tidings of the Holy Spirit.

mark/12.bible_quote: And they sent Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: The Jews expected the Messiah but when He came they rejected Him--they were bereft of sight...Their eyes were covered with veils of traditions and names.

mark/12.bible_quote: And they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: Baha'u'llah warns the Pope not to dispute with Him as did the Pharisees with Jesus.

mark/12.bible_quote: And they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: The Jews rejected Jesus; they were blind to the Reality of Christ.  Literal interpretation of the prophecies veiled them.

mark/12.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 43
mark/12.bible_quote: They sought to lay hold on Him...and they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: They held strictly to the law, there were the causes of the condemnation and crucifixion of Jesus.

mark/12.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*Bahai)
mark/12.bible_quote: They sought to lay hold on Him...and they sent unto Him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to catch Him in His words.
mark/12.note: The Jews cried, O God, hasten the day when the Promised One will manifest Himself.  He claims to be from Nazareth. He was to come with a sword; sit on the throne of David; conquer the East and West;teach the law of Moses...(*Scriptures,p.729-30)

mark/12.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 73
mark/12.bible_quote: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? ...Render unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's and to God the things that are God's.
mark/12.note: Baha'u'llah explained that to obey rulers is to obey God.

mark/12.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 81, 89
mark/12.bible_quote: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar? ...Render unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.
mark/12.note: To obey rulers is to obey God.

mark/12.bible_quote: Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's and to God the things that are God's.
mark/12.note: Live so that the world may see you loyal to God and to your just government.  This is justice.

mark/12.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 7 (*)
mark/12.bible_quote: Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's and to God the things that are God's.
mark/12.note: Religious interests should not be brought into politics.  Reliigions should treat of morals.  Politics of material circumstances. (*Addresses in Paris and London)

mark/12.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 41
mark/12.bible_quote: have ye not read in the book of Moses how in the bush God spake to Him?
mark/12.note: Comparison betwee the response to God's Call, and rejection by the divines of Baha'u'llah.

mark/12.bible_quote: have ye not read in the book of Moses how in the bush God spake unto Him...
mark/12.note: The Call of Baha'u'llah surpassed that of Moses from the Burning Bush.

mark/12.bible_quote: have ye not read in the book of Moses how in the bush God spake to Him?
mark/12.note: Under such appalling circumstances the Most Great Spirit, symbolized in the Zoroastrian, the Mosaic, the Christian and Muhammadan Dispensations by the Sacred Fire, the Burning Bush, the Dove, and the angel Gabriel...

mark/12.bible_quote: have ye not read in the book of Moses how in the bush God spake unto Him...
mark/12.note: The experience of Moses in receiving the Call of God from the Burning Bush symbolic of the Annunciation of the angel to Baha'u'llah regarding His Mission.

mark/12.bible_quote: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength; this is the first commandment.
mark/12.note: This is the original and principle teaching that Jesus Christ spread in the world.

mark/12.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
mark/12.bible_quote: Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.  There is none other commandment greater than these.
mark/12.note: All the Prophets were sent, all the books were revealed, so that the law of love might be promoted.

mark/13.bible_quote: As He went out of the Temple...said unto them, See thou these great buildings?  There shall not be left one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down.
mark/13.note: We see in these days the fulfilment of divine prophecy.  Jerusalem has disappeared...now it is rebuilt.

mark/13.bible_quote: As they sat upon the Mount of Olives, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked Him...Tell us when shall these things be?  And what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?
mark/13.note: Every time He made reply, He set forth a special sign that should herald the promised Dispensation.

mark/13.bible_quote: Tell us what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?
mark/13.note: men will lament the loss of the Sun of divine beauty, the Moon of knowledge, the stars of divine wisdom.  Thereupon they will behold the countenance of the Promised One...

mark/13.bible_quote: wars...nation shall rise against nation...earthquakes...famines... troubles--these are the beginnings of sorrows...
mark/13.note: The revelation of Jesus to His disciples analogous to the statement "commotions shall appear and the voice of lamentation shall be raised and evidences of mischief shall be revealed."

mark/13.ref: Wisdom of 'Abdu'l-Baha, p. 44; Paris Talks, p. 49
mark/13.bible_quote: for they shall deliver you up to councils and in synagogues ye shall be beaten.
mark/13.note: Apostles of Christ steadfastly bore their trials and sufferings with endurance and faithfulness.  Those who suffer most attain greatest perfection.

mark/13.ref: Flowers...from the Rose Garden at Acca, p. 5
mark/13.bible_quote: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the Prophet, standing where it ought not...for in those days shall be affliction...
mark/13.note: Jesus referred to the time when the abomination of desolation shall take place.

mark/13.bible_quote: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the Prophet...
mark/13.note: Prophesied by Daniel and confirmed by Jesus Christ accomplished by the coming of the Bab.

mark/13.bible_quote: And when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the Prophet...
mark/13.note: This ended with the coming of the Bab.

mark/13.bible_quote: for they shall deliver you up to councils and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten.
mark/13.note: The Apostles when they followed Jesus Christ...their ignorance gave place to understanding...they became spiritual...they became sons of God...they became saints.  Pray to God that you may become as angels and beacons of light.

mark/13.bible_quote: the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall from heaven...
mark/13.note: By the sun in one sense is meant the Suns of Truth Who fill the world with a liberal effusion of grace from on high...

mark/13.bible_quote: after that tribulation the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light...and the stars of heaven shall fall...
mark/13.note: Hence it is manifest that by the words "the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light" is intended the waywardness of the divines and the annulment of laws established by divine Revelation...

mark/13.bible_quote: after that tribulation the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light.  And the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.
mark/13.note: Explanation of the sun, moon, stars and the tribulation.

mark/13.bible_quote: the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light. And the stars of heaven shall fall...then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds...
mark/13.note: Christians are expecting the stars to fall and Christ to appear in the clouds, yet these are but symbols.  Jews awaiting Christ from a heaven which does not exist.

mark/13.bible_quote: In those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light.  And the stars of heaven shall fall and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.  And the
mark/13.note: His coming depends upon the fulfilment of certain signs...

mark/13.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
mark/13.bible_quote: and the stars shall fall and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken...
mark/13.note: Science proves that all the stars are much larger than the earth.  The Kingdom of heaven is within your soul.

mark/13.bible_quote: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.
mark/13.note: The stars of heaven of knowledge have fallen--they adduced the proofs in order to demonstrate the truth of Baha'u'llah's Cause.

mark/13.bible_quote: And the stars of heaven shall fall and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken...
mark/13.note: Baha'u'llah proclaims the fall of the stars of the firmament who have turned aside from Him.

mark/13.bible_quote: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
mark/13.note: He Who is the Lord of Lords is come overshadowed with clouds and the decree hath been fulfilled.
