luke/5.note: Baha'u'llah reminded Christians of the response to Jesus' call; and He called them to become the quickeners of mankind.

luke/5.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 124
luke/5.bible_quote: And He said...Follow Me...
luke/5.note: Baha'u'llah asserts, fishers of men is personal, quickeners of mankind is universal.

luke/5.ref: TAB:II, p. 341
luke/5.bible_quote: go and show thyself to the priest and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded for a testimony unto them.
luke/5.note: The greatest friend of Moses was Jesus but the Jews felt He was taking away some of Moses' glory.  They were veiled from the truth and kept away from His bounties.

luke/6.bible_quote: on the Sabbath...His disciples plucked ears of corn...And certain Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath days?...That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabb
luke/6.note: Manifestations changed some of the laws of the Jews, which they called the Law of Moses...Jesus changed laws regarding the Sabbath because of the needs and conditions of the times...therefore they turned away from Him.

luke/6.ref: Utterances in letter from H.E. Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: on the second Sabbath he went through the corn fields...certain Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?
luke/6.note: The Jewish clergy said, The Promised One must promote the laws of the Bible and this man broke the Sabbath.  The clergy created doubts in the minds of the people. (* I.D. Brittingham, Haifa 1/1914, translated by Monever Khanum)

luke/6.bible_quote: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath days?
luke/6.note: The Jews said the Messiah was to spread the law of Moses, but Jesus seeks to destroy it.  They were blind to Reality.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 239 (*Tablet to)
luke/6.bible_quote: And certain of the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath days?
luke/6.note: They declared that Christ was a breaker of the Sabbath. Although on the surface they seemed to defend the Cause of God, yet inwardly their purpose was manifest error.  Violators...show insincere love. (*Mr. & Mrs. Howard MacNutt)

luke/6.bible_quote: the Pharisees said unto them, Why do ye that which it is not lawful to do on the Sabbath days? ...That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: The Jews believed the Messiah would promulgate all the laws of Moses; when Jesus abrogated the law of the Sabbath the people rose against Him.

luke/6.bible_quote: the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: Manifestations change the material laws in conformity with the needs and conditions of the times.  Jews were veiled from reality by ancestral traditions.

luke/6.bible_quote: the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: Jesus changed laws in regard to the Sabbath.  This was according to the spirit of the age, but as these laws were not expedient in the time of Christ, they were abrogated.

luke/6.bible_quote: And He said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: He reformed the law of Moses, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

luke/6.bible_quote: And he said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: Though Jesus Christ abrogated the law of Moses concerning the Sabbath, it did not prevent the spread of the Gospel.

luke/6.bible_quote: the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: Jesus Christ gave the spiritual truth regarding the Sabbath...the Jews were veiled from the truth by narrow literal interpretations.

luke/6.bible_quote: And He said unto them, That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: In the days of Jesus, conditions had changed, Mosaic Laws not adapted to the needs and conditions, therefore He abrogated the Law concerning the Sabbath.

luke/6.ref: Visit at Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 31
luke/6.bible_quote: the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath.
luke/6.note: The Sabbath of the Jews was changed by Christ.  The outward rules may be changed but the reality is unchanging.

luke/6.bible_quote: That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath...Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good?
luke/6.note: Jews were veiled from truth by literal interpretations; therefore they rejected Jesus Christ.

luke/6.bible_quote: That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath...Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good?
luke/6.note: Because Jesus Christ did not observe the Sabbath according to the Scriptures, they rejected Him.  Literal interpretation and narrow practices led them to reject Jesus Christ.

luke/6.bible_quote: That the Son of man is Lord also of the Sabbath...Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good?
luke/6.note: His Holiness Christ proclaimed and completed the Law of Moses.  He abolished certain laws which were not in accord with needs and conditions of the times.  Jews rejected Jesus because of adherence to ancient forms.

luke/6.bible_quote: And the scribes and Pharisees watched Him, whether He would heal on the Sabbath day...Is it lawful on the Sabbath days to do good?
luke/6.note: The Jews did not understand, therefore they were debarred from perceiving the truth of Christ.

luke/6.bible_quote: Names of the eleven faithful disciples...And Judas Iscariot which also was the traitor.
luke/6.note: Jesus Christ really had only eleven disciples as Judas was evil and betrayed Him.

luke/6.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples.
luke/6.note: Only eleven became apostles of Jesus Christ; they illumined the world.  Similar conditions in Paris...little by little the Cause grew.

luke/6.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples of Christ.
luke/6.note: Consider the days of Christ, how the light of guidance brightened twelve hearts.  The lamp of guidance has been lighted in your hearts.

luke/6.bible_quote: And when it was day, He called unto Him His disciples; and of them He chose twelve whom also He named apostles.
luke/6.note: The disciples were watching and observing their Master's conduct, followed His example and after His ascension scattered to various regions.  In the same way we must follow Baha'u'llah.

luke/6.bible_quote: He called unto Him His disciples, and of them He chose twelve, whom also He named apostles.
luke/6.note: The product of the life of Christ was the illumined souls of those who believed in Him.

luke/6.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples.
luke/6.note: Christ in reality educated only eleven men, of whom one thrice denied Him.  The Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands of souls.  Then reflect what will take place in the future.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: He called upon His disciples...and Judas Iscariot.
luke/6.note: Judas began well but failed in the end.  Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants.  It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

luke/6.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
luke/6.bible_quote: He called unto Him His disciples; and of them He chose twelve, whom also He named apostles.
luke/6.note: Twelve believed in Him; one deserted Him, others were not strong.  God will assist all who are firm in His Cause.

luke/6.bible_quote: Names of the twelve disciples.
luke/6.note: In each cycle the guardians or holy souls have been twelve.  So in the time of Christ there were twelve Apostles.

luke/6.bible_quote: And Judas Iscariot which also was the traitor.
luke/6.note: The superstition about number thirteen originated in the fact that it was the thirteenth disciple, Judas, who betrayed Jesus.  Suppositions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.

luke/6.bible_quote: Blessed be ye poor: for your's is the kingdom of God.
luke/6.note: Earth's happiness does not depend upon wealth.  The poor depend on God whereas the rich depend on riches.

luke/6.bible_quote: Blessed be ye poor, for your's is the kingdom of God.
luke/6.note: Christ said so; Baha'u'llah said so...We must be free from the inestimable desire for riches.  The poor are blessed.

luke/6.bible_quote: Blessed be ye poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
luke/6.note: The poor are especialy beloved of God.  No deed is greater than helping the poor.  Poverty is a stimulus towards God.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, XXI:12, p. 354
luke/6.bible_quote: Blessed be ye poor, for your's is the kingdom of God.
luke/6.note: The poor are especially beloved of God.  No greater deed before God than helping the poor.  Poverty is a stimulus towards God.

luke/6.bible_quote: When He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels...
luke/6.note: Jesus referred to Baha'u'llah as the One Who shall come with all His holy angels about Him and all nations gathered before His throne.

luke/6.bible_quote: But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies...
luke/6.note: Hatred and persecution cannot be reconciled with Christ's teachings.

luke/6.bible_quote: But I say unto you which hear, Love your enemies.
luke/6.note: The stern old law...changed into one of love by the Manifestations.

luke/6.bible_quote: Love your enemies...pray for them which spitefully use you.
luke/6.note: Baha'u'llah has revoiced and re-established the quintescence of the teachings of all the Prophets.

luke/6.bible_quote: love your enemies; do good to them which hate you; Bless them that curse you and pray for them which despitefully use you.
luke/6.note: Consider how much the principles of the religion of Christ have been forgotten...and many heresies appeared.

luke/6.bible_quote: Love your enemies, do good to them that hate you...
luke/6.note: Explanation of the justice and mercy of God.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, IV:6, p. 104 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you; bless them that curse you and pray for them which despitefully use you.
luke/6.note: Welcome persecution, hostility and bitterness as the means of testing and increasing your supreme faith in God. (*Words in America)

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: Love your enemies, do good to them which hate you; bless them that curse you and pray for them which despitefully use you.
luke/6.note: The Baha'is must fulfill to the letter the requirements of these holy principles. (*DMAS, 5/23/1914)

luke/6.bible_quote: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other...
luke/6.note: allegiance to the Word of God can alone establish unity and love...

luke/6.bible_quote: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek, offer also the other...
luke/6.note: Explanation of the justice and mercy of God.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, IV:13, p. 54 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek, offer also the other...
luke/6.note: No connection between the holy teachings and war. (*Answers to questions from theologians in Paris)

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, V:8, p. 116
luke/6.bible_quote: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek, offer also the other...
luke/6.note: Remarks about disarmament and Universal Peace.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, VI:?, p. 61 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: And unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek, offer also the other...
luke/6.note: All religions have two departments--spiritual and material.  The investigation of truth... (*Address)

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, II:2, p. 7
luke/6.bible_quote: As ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
luke/6.note: Each one can choose between justice and injustice.  Be just.  Consider the rights of others before your own.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, VII:17, p. 171
luke/6.bible_quote: Why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brother's eye but perceiveth not the beam that is in thine own eye?  Thou hypocrite, cast out first the beam that is in thine own eye...
luke/6.note: Admonition regarding love.  Love melts all opposition, conquers all foes, sweeps away all barriers; a love that triumphs over all obstacles.

luke/6.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 20 (*)
luke/6.bible_quote: For every tree is known by his own fruit.  For of thorns men do not gather figs...
luke/6.note: Each Prophet left after Him some sign by which He meant some significance.  In this day we are commanded to love mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar on 11/10/1908 in Acca)

luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
luke/6.note: God requires each person to produce something towards the building of the Kingdom of Heaven on earth.  Trees that yield no fruit have been and will ever be for the fire.

luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.  For every tree is known by his own fruit...
luke/6.note: His Holiness Jesus Christ summoned all to the Most Great Peace through the acquisition of pure morals.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, VIII:6, p. 68
luke/6.bible_quote: For every tree is known by his own fruit...A good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good.
luke/6.note: By belief we mean that the reality of man will be characterized by divine characteristics.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, XV:6, p. 163
luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
luke/6.note: He will seek to be one of the Kingdom...until this station be attained by man his life will be utterly devoid of results.

luke/6.ref: Star of the West, XV:8, p. 229
luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
luke/6.note: If a believer shows divine qualities we know the true Faith is in his heart.  Three essentials of faith: to confess with the tongue, to believe in the heart, to give evidence in our actions.

luke/6.bible_quote: For every tree is known by his own fruit...
luke/6.note: Every soul is known by his conduct.  Strive to develop a beatific character.

luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit...for every tree is known by his own fruit...
luke/6.note: Therefore we must strive with life and heart that our deeds may be better...cultivate love for all the world.

luke/6.bible_quote: For a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit; neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
luke/6.note: Thou has said aright that verily 'Abdu'l-Baha looketh to deeds and not to words, even as Christ said.

luke/6.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
luke/6.bible_quote: For every tree is known by his own fruit.
luke/6.note: Those who are born of the Spirit have all the Divine qualities of growth.  Without these qualities they are dead.

luke/6.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 59
luke/6.bible_quote: For every tree is known by his own fruit.
luke/6.note: We should give evidence in our actions.  The real quality of man is revealed in his words and deeds.  If a believer shows forth divine qualities we know the true faith is in his heart.

luke/14.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
luke/14.bible_quote: And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests to betray Him unto them.
luke/14.note: Judas began well but failed in the end.  Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants.  It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

luke/14.bible_quote: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
luke/14.note: Divines' rejection of the Truth fell into depths of ignorance.  How many of the ignorant, by acceptance of the Faith, have soared aloft...

luke/14.ref: Star of the West, XIII:4, p. 104
luke/14.bible_quote: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
luke/14.note: The humbler man is, in the path of God, the more exalted is he.  Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of the friends of God.

luke/14.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
luke/14.bible_quote: Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the Kingdom of God.
luke/14.note: This is the blessed supper of the Lord...the Heavenly Books prophesy they shall come from the East and the West to sit down in the Kingdom of God.

luke/14.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 9
luke/14.bible_quote: Parable of the great supper (see wedding feast of Matthew 22:3.)
luke/14.note: The invited guests, leaders of the day made excuses to remain away.

luke/14.bible_quote: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
luke/14.note: Learned men arose against Muhammad while a mere shepherd recognized and embraced Him.

acts/9.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)
acts/9.note: Paul was an enemy of Christ but he became one of His best servants. It is wrong to judge, the end of man is not known. (*Discourse)

acts/9.bible_quote: And Saul, yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord...
acts/9.note: Paul in his early life was an enemy of Christ whilst later he became His most faithful servant.  For whether we live, we live unto the Lord, and whether we die, we die unto the Lord.  We can enver know what will be the final judgment.

acts/2.ref: Star of the West, III:2, p. 3 (*)

acts/2.bible_quote: And when the Day of Pentecost was fully come...they were all filled with the Holy Ghost...

acts/2.bible_quote: And when the day of Pentecost was fully come...they were all filled with the Holy Ghost...
acts/2.note: Explanation of descent of Holy Spirit.

acts/2.bible_quote: and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit...then they gladly received his word were baptized...and there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
acts/2.note: Through the power of the Holy Spirit, Peter the simple fisherman was transformed.

acts/2.bible_quote: They were all filled with the Holy Ghost...the same day were added unto them about three thousand souls...
acts/2.note: through the bounty of the Kingdom he became a great apostle.

acts/2.bible_quote: And they were all filled with the Holy Ghost...
acts/2.note: I beg God to confirm thee to His will as he confirmed the apostles of Jesus aforetime.

acts/2.bible_quote: Then they were all filled with the Holy Ghost...the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
acts/2.note: All that hath since been effected...in diffusing fragrances of Christ...hath been the result of the effects produced by that assemblage of disciples.

acts/2.note: Christ taught Peter.  Peter planted the seed.  Through actions qualities express themselves.

acts/2.note: The Jews denounced and slew Christ saying, Behold when the Messiah shall come signs and wonders shall testify that He is Christ...they have not appeared.

acts/2.note: According to the Jews, Jesus Christ fulfilled none of these conditions therefore they rejected Him.

acts/2.bible_quote: Peter lifted up his voice and with many words did he exhort...they that received his word were baptized...there were added unto them about three thousand souls.
acts/2.note: After the baptism of the Holy Spirit, Peter became a great preacher.

acts/2.ref: Star of the West, X:3, p. 39 (*)
acts/2.bible_quote: Then they that gladly received his word were added to them...about three thousand souls.
acts/2.note: Do not look at present conditions.  The apostles of Christ were insignificant persons, see what took place.  Baha'u'llah's bounties shall in the future be made manifest. (*Tablet to Mrs. Agnes Parsons, 1/8/1919, translated by Shoghi Effendi)

acts/2.bible_quote: Then they that gladly received his word were baptized...about three thousand souls.
acts/2.note: Lowly Peter reached immortal glory while Caiaphas and Annas fell to greatest abasement.

acts/2.bible_quote: the same day there were added about three thousand souls...
acts/2.note: The disciples of Christ engaged in guiding peoples, proved self-sacrificing and suffered martyrdom.

acts/2.bible_quote: And they, continuing daily with one accord in the temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart.
acts/2.note: Observe the gatherings of the disciples after Christ...their trumpets still resounding throughout the ages.

acts/2.bible_quote: Now all who believer were together and had all things in common, and sold their possessions and goods and divided them among all, as anyone had need.
acts/2.note: Only communism recommended by the Bab was that of the early Christians, sharing goods in common.

luke/7.bible_quote: a woman...which was a sinner brough an alabaster box of ointment...began to wash His feet with her tears and wipe them with her hair and kissed His feet and annointed them with the ointment...I say un
luke/7.note: Through Jesus Christ the unchaste and wayward were healed.

luke/7.ref: Star of the West, VIII:11, p. 144
luke/7.bible_quote: a sinner brought an alabaster box of ointment and stood at His feet...began to wash His feet with tears and did wipe them with the hairs of her head and hissed His feet and annointed them with the oin
luke/7.note: The bottle containing the perfumed oil represents the heart of Mary; the ointment, the love of God in Mary's heart...the poor, those who needed love.  We must always cherish the poor for His sake.

luke/7.bible_quote: A woman...a sinner brought an alabaster box of ointment...began to wash His feet with tears and wipe them with the hairs of her head...kissed His feet, anointed them...Thy faith hath saved thee, go in
luke/7.note: No mention of wasting the ointment or of its sale in this Gospel.

luke/7.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 42
luke/7.bible_quote: A woman...stood at His feet behind Him weeping and began to wash His feet with tears and did wipe them with the hairs of her head and kissed His feet and annointed them with the ointment...Thy faith h
luke/7.note: In embalming they washed and perfumed the dead.  Christ was to be crucified so she annointed Him and dried it with her hair.

luke/8.ref: Star of the West, X:12, p. 232 (*)
luke/8.bible_quote: He went preaching...and the twelve were with Him and certain women which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities; Mary Magdalene out of whom went seven devils...
luke/8.note: From Mary Magdalene Jesus Christ cast out seven devils...observe her transformation and glory after she believed in Christ. (*Tablet to Mrs. Annie L Parmerton, Cincinnati, Ohio)
Symbolic of the seed of life which needs experience.
Attainment is dependent upon capacity.  Unless capacity is developed the summons of the Kingdom cannot reach the ear--the light of the Sun of truth will not be observed.

luke/8.ref: Star of the West, XII:11, p. 182
luke/8.note: Some have no capacity; others hear and understand and forget; others have great capacity and live accordingly.

luke/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
luke/8.note: All souls have capacity for enkindlement by the Spirit.  The disciples were humble fishermen.

luke/8.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 43
luke/8.note: The seed contained the truth.  Some behold the seed only a hard substance while others see in it the life principle.

luke/9.bible_quote: Then He called His disciples and gave them power and authority over all devils and to cure diseases.  And sent them to preach the Kingdom of God.
luke/9.note: He recalled the words of Jesus to His disciples and emphasized the superlative greatness of the New Day. (*the Bab, in His charge to the Letters of the Living)

luke/9.bible_quote: then He called His twelve disciples together, and gave them power and authority...And whosoever will not receive you, when you go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimo
luke/9.note: The Bab admonished the Letters of the Living to ponder the words of Jesus addressed to His disciples as He sent them forth, to shake off the dust from the feet in departing from the city which refused to receive them. (*Bab to Letters of the Living)

luke/9.bible_quote: Then He called His twelve disciples together and gave them power and authority...He sent them to preach the Kingdom of God and heal the sick.
luke/9.note: Always with their teacher, the divine Master, they learned through His teachings...watching and observing their Master's conduct, followed His example and after His ascension scattered to various regions...we must follow Baha'u'llah

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, V:14, p. 215
luke/9.bible_quote: He gave them power and sent them to preach the Kingdom of God and heal the sick.
luke/9.note: We must act accordingly and follow in their footsteps...

luke/9.bible_quote: Then He called the twelve disciples...gave them power and authority...sent them to preach the Kingdom of God...
luke/9.note: Let them that are men of action follow in their footsteps.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 41
luke/9.bible_quote: He sent them to preach the Kingdom of God...and they departed and went through the towns...
luke/9.note: We must be willing to give our lives...we must go among the people of the world...summoning them...

luke/9.bible_quote: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
luke/9.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha's instructions for traveling teachers: travel like 'Abdu'l-Baha, free from every attachment.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, VII:13, p. 122 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
luke/9.note: Man must live in the utmost sanctity and purity and he must ever think to assist others and not beg assistance from them. (*DMAS, 10/15/1914, Haifa)

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, XI:2, p. 34 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: And whosoever will not receive you, when you go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
luke/9.note: Teachers must travel to all parts of the world like 'Abdu'l-Baha.  He was sanctified and free from every attachment. (*Tablet unveiling the Divine Plan for the World)

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 241 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
luke/9.note: The Covenant is like unto the ocean which preserves Baha'i unity...I acted in accordance with Christ's admonition.  (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. Howard MacNutt)

luke/9.bible_quote: And whosoever will not receive you, when ye go out of that city, shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
luke/9.note: They should bear the utmost poverty, seek no reward...Thou shouldst utter with eloquence and joy and guide to manifest light.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, V:1, p. 6
luke/9.bible_quote: And they departed and went through the towns, preaching the gospel and healing everywhere.
luke/9.note: Command to travel in every country as did the apostles of Jesus Christ.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, VII:14, p. 131
luke/9.bible_quote: And they departed and went through the towns, preaching the gospel everywhere...
luke/9.note: Consider with what severance, attraction and enkindlement the apostles arose in the promulgation of the Word of God...

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, VII:11, p. 106
luke/9.bible_quote: And He took the five loaves and two fishes...blessed them and brake and gave to the disciples to set before the multitude...there was taken up fragments twelve basketsful...
luke/9.note: Explanation of the miracle and the symbolism of the loaves and fishes.

luke/9.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 34
luke/9.bible_quote: Then He took the five loaves and two fishes, and looking up to heaven, He blessed them, and brake, and gave to the disciples...remained to them twelve basketsful.
luke/9.note: Interpretation of loaves--words of Jesus which satisfied the multitude; the fish representing spiritual meaning; remaining basketsful--the superabundance of teaching for others.

luke/9.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27(*)
luke/9.bible_quote: And He said to them...if any man will come after Me, let him deny himself and take up His cross daily and follow Me.
luke/9.note: No one is able to follow Christ entirely except he give up all material things such as honour, wealth, etc. (*Talk by Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908)

luke/9.bible_quote: When He shall come in His own glory and His Father's.
luke/9.note: The land of Syria hath been honoured by the footsteps of its Lord.  The Scriptures have been fulfilled.

luke/9.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 57
luke/9.bible_quote: when He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's...
luke/9.note: We have said, He who is the Father is come...Blessed is he that draweth nigh unto Him and woe betide them that are far away.

luke/9.ref: The Advent of Divine Justice, p. 65
luke/9.bible_quote: when He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Every Prophet hath announced the coming of this Day...allusions were made to it in all the sacred Scriptures.

luke/9.bible_quote: when He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha reminds us that we are veiled from the Truth by literal interpretations.

luke/9.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 18
luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha invites all to partake of the fruit of the Kingdom of the Father, for He discloses mysteries of the teachings of His father, Baha'u'lah. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/10/1908, Acca)

luke/9.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 19 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: when He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Jesus Christ did not claim to have come in that station but that He would return in the Glory of the Father.  This glory was to be the unity of mankind. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/10/1908, Acca)

luke/9.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 32 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: when He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and in the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Baha'u'llah is the Trunk, the branches and the root--all except the fruit, which is 'Abdu'l-Baha.  The teachings of Baha'u'llah have been spread throughout the world. (*Talk by Ibn Abhar, 11/12/1908, Acca)

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's, and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Jesus Christ referred to His as...Isaiah alluded to Him as the Everlasting Father.

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Baha'u'llah arrived in Baghdad.  In it the Tabernacle of the Lord of Hosts was first erected and the foundations of the long-awaited Kingdom of the Father unassailably established.

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Jesus Christ returned in the glory of the Father and was about to ascend the throne.

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Baha'u'llah announced the advent of One with Whose name the Evangel was adorned.

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Baha'u'llah asserts that Jesus Christ was the Herald of His Mission.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, IV:15, p. 258
luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: The Father, whose coming Jesus Christ foretold, has come among us and opened the Greatest Door of bounteous gifts and Divine favours.

luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Return of Christ signifies the One to come after Him.  He shall come with the Kingdom of God.

luke/9.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 139
luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's...
luke/9.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha is not a Manifestation of Godl He is not the Return of Christ, the Son Who will come in the glory of His Father.  He reflects even as a clear mirror the rays of Baha'u'llah's glory.

luke/9.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 61
luke/9.bible_quote: He shall come in His own glory and in His Father's and of the holy angels.
luke/9.note: Holy Scriptures announce the coming of two Manifestations and these two are the Bab and Baha'u'llah.

luke/9.bible_quote: He took Peter and John and James, and went up into the mountain to pray...as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistening.
luke/9.note: The faithful are ever sustained by the presence of the Supreme Concourse.

luke/9.bible_quote: He took Peter and John and James and went up into the mountain to pray, and as he prayed, the fashion of his countenance was altered, and his raiment was white and glistening...there talked with him t
luke/9.note: A spiritual condition expressed as a physical meeting...

luke/9.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 32
luke/9.bible_quote: While He thus spake there came a cloud and overshadowed them...a voice out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son: hear Him.
luke/9.note: Proof of the Manifestation is the Manifestation Himself.  Jesus Christ was born in a manger but He changed the whole world by His power and divinity.

luke/9.bible_quote: And there came a voice out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son: hear Him.
luke/9.note: God conferred upon that Essence of the Spirit Who was known amongst the people as fatherless, the glory of Prophethood and made Him His testimony unto all that are in heaven and on earth.

luke/9.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 113
luke/9.bible_quote: And there came a voice out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son: hear Him.
luke/9.note: The Sonship of Jesus Christ is asserted by Baha'u'llah.

luke/9.bible_quote: And there came a voice out of the cloud saying, This is My beloved Son: hear Him.
luke/9.note: The Sonship station is the heart of Christ and the Holy Spirit is the station of the Spirit of Christ...meaning that the Sun is visible and manifest in the Mirror.

luke/9.bible_quote: But they understood not this saying and it was hid from them that they perceived it not.
luke/9.note: The disciples of Christ were physically weak and apparently vanquished at first, yet in the end they were victorious.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: he that is least among you all, the same shall be great.
luke/9.note: The humbler a man is in the path of God, the more exalted is he; the greater his meekness and submissiveness, the more beloved is he... (*Talk to believers and students, 7/1914, Haifa)

luke/9.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27
luke/9.bible_quote: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives but to save them.
luke/9.note: Jesus gave His life for mankind.  He proved His own teaching; people believed, then obeyed, then found deliverance from sin.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 156 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives but to save them.
luke/9.note: All the Prophets appeared that the oneness of man might be taught.  How much suffering these Prophets had to endure to unfold this illumination.  His Holiness offered His life. (*Address in Stuttgart, Germany, 4/6/1913)

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, VII:18, p. 179 980
luke/9.bible_quote: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives but to save them.
luke/9.note: Let us have the life of the Spirit...of sympathy, practical cooperation, celestial brotherhood... (*DMAS, 5/1914)

luke/9.bible_quote: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives but to save them.
luke/9.note: It is for this purpose that the Manifestations unveil themselves, bear every ordeal to make ready soul's dawnings of light.  This is the real atonement Christ made...He sacrificed Himself for the world...

luke/9.bible_quote: Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: So fierce was the opposition to Jesus that He wandered from place to place.  All Manifestations were fiercely opposed.

luke/9.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 49
luke/9.bible_quote: And Jesus said unto him, Foxes have holes, and birds have nests.  But the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Baha'u'llah's explanation for Jesus' celibacy given to monks...

luke/9.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: He will be seated on the throne of David; behold this man has not as much as a mat whereon to sit...Jews did not see the Reality, the symbol.

luke/9.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Manifestations met with fierce opposition by divines.

luke/9.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 83
luke/9.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Treatment of Jesus by Jewish leaders analogous to that of Baha'u'llah by divines.

luke/9.bible_quote: Foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Jesus was an exemplar--detached from all that would bind Him to the material, detached from desire.

luke/9.bible_quote: Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Let us compare the lives of some of the Popes with the religion of Christ.  Reflect upon the instructions of Christ and investigate the habits and customs of the Pope.

luke/9.bible_quote: Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Be not sorrowful when meeting with worldly difficulties and depressions.  Remember the poverty of Jesus Christ and the Jews' persecution--that poverty was a Light which hath illumined heaven and earth.

luke/9.bible_quote: the Son of man hath not where to lay His head.
luke/9.note: Jesus Christ had no home.  He did not sit on the Throne of David, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead but go thou and preach the Kingdom of God.
luke/9.note: Deadness is in the hearts of those who have been deprived of the Holy Spirit.

luke/9.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Without the protection and quickening of the Holy Spirit the human world would be extinguished.

luke/9.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Souls alive physically,  but Christ pronounced them dead in that they were devoid of the spirit of faith.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: The alive are those souls in whom the spirit of life is breathed from the Presence of God...all besides them are dead.

luke/9.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 42
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: When souls that are not good die, they go entirely away from this earth and so cannot influence anyone.  They are spiritually dead.

luke/9.bible_quote: let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: The Spirit of Faith is the flame of reality--cause of eternal illumination.  Though the souls of humanity are living yet if they are deprived of contact with the spirit they are as dead.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Souls deprived of the divine spirit accounted as dead.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Body in need of physical and mental energy but his spirit requires fortification of the Holy Spirit.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Without illumination from God the physicaly alive would be spiritually dead.  Were it not for the love of God the pathway of the Kingdom would be closed.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Those heedless of God are dead compared with the people of faith.

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, IV:2, p. 38 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Whosoever hath not received a portion of the Spirit is as dead. The Spirit is the life of humanity, the cause of eternal illumination. (*Talk, 1/30/1913, rue St. Didier, Paris)

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, VII:16, p. 150 (*)
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: It is evident that life, in its true sense, is the life of the spirit and that life is the love of God, divine inspiration, spiritual joys and glad tidings of God. (*early Tablet)

luke/9.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:7, p. 224 (*Tablet)
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead...go thou and preach the Kingdom of God.
luke/9.note: The body of man is in need of physical and mental energy--the human spirit which is not fortified by the Holy Spirit is dead and in need of resurrection by divine power. (*Tablet)

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: He would busy these dead was alive with a rational human soul, yet did Christ./..declare such dead; this person was devoid of the Spirit of Faith.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: You have found eternal life from among the dead; that is, you have heard the call of the Divine Kingdom...have turned your faces toward the Center of the Light of truth.

luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Souls who are nt vivified by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead...

luke/9.bible_quote: Le the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Those souls who are not vivified and attracted by the Holy Spirit are accounted among the dead.

luke/9.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 41
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury the dead.
luke/9.note: Those born of the Spirit have Divine qualities; without these they are dead and are without the power of growth.

luke/9.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 18
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: Those who lived merely a physical life were to Christ as dead--real life is the life of the Spirit--life eternal.

luke/9.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 39
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: At death the dust of man is non-existent but in its own station it has existence--the human beings may be spiritually dead bwhile still alive.

luke/9.ref: World Order through World Faith, p. 31
luke/9.bible_quote: Let the dead bury their dead.
luke/9.note: The soul that is portionless and bereft is dead...the human soul is in need of Divine...vivification emanating from the Holy Spirit.

luke/10.ref: Star of the West, XVI:3, p. 458
luke/10.bible_quote: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God...and thy neighbor as thyself.
luke/10.note: God is absolute love even as His Holiness Jesus Christ declared.  The Prophets have appeared in this world that human souls may attain to love and amity...

luke/10.bible_quote: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy strength and with all thy mind...
luke/10.note: This is the original and principal teaching that Jesus Christ spread in the world.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done as in heaven so on earth.
luke/11.note: If people of the entire globe are welded into one great commonwealth the prayer, Thy Kingdom come...will be a reality.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: Reconstruction of the world must take place before the Will of God can be done on earth...signs of awakening since Baha'u'llah's Revelation.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: 'Abdu'l-Baha made plans to carry Divine Plan for establishing the Kingdom of God to the Western world.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: Prayer is also a prophecy, it depends on you.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: Baha'u'llah is the establisher of the Kingdom which Jesus Christ promised.

luke/11.bible_quote: Our Father which art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name, Thy kingdom come...
luke/11.note: It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: Strive that your ideals may be realized in the Kingdom of God on earth.

luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: This is the glorious time of which the Lord Jesus Christ spoke when He told us to pray, Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven.  I hope this is your expectation and great desire.

luke/11.bible_quote: Hallowed be Thy name...
luke/11.note: Glory of God eternal...

luke/11.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 46
luke/11.bible_quote: Thy Kingdom come, Thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
luke/11.note: A world catastrophe must usher in the Kingdom of the heavenly Father, as anticipated by Jesus Christ.

luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub.
luke/11.note: Moses announced the coming of Jesus Christ.  Israelites were waiting impatiently for Him but when He came they called Him Beelzebub.

luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub...
luke/11.note: In His day Christ was called Satan, Beelzebub, but hear the bells now ringing for Him!  Although men may rise against the Kingdom, the dominion and sovereignty of God will be set up.

luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub...
luke/11.note: The Jews rejected Jesus and tortured Him but Christianity advanced daily.

luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.
luke/11.note: The Pharisees called Jesus Beelzebub.  The deriding enemy addeth joy to the heart...becometh the means of spirituality.

luke/11.ref: Utterances in letter from H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. (*)
luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub...
luke/11.note: When Jesus came the Jews called Him Beelzebub.  I have always said that the believers will be blamed for this Cause.  The Jews proved from the Bible that Jesus was a false prophet.  It is always so. (*I.D. Brittingham, 1/1914, Haifa)

luke/11.ref: Star of the West, IV:14, p. 241 (*)
luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub...
luke/11.note: The Cause of Christ had no importance then.  Importance of the Covenant not yet known befittingly. (*Tablet to Roy C. Wilhelm)

luke/11.ref: Star of the West, XXV:?, p. 258
luke/11.bible_quote: He casteth out devils through Beelzebub...
luke/11.note: Christ in His day was called Satan, today He is as the Word of God.

luke/11.bible_quote: No man, when he hath lighted a candle putteth it...under a bushel, but on a candlestick that they which come in may see the light.
luke/11.note: Be upon your guard in order that none may assail the oneness and integrity of Baha'u'llah's Cause.

luke/11.ref: The Principle of Religious Unity, p. 2
luke/11.bible_quote: Woe unto you, Pharisees!  For ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God; these ought ye to have done and not to leave the other undone.
luke/11.note: when the Jews became fettered by empty forms and imitations, the wrath of God became manifest...

luke/11.bible_quote: Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge; ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.
luke/11.note: The Jews knew by heart the letter but of the Spirit they understood not a word...they did not understand Scriptures.  Because Jesus dared to rebuke the Pharisees, the Jews rejected Him.

luke/11.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 41
luke/11.bible_quote: the Pharisees began to urge Him vehemently and to provoke Him to speak of many things.  Laying wait for Him and seeking to catch something out of His mouth that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: Analogy between the doctors disputing and rejecting Jesus to the divines rejecting Baha'u'llah.

luke/11.bible_quote: the Pharisees began to provoke Him to speak of many things...Laying wait for Him, and seeking to catch something out of His mouth, that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees disputed with Jesus.

luke/11.bible_quote: the scribes and Pharisees began to urge Him vehemently and to provoke Him to speak of many things.  Laying in wait and seeking to catch something out of His mouth that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: Beware that thou dispute not with Baha'u'llah even as the Pharisees with Jesus.

luke/11.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 31
luke/11.bible_quote: The scribes and Pharisees began to urge Him vehemently and provoke Him to speak of many things...Laying in wait for Him and seeking to catch something out of His mouth that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: Baha'u'llah warned the Pope, Guard thyself lest darkness spread its veil over thee.  Consider those who opposed Jesus and disputed with Him.

luke/11.bible_quote: The scribes and Pharisees began to urge Him vehemently and to provoke Him...Laying in wait for Him and seeking to catch something out of His mouth that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: The enemies of His Holiness Jesus Christ were the Jews, the Pharisees, but the enemies of Baha'u'llah were kings representing some 15,000,000 people.  At last His banner was upraised.

luke/11.ref: Utterances in letter of H.E.Hoagg to Mrs. I.D. (*)
luke/11.bible_quote: The scribes and Pharisees began to urge Him vehemently and to provoke Him to speak...Laying in wait and seeking to catch something out of His mouth that they might accuse Him.
luke/11.note: In the time of Jesus the Jewish clergy created so many doubts that there were very few who believed, in reality there were only twelve and one of those denied Him.  Why? Because the tests at that time were very great.

luke/12.bible_quote: But rather seek ye the Kingdom of God.
luke/12.note: It is the duty of the followers of Baha'u'llah to proclaim the approach of the Christ-promised Kingdom.

luke/12.bible_quote: Blessed are those servants whom the Lord when He cometh shall find watching...
luke/12.note: Thou wast awake, therefore thou didst advance and engage in the service of the Word of God...

luke/12.bible_quote: Suppose ye that I am come to bring peace, nay rather division.
luke/12.note: The prophecy in Isaiah 6:9 that He shall be called the prince of peace was not fulfilled in Jesus Christ, therefore the Jews rejected Him.

luke/13.bible_quote: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth...and they shall come from the east and the west and the north and the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: The Divine Gardener separates or unites.  Separation or unity will be the result of your own personal choice or actions.

luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and the west and the north and the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: They did not see these conditions fulfilled.  Roman tyranny enveloped the world.  They rejected Christ.  They crucified Jesus Christ.

luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and the west and the north and the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.  And behold, there are last which shall be first and there are first that shall be
luke/13.note: The meaning is obvious.  Be steadfast...some of the nearest are cut off, while those from a distance receive these gifts.

luke/13.ref: Star of the West, II:13, p. 3 (*)
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and the west and the north and the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: Give a thousand thanks to God that as the Great Sun arose in the East, His brilliant Light shone upon the West and brightened the Western world...that though you were apparently cut off, in reality you were near. (*Tablet)

luke/13.ref: Star of the West, VIII:14, p. 191 (*)
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: This is the Day when the East and the West shall embrace each other like unto two lovers, war and contention shall be forgotten... (*Centennial of birth of Baha'u'llah)

luke/13.ref: Star of the West, VIII:17, p. 225 (*)
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: Know of a certainty that the question of the gathering of the descendants of Abraham in Jerusalem is a fact... (*Words to sons of Agha Joseph)

luke/13.ref: Star of the West, IX:9, p. 100 (*)
luke/13.bible_quote: there are last that shall be first and there are first that shall be last.
luke/13.note: Every firm believer will consider himself the servant of all the friends of God--of the world of humanity.  The honor of man depends upon this.  (*Talk to believers and students in DMAS, 7/15/1914)

luke/13.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 25
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God, and behold, there are last which shall be first and there are first
luke/13.note: The Divine Gardener cuts off the dry and weak branch from the good tree and grafts it to the branch from another tree.  Persons through their acts and words deprived themselves from the bestowals of the Blessed Beauty...Tree of life=Book of Covenant

luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: the brilliancy of the Kingdom of Abha shall shine...spirituality of 'Abdu'l-Baha become manifest.  Divine food served in loving fellowship.

luke/13.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 2
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom of God.
luke/13.note: It is a heavenly privilege to know the Truth and become a child of the Kingdom.

luke/13.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 75
luke/13.bible_quote: And they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the Kingdom  of God.
luke/13.note: One language, outer language of unity;Baha'u'llah revealed inner language of the spirit.  The Heavenly Books prophesy they shall come together in spiritual unity.

luke/13.ref: The Unfoldment of World Civilization, p. 16
luke/13.bible_quote: O Jerusalem...which killest the prophets and stonest them that are sent unto thee!  How often would I have gathered thy children...as a hen doth gather her brood but ye would not...
luke/13.note: Baha'u'llah compares the evidence of Divine visitation which befell the persecutors of Jesus and Himself.  Baha'u'llah's apostrophe to Constantinople.

luke/13.ref: The World Order of Baha'u'llah, p. 176
luke/13.bible_quote: O Jerusalem...which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children as a hen doth gather her brood, but ye would not.
luke/13.note: Compare Jesus' words with Baha'u'llah's apostrophe to Constantinople as He lay in prison.

luke/16.ref: Epistle to the Son of the Wolf, p. 58
luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also who were covetous heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: Analogy between the rejection of Jesus Christ and the divines' rejection of Baha'u'llah.

luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: The Jews jeeringly turned away.  The Jews were bereft of sight...their eyes were covered with the veils of traditions and names.

luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: Certain persons, like the Pharisees...will exert themselves to deprive souls of the glad-tidings of the Holy Spirit.

luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: Treatment of Baha'u'llah analogous to that of Jesus Christ at the hands of ecclesiastical leaders.

luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: The scribes and Pharisees spread slanders and caused Him to be crucified.

luke/16.ref: The Promised Day is Come, p. 30
luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: Warning to Pope Pius IX not to dispute with Baha'u'llah as did the Jewish doctors with Jesus.

luke/16.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 14 (*)
luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: The Jews were entreating for the coming of the Promised One but when He came with manifest power they turned away from Him...hidden because His name was Baha'u'llah. (*Lawh-i-Aqdas, Baha'u'llah)

luke/16.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 633 (*)
luke/16.bible_quote: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things and they derided Him.
luke/16.note: The Jews cried, O God hasten the day when the Promised One will manifest Himself...they could not see the Reality (*Bahai Scriptures, p. 729-30)

luke/16.ref: The Principles of Religious Unity, p. 3-4
luke/16.bible_quote: If they hear not Moses and the Prophets neither will they be persuaded...though one rose from the head.
luke/16.note: Jesus Christ revoiced and confirmed the essential teachings of Moses.  Christ ratified and proclaimed the foundation of the law of Moses.

luke/22.bible_quote: Then he [Judas] promised and sought opportunity to betray Him to them [the Pharisees] in the absence of the multitude.
luke/22.note: Treatment of Baha'u'llah analogous to that of Jesus Christ at the hands of ecclesiastical leaders.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, VI:6, p. 43
luke/22.bible_quote: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill Him.
luke/22.note: The Pharisees held strictly to the law, they were the cause of the condemnation and crucifixion of Jesus.

luke/22.bible_quote: Then entered Satan into Judas...and he promised and sought opportunity to betray Him.
luke/22.note: The betrayal of Baha'u'llah by Mirza Yahya more shameful than the betrayal of Jesus Christ by Judas.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XIII:1, p. 21,25
luke/22.bible_quote: Then entered Satan into Judas and he communed with the chief priests how he might betray Jesus...
luke/22.note: Judas separated himself from Jesus.  Any person who has left the Cause did so because of his own actions, intrigues and evil plots.  The Tree of life is the Book of the Covenant.

luke/22.bible_quote: And he went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: There were people like Judas, who, by their own deeds, separated themselves from Christ...similar to violators who turned against 'Abdu'l-Baha.

luke/22.bible_quote: And he went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: Judas chose to turn from Jesus and go his own way.  'Abdu'l-Baha was rejected by the violators.

luke/22.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
luke/22.bible_quote: And he went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: Verily tests withheld a great apostle from the mercy of God and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.

luke/22.bible_quote: And he communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them...
luke/22.note: Be pure, to be pure is to be selfless.  Jesus had eleven disciples for the twelfth was the cause of His crucifixion.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 269 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: And Judas went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them...they covenanted to give him money.
luke/22.note: Violators like Judas Iscariot have for financial motives deviated from the Covenant.  Baha'i unity cannot be preserved save through the Covenant of God. (*Tablet to Miss Mary Lesch)

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, X:14, p. 270 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: And Judas went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: Judas Iscariot first believed in Christ, then turned away from Him.  I hope that thou mayest daily increase in firmness and become the cause of the guidance of other souls. (*Tablet to an American Baha'i)

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XI:14, p. 242 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: He communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: The ocean of the Covenant is eternally surging.  The violators manifest temporary activity but soon, like Judas and his associates and those who approve of his conduct, they shall be completely forgotten (*Tablet to Mr. & Mrs. MacNutt)

luke/22.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 17
luke/22.bible_quote: And he went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: Judas was one of the twelve apostles who was not firm.  He betrayed Jesus.  God will assist all those who are firm in His Cause.

luke/22.bible_quote: And he promised and sought opportunity to betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: The superstition about the number thirteen had its origin in the fact that it was the thirteenth apostle who betrayed Jesus.  Suppositions regarding lucky or unlucky numbers are imaginary.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
luke/22.bible_quote: Go and prepare us the Passover...Where wilt thou that we prepare?...Ye shall say to the good man of the house, Where is the guest chamber where I shall eat the Passover with My disciples?
luke/22.note: It was not in a church.  On that night they had the Last Supper it was in a room.  The idea is the heart should be attracted, the spirit filled with the divine glad tidings and the soul turned to God.  This is the object, other things are secondary.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 269
luke/22.bible_quote: and when the hour was come, He sat down and the twelve apostles with Him.
luke/22.note: It is well to worship in any place...the object is to turn to God.  When Christ gathered His disciples together for the Last Supper, it was not a church, it was in a room.  The heart should be attracted, spirit filled with divine glad tidings

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XVIII:9, p. 284
luke/22.bible_quote: And they made ready for Passover...and He sat down and the twelve with Him.
luke/22.note: Because there was excessive love among the individual members who had convened there, that table surpassed the royal tables and it was established as the Lord's Supper, even now it is known as such.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XI:16, p. 275(Tablet to Mr.Hall)
luke/22.bible_quote: When the hour was come He sat down and the twelve with Him.
luke/22.note: The manna which came down from heaven for the disciples was bounty, knowledge, faith and assurance, love, attraction, attachment and enkindlement by the fire of the love of God.  My hope is that that gathering may also eat from this manna

luke/22.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 74
luke/22.bible_quote: And they made ready the Passover...he sat down and the twelve with Him.  And He said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this Passover with you.
luke/22.note: This is the blessed Lord's Supper...for we have gathered under the shadow of the Blessed Perfection...This was indeed a spiritual feast where Love reigned.

luke/22.bible_quote: and they made ready the Passover.  And when the hour was come He sat down and the twelve apostles with Him.
luke/22.note: The Supper was a spiritual one.  The heavenly food consisted in the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowal of God.  Religious leaders thought material food was changed into spiritual food.

luke/22.bible_quote: And He took bread and gave thanks and brake it and gave unto them, saying, This is My body which is given for you; do this in remembrance of Me.
luke/22.note: Whenever such an entertainment is arranged through spiritual sentiments it is the Lord's Supper.

luke/22.bible_quote: And when the hour was come He sat down and the twelve apostles with Him.
luke/22.note: Designated as the Lord's supper they ate heavenly food at that supper, the love, knowledge, mysteries and bestowals of God.

luke/22.bible_quote: With desire I have desired to eat this Passover...And He took bread...this is My body which is given for you; this do in remmebrance of Me...
luke/22.note: because there was excessive love amongst them, that table surpassed the royal tables.

luke/22.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the Kingdom of God shall come.
luke/22.note: It is evident that Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual state which He interpreted as fruit. (*also in Star of the West, II:15, p. 16)

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, II:15, p. 16 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: It will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the Kingdom of God shall come.
luke/22.note: It is evident that His Highness Christ did not mean material grapes but it was a spiritual condition and a heavenly state which He interpreted as fruit. (*Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 90)

luke/22.bible_quote: I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the Kingdom of God shall come.
luke/22.note: The appearance of the holy Manifestations is for the purpose of causing the heavenly table to descend.  This table means divine virtues and characteristics...

luke/22.bible_quote: And He took bread and gave thanks and brake it and gave unto them saying, This is My body which is given for you, this do in remembrance of Me.
luke/22.note: Make an effort in every meeting that the Lord's Supper may become realized and heavenly food...love, affinity, union of souls...then 'Abdu'l-Baha will be present also.

luke/22.bible_quote: This is My body which is given for you...
luke/22.note: When Christ appeared...He invited men to eternal life; the Light of guidance was shed forth by that radiant Star and He at length gave His life in sacrifice for humanity.

luke/22.bible_quote: And he took bread saying, This is My body which is given for you...
luke/22.note: Jesus was not transformed into bread and wine.  They were symbols for bounties.

luke/22.bible_quote: This is My body which is given for you...
luke/22.note: Christ, who is the Word of God, sacrificed Himself.  This has two meanings, an apparent and an esoteric.  He arose to teach and educate men, He sacrificed Himself to give the spirit of life...to quicken others.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, IV:1, p. 4
luke/22.bible_quote: This is My body which is given for you.  This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: Consider what His Holiness endured, finally sacrificing His own life so that a tent of love and concord might be raised over humanity.

luke/22.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 21,26
luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: Christ came of His own will to be a sacrifice that people might become educated and progress.  He saved all who believed on Him.  He wished to become a sacrifice.

luke/22.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 27 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: There is no love...more perfect than to give one's life...Jesus proved His love...then His followers believed and were delivered from sin. (*Mirza Haydar 'Ali on 11/11/1908, Akka)

luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: These verses have been interpreted by the churches in such a superstitious way that it is impossible for human reason to accpet the explanations...Christ gave His blood in order to guide mankind...He accepted all His trials because of the immortality

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XXII:9, p. 258 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: He came people say to redeem us through His blood.  He sacrificed His life that He might educate and illumine the world. (*Tablet)

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XVI:9, p. 632
luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: The Christ sacrificed Himself so that men might be free from the imperfections of the physical nature.  Explanation of the esoteric meaning of Jesus' sacrifice.

luke/22.bible_quote: This cup is the new testament in My blood which is shed for you.
luke/22.note: Esoteric meaning of the sacrifice of Christ.

luke/22.bible_quote: Behold, the hand of him that betrayed Me is with me on the table...woe unto that one by whom He is betrayed!
luke/22.note: If the foot of man deviate as did that of Judas, so that he became human after he had been divine...there is in that a great lesson to people of understanding...

luke/22.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 35
luke/22.bible_quote: he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one.
luke/22.note: The sword which Jesus meant was the eloquent tongue dividing right and wrong...

luke/22.ref: Visit to Acca, Mary Lucas, p. 35
luke/22.bible_quote: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment and buy one.
luke/22.note: Sword is the eloquent tongue described in Isaiah 49:2.  It separates right and wrong as described in Hebrews 4:12.  Peter misunderstood so he was commanded to put his sword into its sheath in John 18:11.

luke/22.bible_quote: Garden of Gethsemane.
luke/22.note: Death of Quddus exceeded the agony of Jesus Christ.

luke/22.ref: Daily Lessons received at Acca, p. 98
luke/22.bible_quote: Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
luke/22.note: Verily, tests withheld a great apostle from the mercy of God, and made him take part in the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ.  Many a just soul did not endure the power of tests.

luke/22.bible_quote: Judas betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
luke/22.note: Baha'u'llah has left no room for dissension--some wish to create dissension as did Judas.  There should be no association with violators.

luke/22.bible_quote: Judas betrayeth thou the Son of man with a kiss?
luke/22.note: Judas turned away from Jesus because of jealousy of Peter.  Consider the predecessors as a warning... and purify thy heart.

luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...while yet he spake, the cock crew.
luke/22.note: Three times Peter denied that he knew Jesus, in a moment of weakness, but he repented and remained firm.

luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...
luke/22.note: Christ educated eleven; the greatest was Peter who denied Him thrice.  In spite of this the Cause of Christ permeated the world.  Baha'u'llah has educated thousands--reflect what will take place in the future.

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, IV:12, p. 207 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...
luke/22.note: Comparison between men and women, between Peter and Mary Magdalene.  (*Interview with San Francisco correspondent, 10/3/1912)

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk 12/22, Haifa)
luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...
luke/22.note: When Jesus Christ was crucified He had twelve disciples...one denied Him...another sold Him for money; notwithstanding this, consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would have sacrificed their lives for Baha'u'llah

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, IX:2, p. 23(Talk 12/22,Haifa)
luke/22.bible_quote: And he [Judas] went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray Him unto them.
luke/22.note: When Jesus was crucified He had twelve disciples...one denied Him...another sold Him for money; notwithstanding this consider how important was His Cause.  There were at least 100,000 souls who would have sacrificed their lives for Baha'u'llah

luke/22.ref: Star of the West, XIV:12, p. 353
luke/22.bible_quote: Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...and the Lord turned and looked upon Peter...and Peter went out and wept bitterly.
luke/22.note: Even the glorious Peter was not rescued from the flame of trials--and wavered.

luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter said, Man I know not what thou sayest...
luke/22.note: Baha'is must become steadfast...so solid that thousands of deliverances would not cause thee to tremble.

luke/22.bible_quote: And the Lord turned and looked upon Peter.  And Peter remembered the word of the Lord how He had said unto him before the cock crow thou shalt deny Me thrice.
luke/22.note: This was before the Holy Spirit descended upon him at Pentecost.

luke/22.bible_quote: And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how He said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny Me thrice.
luke/22.note: The events connected with Peter and Magdalene was a proof that woman is equivalent and complement of man.

luke/22.bible_quote: Peter remembered the word of the Lord how He had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny Me thrice.
luke/22.note: the great one, Peter...denied Him three times; Jews scoffed, persecuted, despised Him...they thought they had quenched the Fire but that Fire burned until it kindled the hearts of the righteous.

luke/22.ref: Tne Days in the Light of Acca, p. 33
luke/22.bible_quote: Peter remembered the word of the Lord how He had said unto him, Before the cock crow thou shalt deny Me thrice.
luke/22.note: Peter was the greatest of all disciples, yet he denied Jesus Christ three times.  What happened?  The Truth in Christ spread all over the world.

luke/22.bible_quote: And the men that held Jesus mocked Him and smote Him...blindfolded Him...struck Him in the face and many things blasphemously spake against Him....
luke/22.note: Reflect how the Pharisees persecuted Christ.  The result was that His lamp became ignited; His Light began to shine.

luke/22.bible_quote: And the men that held Jesus mocked Him and smote Him...struck Him and many other things blasphemously spake against Him.
luke/22.note: All through life He suffered oppression and hardship.  Humanity was His enemy.  In spite of all this He was the embodiment of pit, supreme goodness and love.

luke/22.bible_quote: chief priests and the scribes led Him unto Pilate...
luke/22.note: The adversaries of Jesus Christ were rabbis and their associates while those of the Bab represented the civil and ecclesiastical leaders who conspired against Him from the time of His declaration to His execution.

luke/22.bible_quote: chief priest, elders and scribes led Him unto Pilate...
luke/22.note: Analogous to treatment of Baha'u'llah by ecclesiastical leaders.  Adversaries of Jesus Christ were rabbis and their associates ; those of the Bab represented the civil and ecclesiastical authorities who conspired against Him...

luke/22.ref: Flowers culled...Rose Garden at Acca, p. 8 (*)
luke/22.bible_quote: Then they said, Art thou then the Son of God?  And He said unto them, Ye say that I am.
luke/22.note: Christ said of Himself that He was the Son of God. (*Munirih Khanum on 11/8/1908, Akka)

luke/17.bible_quote: there met He ten men that were lepers...as they went they were cleansed.
luke/17.note: Through Jesus Christ the leper recieved healing from the leprosy of ignorance.

luke/17.ref: Ten Days in the Light of Acca, p. 27
luke/17.bible_quote: the Kingdom of God is within you.
luke/17.note: The Kingdom of Heaven is within your soul; when the heart is filled with the Light of the Spirit we know that we are in the true heaven.

luke/18.bible_quote: for everyone that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
luke/18.note: Learned men arose against Muhammad while a mere shepherd recognized and embraced Him.

luke/18.bible_quote: Suffer the little children to come unto Me and forbid them not; for of such is the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: The true Baha'i loves the children.  A simple pure heart is near to God.

luke/18.bible_quote: Suffer little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: Blessed are the children of whom His Holiness Christ said, Of such are the Kingdom of heaven.  Hearts must become pure.

luke/18.bible_quote: Suffer little children to come unto Me..for of such is the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: As long as you are the children of the Kingdom, no matter what language you use, it is sweet.

luke/18.bible_quote: Suffer little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: Spiritual children are dearer than physical children.

luke/18.ref: Star of the West, IV:9, p. 155 (*)
luke/18.bible_quote: Suffer little children to come unto Me...for of such is the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: Because children are tender plants, their hearts are clear and transparent. (*Talk in Stuttgart and Esslingen, Germany--from letters)

luke/18.bible_quote: Whosoever shall not receive the Kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.
luke/18.note: Develop virtues, grow daily in faith and assurance...develop Baha'i attributes, such as obediance and kindness.

luke/18.bible_quote: If thou wilt enter into life keep the Commandments.
luke/18.note: I hope that it is proved to you that Moses had no better friend than His Holiness Christ.

luke/18.bible_quote: If thou wilt enter into life, keep the Commandments.
luke/18.note: By promoting the laws of the Bible, the law of Moses was meant.  Christ promulgated Sinaitic law and gave it spiritual expression.

luke/18.bible_quote: If thou wilt enter into life keep the Commandments.
luke/18.note: Jesus renewed the commandments which Moses had received from Jehovah.  He said the Commandments in the Pentateuch were to be followed and He insisted they must be maintained.

luke/18.ref: Star of the West, III:17, p. 8 (*)
luke/18.bible_quote: If thou wilt enter into life, keep the Commandments.
luke/18.note: Christ spread the name of Moses throughout the world and promulgated the Old Testament throughout the nations. (*Address in London)

luke/18.bible_quote: All these have I kept from my youth up...sell all that thou hast and distribute unto the poor...come and follow Me.
luke/18.note: Each soul must devote his life to God's service.

luke/18.bible_quote: How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the Kingdom of God...
luke/18.note: The true seeker will gladly renounce worldly desires and possessions if they interfere with spiritual growth.

luke/18.bible_quote: For it is easier for a camel to go through the needle's eye than for a rich man to enter into the Kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: Blessed are the poor.  I love you all, you are children of the Kingdom and are accepted of God.

luke/18.bible_quote: For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
luke/18.note: one's heart must not be attached to the things of this world.
